

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 1 - 1: The Beginning
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"My name is Kyle and what can I say? My life is the definition of shit…" A man standing at the edge of a bridge typed his final message on his phone.

There was no doubt that this was a suicide letter but the question was, what led Kyle to the decision to end it all?

-

"Damn it! Another shift!?" A young man cussed out, he had green eyes but there was no life left in them but that was to be expected working an extra shift in a minimum wage paid job.

There was no doubt that this job was taking a toll on his body but he understood that this was something that he couldn't escape, unfortunately.

"Kyle, the boss wants you to close up!" Another employer said but Kyle was already lost in his head, regretting the choices he made to land him at this point of his life.

"I will be going! Don't stay too late…" The man cracked a joke that Kyle didn't find funny.

Kyle couldn't complain because he was the one who asked for extra shifts to be given to him as he needed all the money he could muster.

Kyle thought about returning to school to complete his education and increase his chances of having a decent life even though he was in his mid-twenties.

The sun was nowhere to be seen as it was well into the night, Kyle sighed in defeat but the thing about his store was that they rarely even had customers and it would only be a matter of time before it went bust.

Kyle lived in one of the busiest cities in the United States and that was Los Angeles, the place where stars resided and that meant that the cost of living was exceptionally high.

It would be a miracle to see anything of real value around without breaking the bank, it wasn't designed for the average man.

Kyle sat and just stared blankly at the door, he momentarily thought about a bullet flying in and putting an end to his miserable life but the cackling thunder in the distance brought him back to reality as his thoughts were already entering dangerous territory.

Before he knew it, rain began to pour outside and Kyle realized at that moment that he had forgotten his umbrella at home.

The moment he realized this, he just began to laugh at how ridiculous fate had it out for him.

This had to be something supernatural, there was no other explanation at this point.

"Zeus! Is that how you face me!" Kyle shouted at the top of his lungs referencing one of his favourite games but during his shouting, a customer walked in.

Kyle immediately shut his mouth as his eyes settled on the person who had walked in.

She was drenched from head to toe and most likely entered the shop for shelter however Kyle was amazed by how stunning she looked.

She could be a supermodel but considering this was Los Angeles, there was a chance she probably was.

Kyle's eyes couldn't help but trace her figure in the body-hugging black dress she had on that left just enough space to let her cleavage out, she stood at the door without even acknowledging him.

Kyle didn't expect much from her because this was his reality, no one ever took him seriously especially beautiful women.

He was tempted to ask her to get the fuck out, the camera in the shops wasn't working but his boss left it there so it would deter theft.

But before he could speak, she began to walk around the shop like she got the memo.

She began to walk around the shop but all Kyle saw was more work because each step she took, dirtied the floor.

He was already overstimulated and knew that his breaking point was just around the corner.

The lady walked through the aisle but she came out with a bunch of snacks and a pack of cigarettes that didn't even cost up to $15 but that was a decent amount.

Kyle didn't notice the bag hanging from her shoulder was a Louis Vuitton, this cost more than what he made in a lifetime but this was mainly because of the angle at which the label was tilted.

"That will be $15.56, ma'am," Kyle tried his best to give her the necessary respect a customer was entitled to.

"Here…" The lady handed him a card but the perfume that oozed from her body was alluring, it felt like she put Kyle under a momentary spell.

There was this look of disgust on her face because she caught Kyle sneaking a peak at her chest but who could blame him, it was right in his face.

Kyle quickly collected the card and swiped it, it was normal that people didn't carry cash around with them.

Kyle handed her back her card but he knew that she wasn't going anywhere, the fact that they had yet to restock their umbrella section meant that they both had no choice but to stay here.

"Um… Do you need a chair?" Kyle tried to be polite but immediately regretted it as she glared at him like it was forbidden to speak to her.

She brought out a cigarette but it was forbidden to smoke here and Kyle wasn't about to let her have her way. It was clear that she was used to having her way as she lacked respect.

"You can't smoke in here, ma'am. I am afraid you will have to go outside for that," Kyle instructed her, pointing to the bold sign that clearly showed that smoking was prohibited.

But this was the single moment that ultimately shaped Kyle's life.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 2 - 2: Troublesome Customer
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The rest of the night passed by in a blur and Kyle found himself back home wondering what the deal with the lady he encountered in the shop was.

People in Los Angeles were typically disrespectful but not only did she make a mess of the shop with her wet clothes and muddy shoes, she was rude at that.

Kyle didn't understand why it was so hard for people to be kind but he didn't care much as long as they didn't bother him like this lady did.

She walked out of the shop after receiving a call soon after he forbade her from smoking but something told him that she would have done whatever she wanted either way.

In hindsight, if it was anyone of prominent stature then he would be in trouble even if he was in the right as the store owner would most likely side with them.

Celebrities were everywhere in this city and they always got preferential treatment but with so many upcoming people in the industry, it was impossible to keep track.

Kyle didn't understand the worship but once again, as long as his bills were paid.

He didn't care about anything outside of his immediate sphere.

The one-bedroom flat he lived in barely had any furniture, just a chair and a table with an old TV hung on the wall.

It wasn't like he was getting any coochie so there was no point spending more money than needed as he didn't even have time to have a social life.

Kyle heard his stomach rumbling like an earthquake was about to tear it down and at that moment, he realized that he hadn't gotten dinner.

He was supposed to pick up leftovers at a discount from the restaurant, they did this for meals that weren't touched or barely eaten.

It was against restaurant policy but Kyle used the neat old "it is for my dog" trick to escape judgment.

The fact that they resold it to him, however, was strange but Kyle didn't complain because he knew the cost of these things.

"Fucking hell… Universe 7. Kyle 0," Kyle said under his breath as he unwillingly took himself to his fridge but he already knew that there was nothing there to eat.

He just opened it and stared at it, all there was milk that he didn't even know was good or not with leftover food that wasn't suitable for human consumption.

"You do only live once…" Kyle thought to himself, weighing the option before him before quickly pushing the idea out of his head.

"I guess no dinner tonight…" Kyle muttered to himself in defeat, dragging his feet across the floor as he crashed into his bed.

But his phone buzzed, Kyle already knew who it was as he didn't have anyone that check up on him.

He had no friends and just as he thought, it was his landlord threatening to evict him if he failed to pay rent this week.

Kyle couldn't be mad at Mr Jones, he was a kind man that has been understanding of his situation and even waved a few payments but he wasn't running a charity, he was running a business.

Kyle was living paycheck to paycheck, if he missed a month's paycheck, he had no idea what he would do if he lost his job but the good thing about the life he lived was that nobody would miss him or even notice if he suddenly disappeared.

Kyle slept on an empty stomach because he knew that he would rectify this dilemma tomorrow.

-

The night passed by in a blur and Kyle was ready on time, he wasn't looking forward to this but he needed all the shifts he could get. Each day he lived, it felt like something inside of him died.

He lost the ability to feel shame, he would do everything in his power to make money even if it meant walking down the street butt-naked.

Kyle walked to work but this time, he made sure to carry his umbrella to avoid another mishap.

His place of work was a few minutes from where he lived, the bustling street of Los Angeles never slept.

"Hi Kyle, you're in early today," One of his coworkers greeted him, he was a young college student with everything to live for but even with that, he was getting paid more than Kyle due to his looks.

His presence alone brought certain customers because he was attractive and was good with his words so women kept returning to have a go at him with his wavy blonde hair dangling just above his shoulder with his ocean blue eyes making a certain place rather wet, it was no wonder he was an eye candy.

Sex appeal was something that has always sold, be it in commercials or movies, attractive people had advantages that average-looking people couldn't even dream of having.

Kyle didn't particularly care about him because he was a part-timer but he always had one question, why did he choose to work here?

The day progressed as it normally did with Nate ending his shift by noon and heading out but with the lack of customers the store generally had, the man had to cut corners in terms of labour meaning that it was Kyle who was going to be in the shop for the rest of day.

But his day was about to take an unexpected turn, a man opened the door for a lady to walk in, and Kyle instantly recognized her as the lady from the night before but the man that held the door was huge.

It looked like he was some sort of bodyguard and Kyle immediately tightened up.

"Hello, can I help you with some-!" Kyle was shut down when the lady screamed at the top of her lungs.

"I WANT TO SEE THE MANAGER!" She screamed and this startled Kyle, he had to take a step back because he found this situation threatening.

The man that held the door soon joined her but before things could escalate, the manager came from his office. And Kyle instantly knew that he might be in trouble, he was right.

"Calista Wave!?" Mr. Ryder was a bit too excited but Kyle understood that this woman was someone famous but for what? Being a bitch?

Mr. Ryder was a divorced man in his late forties that almost got wrecked by his last marriage but he was standing at 4' 11 as opposed to Kyle, who was standing at 6' 0.

Calista rolled her eyes at the attention she got, in reality, Calista was a small-town actress who gained a little popularity due to a nude scene in a low-budget film that didn't even make it to the cinema.

One could liken it to softcore porn but it said a lot about Mr. Ryder that he knew who she was but who could blame him?

He was a divorced man in his late forties, he didn't exactly have many options in the dating market.

"Your man boy here ruined my bag and I request a full refund!" The lady barked at Ryder and only then did Kyle see that this bag had a Louis Vuitton logo embedded on it but he was sure that it wasn't the same bag she had brought here the day before.

However, he knew that no one would believe him so he wanted to see how his boss would react to this news.

"That bag… It probably can feed me for a month or two…" Kyle thought to himself, realizing that there was no way in hell he could afford that bag and would go into debt if he tried.

"I-I'm so sorry! I will make sure he pays you every penny this bag's worth!" Ryder said and the disgust Kyle felt building inside of him was unlike anything he had felt.

This was his life, people thought they could take advantage of him whenever they pleased but who could blame them?

He had no way to fight back and the man with her, Kyle noticed he had a badge on him so this was a policeman.

"Do you have any evidence?" Kyle asked, he was soft-spoken to the point that people mistook him as timid.

"What?" Calista questioned, her tongue ready to lash at him at any given moment.

"I asked if you had evidence?" Kyle repeated himself and that was when the police officer brought a device and showed Kyle a video that he couldn't even recall happening.

The video showed Kyle being aggressive towards her by basically ripping her bag strap.

"What the fuck is this?" Kyle thought but little did he know that this was how far AI had come.

This was a struggling actress and she couldn't exactly sponsor the life she lived so she had to find other means for that lifestyle to be maintained.

Kyle didn't even know how to defend himself and wasn't to, no one would believe him.

"That is fine… How much is it to sew back the handle?" Kyle said but this was the biggest mistake he could have made at that moment.

If he denied it then they would have to have a proper investigation but the fact that he accepted it acted as a confession.

"Luckily for you, Miss Calista is not interested in pressing charges as long as you get her a new bag," The officer said and Kyle simply chuckled.

"Did he say something funny!?" Calista barked, of all the people to scam she chose him but Kyle knew that this was payback for stopping her from smoking in his shop.

"Oh right, let me just get out the $6,000 I have lying around gotten from my luxurious job…" Kyle mocked her but this only made his situation worse.

Long story short, the police officer allowed Kyle to transfer everything he had in his account to her, which was barely up to $1,000 but that wasn't the worst of it, Kyle also lost his job.

And that was how he found himself at the edge of a bridge, ready to end it until an event that changed his life transpired came to pass.

Kyle was a man that never had any luck in life and he was on the verge of ending it all after being fired from his minimum wage job due to a customer's complaint which turned out to be a lie but due to the customer being an attractive lady, no one believed Kyle.

Beautiful people, they had it easy, didn't they?

This was his last step and he knew that he wouldn't see tomorrow as he searched for a river to throw himself in.

He thought his mind finally broke as there was no explanation for the Cyan Screen that popped up as he clicked the [YES] button.

[Congratulations! You have drawn Charm!]

[Charm: There is nothing the user can't charm his way out of or into! The universe has blessed you with charisma!]

"Hahaha! I must have lost my marbles…!" Kyle muttered to himself as he readied himself for the impact below but his phone soon rang out to show that he had a notification.

And to his surprise, he saw $10,000 sitting in his account and his jaw dropped.

"THIS IS FOR REAL!!!" Kyle screamed at the top of his lungs but this nearly sent him tumbling over the edge, Kyle immediately moved away from the edge.

"Fuck that! I want to live!" Kyle barked as he stared at his phone for a full minute.

He couldn't believe what his eyes were seeing, this was a lot more than what was in his account previously. a

"Holy fucking shit!" Kyle screamed at the top of his lungs and quickly recalled that he was out in public and had to compose himself.

There was one thing he took note of, the requirement for such a thing to come to pass in the first place was spending money on beautiful women.

That was way too easy but Kyle noticed that it was already in the middle of the night and crime was high in this area.

Kyle didn't waste time and headed back home in a heartbeat, his room was a mess but this was the least of his problems.

He jumped on his bed and instantly scrolled through his phone to see which girls he had saved there but the reality of this alone dawned on him.

"Damn… I really do get no girls…" Kyle muttered to himself but there was no use feeling sorry for himself.

He had to take care of business first and that was paying his rent or he would be homeless in no time.

After that, what he needed to do next was to find the next beautiful girl to spend money on the next day.

Kyle went from almost ending his life to having renewed hope for a potential future.

The night came to an end and Kyle was unemployed but who needed a job when life had given him a second lease?

Kyle didn't hesitate, he transferred $6,000 to his landlord because he had backed up rent so why not clear everything at once to get a clean slate?

It wasn't even up to a minute he sent the money but someone was banging on his door.

"KYLE! KYLE!" The voice called out, this was no doubt the voice of his landlord.

Kyle hurried to the door to see what prompted such a reaction and the moment the door opened, the landlord was out of breath.

It was clear he ran up here the moment he received the

"Have you sold your soul to the devil!?" Mr Jones questioned, shaking Kyle to see if he was still in there because there was no way he could cough up that amount from thin air.

"Hahaha! I did sell my soul to the devil but there is a catch…"Kyle said, pausing dramatically.

"Anyone that touches the money will forfeit their soul as well…" Kyle said expecting Mr. Jones to panic.

Mr Jones just stared at Kyle before letting out a faint chuckle and turning around.

"See you in hell then, buddy," Mr. Jones said before leaving the house.

He knew this was an obvious joke but this only showed the relationship between both men as Mr. Jones had a family to feed.

Kyle didn't know what to do for the rest of the day as he was now unemployed but he could recall the name of the lady that had fucked him over.

"Calista…" He muttered under his breath, realizing he might have the power to tame her in one way or another.

He quickly flipped through the internet, searching for her but to no avail, she must have been anything but famous hence why she resorted to such a trick in the first place.

Kyle sighed but just as he was about to give up, he stumbled on a page with a lady that looked exactly like her with the bio clearly pointing out that she was an actress/model in need of a gig.

Kyle smiled to himself, there had to be a way he could use this to his advantage but he needed to get his money up first.

He put on the nicest clothes he had but there was nothing fancy about his clothes seeing as he got them from the thrift store with his sense of fashion being anything but impressive.

Kyle noticed there was no money coming back to him upon paying for his rent so this thing only registered when he spent money on women or at least that was the information he managed to get from it.

Kyle stepped out but there was a spring in his steps as he had something to look forward to, this was California, Los Angeles, crawling with some of the most beautiful women in the world.

Maybe, just maybe he would bag himself an A-list celebrity but there was one thing he was sure about. He could only go up from her.

Kyle knew he had to be careful with the money in his possession because the condition for multiplying was spending it on beautiful women.

He looked around, desperately trying to find a pretty woman but the busy streets of Los Angeles only gave him seconds to approach them with everyone always in a hurry to get somewhere.

Kyle knew his best bet would be to visit a store and offer to pay for any pretty lady's goods.

But the store couldn't be too expensive, how embarrassing would it be if he offered to pay for something only to find out he couldn't afford it?

Kyle looked over his shoulder and luckily, there was an Apple store around the corner and what better place to blow money than Apple devices?

The moment Kyle took a step into the store, he was hit with the fresh air that reminded him the earth wasn't made up of heat.

But a lady slowly approached him with a wild smile on her face, Kyle could recognize that smile anywhere, it was the smile marketers wore right before advertising a product.

"Welcome sir, how may I help you?" A lady with blue eyes said.

Kyle found her mildly attractive as she was a bespectacled lady with somewhat of a slender build.

"I would like to buy a phone and I want to pay it in full…" Kyle pointed out and this was surprising as most people paid in installments but this made her suspicious.

"What is up with this guy?" She thought to herself, this lady was in her early twenties, she was exactly 20 years old but this information didn't matter to Kyle.

All that mattered to him at that moment was getting his money up as his eyes glanced around the store to see if there was anyone worthy of spending his money on.

However, his eyes met an almost empty store with employees all wearing an artificial smile.

Kyle sighed as he looked at the girl's name-tag before him.

"You're Jane… So Jane, how would you like an IPhone?" Kyle asked right away prompting Jane to raise a suspicious brow.

"Um… Sir, are you sure about that?" Jane was taken back by this, she had an old Apple device that was in need of replacing but it was still in optimal condition so she never got around to getting a new one. It wasn't like a phone was her top priority.

"I am absolutely certain! Let's get you the IPhone 15 Pro Max and a Mac Pro with a little phone insurance to sweeten the deal." Kyle said in an upbeat tone.

"Are you one of those Youtubers?" Jane questioned, looking around frantically for a camera but her eyes saw nothing.

The moment it settled on the man, he was already paying for the items, Jane was speechless but she wasn't going to turn down this act of kindness.

Chapter 4 - 4: System Upgrade!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Kyle had no idea what was going on but the first thing he did was to check his phone to make sure the money had been reflected in his bank account and the smile that crept up on his face made the cashier take a step back.

"Fuck yeah!" Kyle thought to himself, that he had over $50,000 in his bank account, which was more than he had ever had in his life.

Kyle didn't even realize that the system had added a new feature, it sunk in a second after checking his balance.

"Wait, what? Harem function?" Kyle thought to himself. He didn't need to verbalize his thoughts as the system took it upon itself to explain what it was.

[The harem function allows the user to recruit women into his harem by gaining their affection. A special cashback bonus is given in addition to the cashback granted by the system tier with each amount spent on the women within the user's harem.]

Kyle was perplexed by this, he thought this was all about money but he just found out there's a way to earn even more.

"Holy shit!" Kyle thought to himself, to everyone else he was staring at the cashier and making funny faces.

"Um, are you okay sir?" The cashier finally questioned, snapping Kyle back to reality.

Kyle realized he was in public and quickly composed himself, his items were packaged for him and he handed them to Jane.

Kyle looked at his Cyan screen and pressed the [YES] button so the draw could begin, he noticed that he had something called Extreme Lottery and hoped it was something decent otherwise it would be a waste.

[Congratulations! You have drawn Extreme Memory Recall!]

[Extreme Memory Recall: This user has the ability to recall every information or events experienced right down to the most insignificant details.]

[Congratulations! You have drawn Vehicular Mastery!]

[Vehicular Mastery: The user is an innate master of operating vehicles. Be it cars, bikes, or even planes!]

[Congratulations! You have drawn a Jaguar XF!]

[The Jaguar XF: The user owns an exotic car! It can be found in the driveway!]

[Congratulations! You have drawn JPD Agency Group!]

[JPD Agency Group: The user is now the owner of a small acting Agency in Beverly Hills! It is promising and has the potential to be among the best! The necessary documents can be found in your car alongside the address!]

Kyle had the biggest smile on his face, his life was turning around in the blink of an eye and he wasn't about to question it.

Kyle walked out of the building and the only car that looked out of place hence easy to locate.

The car key spawned within his pocket.

Kyle ran to the car but not before someone tugged at his shirt, Kyle looked back and realized that it was Jane.

She gave him her number on a piece of paper but she couldn't even hold his gaze, Kyle noticed her colleagues giggling at the back as he realized that they were probably the ones who urged her to give him her number.

[Harem Members: 1/2]

[Name: Jane.]

[Age: 23

[Affection: 5%]

Kyle stopped in his tracks and spared her another glance only to see her stealing a glance at him before averting her gaze once again.

The system not only told him women that are interested in him so he didn't need to guess but it also granted him items that spawned in real time.

This felt surreal, if this was black magic, Kyle hoped the caster wouldn't stop because he wasn't even half-done.

Kyle wanted to check out the property that he had acquired, opening the door to his car and the new car smell hit his nostrils.

"Now that's a new car!" Kyle thought, luckily he had a driver's license due to one of the jobs he had gotten in the past involving delivery.

Kyle, thanks to the skill acquired by his system, could drive the car with relative ease

He instantly knew how to operate it but he first needed to find the documents of the agency he now owned and just as he suspected, it was there.

Everything was signed and official in his name.

-

"Damn it, this agency might as well eat a double-decked shit sandwich! I chose you and I haven't had a half-decent gig in years!" A lady barked at a woman with a clipboard who just stared at her blankly.

"The lack of jobs isn't because of our inability to find jobs, I think it is time for you to consider actual acting classes…" The woman responded, her face unmoved as she looked unimpressed by what she had seen.

"You bitch! I am naturally born talent!" The lady continued to bark prompting the woman to take a deep breath.

But before she could respond, one of her employees called her to the side.

This lady had a powerful aura around her, she was clearly in her mid-twenties but the power she exuded showed that she was used to leading.

She had that

"What is it?" The woman asked in a dismissive tone, the truth about this was that this agency was about to go under, they were barely floating above water as people would rather choose the more established agencies.

"Miss Isabella, I have great news!" The employee responded, she was giddy and Isabella could instantly tell that it might be something worth listening to.

"Speak Carly, I'm a busy wo-!" But before Isabella could complete her sentence, Carly interfered by showing her a page on her phone.

Isabella's face went from neutral to surprised, she immediately snatched the device out of her hand.

"What is this!? Someone bought the entire share of the agency!? This means…!" Isabella screamed before clasping her mouth shut.

"We are saved, boss!" Carly responded.

"Are you done!? I have places to be!" The talent-less upcoming actress exclaimed and Isabella realized at this moment that she didn't need to put up with this anymore.

She walked up to the lady, she had to tolerate her before because they needed all the money they could get but that problem had been solved by Kyle.

"I am afraid you're no longer qualified to work with us, you will be refunded your agency fees. Leave or I will throw you out myself, you spoiled brat," Isabella felt the weight lift off her chest the moment she said this, she had wanted to say this for the longest time.

She already knew that the new shareholder would be coming here at any moment and realized she had to prepare the place to welcome him.

Chapter 5 - 5: JPD New Owner!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Kyle arrived at his new agency and it was a lot bigger than he thought, it had an intricate design that exuded elegance with top-class infrastructure.

It stood out compared to the buildings around it but this was one of the more expensive parts of Los Angeles.

Kyle realized only after he arrived that he should have bought something fancy to wear as first impressions mattered.

The clothes he wore weren't a reflection of his status, anyone would mistake him for an average bum.

Kyle exited the car and Carly was already waiting outside to welcome him.

When Kyle left the car, Carly was surprised that he looked like the average American.

He didn't stand out but this only made her mistake this as humbleness and not the fact that he was indeed a bum only a few hours ago.

"Good day sir, I have come to personally welcome you to the JPD," Carly greeted with a smile but Kyle was surprised that this was a genuine smile.

She was truly glad to see him prompting him to wonder why but this was an insignificant detail.

"Thank you…" Kyle said, pausing abruptly as he looked at her name tag.

"Carly, are you the CEO of this agency?" Kyle questioned but this only made Carly blush at the thought of him thinking she could hold that position not knowing that Kyle had no idea how a CEO should look.

"I'm afraid not, sir. She has been expecting you," Carly responded as she led the way.

"Alright," Kyle responded, he didn't know what else to say as he was still shocked this was happening.

Kyle entered the building and the fragrance that invaded his nostrils sent him into a trance.

"This place is so fucking fancy!" Kyle thought to himself as he was led to an office at the end of a corridor.

"Miss Isabella will see you now," Carly said but she emphasized the Miss as if subtly telling Kyle she was single. However, Kyle wasn't paying enough attention to pick up on this.

Kyle made his way into the room and to his surprise, there sat a woman in a white uniform top with a black shirt just above her knee, exposing a little bit of her thighs in the process with long socks and high heels.

But the thing that got Kyle's attention was how her shirt was barely able to keep those weapons of mass destruction intact without the buttons popping off.

Kyle gulped as he stepped inside the room, there was a transparent table separating them but that was an interesting choice.

She got up the moment Kyle entered, he was officially her boss and what he did to the agency was up to him however this was her moment to show him her vision.

"Mr. Kyle! So nice to meet you, I'm Isabella!" Isabella greeted, stretching her hand for a handshake.

She was confident no doubt but this didn't scare Kyle, he has met women like her at his various jobs and they all had one thing in common, they were stuck-up bitches.

And Kyle knew she was no different from them, the only reason she was like this was due to his position and it wasn't a reflection of who she was.

*Isabella, that's a great name but I would like for us to get down to business," Kyle knew he had to make a strong first impression.

"O-Of course, please sit," Isabella stammered, she didn't know why but just as Kyle knew how important a first impression was, so did Isabella.

"You don't need to be so nervous, I'm not the grim reaper or anything," Kyle made a silly joke, trying his best to break the evident tension in the air.

Isabella was embarrassed that he noticed that, even if Kyle didn't have the skill granted by the system, he would still have spotted this without a doubt.

"I'm sorry, sir," Isabella quickly apologized. Kyle thought she was a powerful woman when he walked in here but she was awfully submissive right now.

"I have to ask, why the transparent table?" Kyle couldn't hold his curiosity as it was a bizarre choice, to say the least.

"Our motto is transparency so this is a literal demonstration that we hide nothing," Isabella responded, she knew how corny this sounded but it was a clever marketing strategy.

"Ah, that is clever! Now that is how you get the client!" Kyle was a little more excited than he should have been and Isabella blushed as she didn't expect this reaction from him.

He had taken the first step of achieving his goal and that was to conquer Los Angeles.

-

Calista sobbed her heart out, she cried until she wasn't able to but usually, when they reject or retract a given role from an individual.

They were informed that Calista hadn't received the message yet but she knew it was coming.

Lucas Rossini was famous as he was controversial, there had been rumours about him sleeping with women but it has neither been confirmed nor debunked but there was no doubt that the women had a boost in their career after this.

Calista, however, got a call from Isabella. A call she wasn't expecting to say the least.

"Hello?" Calista greeted, her voice hoarse from all the crying she had done.

[[Is something wrong Miss Wave?]] Isabella was quick to ask.

"No, no. I'm alright. Just allergies," Calista dismissed her concern, she couldn't tell her what had happened after all, she didn't want her pity.

[[Take care of yourself. I have been informed that you got the role and formally wish to congratulate you.]] Isabella said but this took Calista unaware.

She was certain that it would have been retracted by now, why was it taking so long?

"Thank you but are you sure I got the role?" Calista had to be sure, she wanted to confirm when Isabella was told about the role.

[[I just got off the call, a contract has been sent over as well]] Isabella informed her.

Calista was shocked, Lucas hadn't taken away her role despite her turning down his advances.

Chapter 47 - 47: Apology.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Kyle finally returned home, his body didn't go through too much physical assertion but he felt weak the moment the building came into view.

He could already imagine his body on the soft cushion, embracing the beauty of slumber.

Kyle walked out of his car after parking it, he saw Mr Jones' car parked outside as well.

This made him feel easy for some reason but there was no telling what awaited him inside the building.

He hoped that he wouldn't run into Aiysha again.

He had to find a way to distance himself from her because he knew what could potentially happen if they kept running into each other.

Kyle didn't trust himself to always have such self-control, he hoped that Calista had left his home so he could have his space all to himself.

He needed it and fortunately for Kyle, he didn't run into either Aiysha or Calista.

Kyle walked right into his bedroom, crashing into the soft mattress in front of him, he didn't know living could be so exhausting.

"Why am I so tired having done so little?" Kyle thought to himself.

He didn't know what else to do because he couldn't sleep either, he was restless for some reason.

Calista's scent still lingered and only then did Kyle remember what she had told him earlier on the phone.

He sighed, staring at the ceiling, contemplating his lift.

Kyle couldn't believe he had come this far, there was no telling what heights he could reach.

The sky wasn't the limit for him, it was just the beginning.

"I should probably text her, shouldn't I?" Kyle muttered, lazily taking out his phone from his pocket.

"Fuck, do I have to…" Kyle blurted out, typing on his phone.

[[Hey, I know you think you bombed the interview but I'm certain that you will get that role, I have no single doubt in my being so get ready to nail that role! I believe in you,]] Kyle texted, it was cliche and corny but that should do the trick.

Reassurance was the ultimate human delusion that fed one's need for security, a very powerful tool when it came to the act of manipulation.

But before he could put the phone down, Calista replied immediately.

[[Calista: You wouldn't believe it! I got the role! I don't know how you have so much faith in me but thank you!!]]

[[Kyle: I knew you could do it! You're Calista Wave for crying out loud, there's nothing you can't do. I mean you even got me fir-!] Kyle thought about it for a second before deciding to erase a few words.

[[Kyle: I knew you could do it! You're Calista Wave for crying out loud, there's nothing you can't do!]]

[[Calista: Hey Kyle, about that day…]]

[[Calista: I never got the chance to apologize for getting you fired. You embarrassed me so I wanted to hurt you. I didn't mean for you to lose your job.]]

"The fuck?" The disgust on Kyle's face spoke volumes because she had an edited video created by AI.

Now that Kyle thought about it, he would have been found innocent if he stood his ground but he was already so mentally exhausted.

Even though he knew that wouldn't have stopped him from losing his job because he worked for a moron.

Kyle didn't believe a word she said, she went too far just to hurt him unless she was a sociopath.

[[Kyle: It's fine, I was going to quit soon either way but you really were a cunt. LOL.]]

[[Calista: I'm so glad! I can neither deny nor confirm the allegations at this time. >_<]]

Kyle chuckled, this was a funny response but it didn't change his mind about her.

Calista was only apologizing because she had more to lose than gain if she lost Kyle.

"What the hell is that emoji though?" Kyle questioned, confused as to what it represented.

This was his lack of social skills sipping through.

[[Calista: I enjoy spending time with you, Kyle]]

Calista double texted after Kyle hadn't replied immediately due to trying to figure out what that emoji meant.

"More like you like the money but okay," Kyle thought to himself.

[[Kyle: What a coincidence! Because I enjoy spending time with you too, another thing with have in common!]]

[[Calista: Another? What's the first?]]

Kyle smiled because she took the bait, he was about to deliver the punchline.

[[Kyle: Where's the fun in that?]]

Kyle knew this would get her in her head trying to figure out what it was, sometimes a cliffhanger is much more effective than a conclusive story.

It keeps the readers guessing to the point of sheer madness but what if Kyle could condition her to associate her dopamine to him?

That was the end of the text, Calista responded but Kyle didn't bother checking it because his eyes had shut.

-

Aiysha welcomed her husband, he was happy and ecstatic right from the first step into his home.

"Darling, are you okay?" Aiysha asked her husband with a smile, eager to share in his excitement.

"I am! I am! I think the solution to all our problems just arrived, honey!" Jones exclaimed, he was like a child that just discovered a new flavour of ice cream.

Jones didn't pay her much attention and she knew why, it was their kids.

He always went to them first to tell them he was home and do a little ritual that was exclusive to them.

Jones was a great father and an even better husband

He came back out and focused all his attention on his wife and Aiysha's face went from shocked to surprise to shock once again.

"Kyle offered that?" Aiysha couldn't believe what she was hearing.

"Yes, baby! I don't want us to celebrate just yet until everything is signed and sealed but things are looking good!" Jones never knew that his single act of kindness would yield such a reward.

In the end, maybe kindness does pay.

Chapter 48 - 48: Nightmare
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

There was good news all around but Kyle was in a deep slumber, he didn't like sleeping previously because he never had peace of mind.

He always had to wake up due to the requirements from his work but either way, Kyle knew he had priorities.

But he had a strange dream, Kyle couldn't recall the place or scene but was a seven-year-old child, standing in the middle of an abandoned playground.

Kyle looked around, there was no one there, no cars, or any sign of life.

This place felt familiar even though he had no memory of it, Kyle had the consciousness of his current self even though he was a child in this dream.

"Hello!?" Kyle called out but got no response, only the echo of his voice.

Kyle knew he had to explore the area, so he walked around hoping to find signs of life.

He walked carefully, this didn't feel like a dream, if anything it felt like reality was mixed into some morbid fantasy.

"Where the fuck am I?" Kyle thought to himself, looking at his hands, it was so small.

"This isn't right…" Kyle muttered to himself in fear but saw a figure seated a couple of paces in his front with its back turned towards him.

It had long brunette hair dangling just above its lower back, Kyle knew this person wasn't there a moment ago so how did they magically appear?

Either way, this was the sign of life he searched for, approaching the person slowly.

"... Hello?" Kyle called out in, a calm voice, as if he didn't want anyone but them to hear it.

He got no response, not even a twitch to acknowledge his presence. Kyle expected this much but once again, there was something familiar about this.

Kyle couldn't wrap his fingers around it but he knew this person or at least he thought he did.

The way they sat, their posture and hair but he was within range.

He grabbed the shoulder of the person, slowly turning them around but what he saw horrified him.

Kyle saw a person with no eyes in their sockets, no tongue, and blood dripping out of their ears.

It tried to speak but it couldn't, Kyle was frightened and backed away instantly, dropping on his arse.

"What the fuck man!?" Kyle barked.

The figure got to its feet and slowly walked towards Kyle with its hand outstretched.

He tried to crawl away from her, but his legs were weak and didn't respond to him.

"This has to be a fucking dream!" Kyle exclaimed, he tried to force himself awake but to no avail.

He wasn't waking up regardless of how hard he tried to but suddenly, the sluggish creature was walking towards him accelerated, making Usain Bolt look like a snail, as it was mere inches from grabbing Kyle's face, only then did Kyle jolt awake.

His breathing was heavy and sweat trickled down his forehead but the problem with this nightmare was that Kyle couldn't remember anything that had just transpired or at least that would have been the case before he came into contact with the system.

"I-I remember…" Kyle was shocked, he remembered every single thing he had seen in this nightmare which made it scarier. He no longer had the luxury of forgetting.

Kyle was still in a dazed state but checked the time only to realize he had slept for approximately two hours, it was the early hours of the evening and the time was reading 3:12 PM.

Kyle didn't have anything planned on his agenda today but had to get out of the house to get some fresh air. It felt like he would suffocate if he stayed here any longer.

Kyle rushed to the bathroom and placed his head under the shower to cool off before looking at his reflection in the mirror.

He didn't like what he saw, he realized that he needed a haircut. He needed to change his overall appearance so he could look neat.

He looked a tad bit unkempt and Kyle realized that his next destination should be a beauty parlour even if he had no idea what kind of haircut he wanted.

Kyle stumbled out of his apartment but didn't feel the need to use the vehicle.

He opted to walk instead because he needed to clear his head and didn't need the attention that came with driving such a vehicle.

Kyle didn't want to be perceived as he headed to the nearest salon to get a haircut.

The scorching heat didn't do him any favour but it was the least of his problems, Kyle kept recalling the figure he saw in his nightmare and what it could mean.

Why was it in such a state?

"WATCH IT PUNK!" A man barked at him the moment Kyle bumped into him.

"I'm sorry!" Kyle quickly apologized. Fortunately for Kyle, this was enough to de-escalate the situation.

The man hissed in frustration and walked away but Kyle knew he had to get it together.

He realized that this skill to remember everything might be a curse rather than a blessing.

Kyle didn't need to walk for long because he was fortunate to see a salon around the corner.

He had to take care of himself, he had to look how he wished to be addressed.

People couldn't keep paying attention to him just because he drove a fancy car, he had to groom himself to garner that respect.

Kyle entered the salon and the breeze that brushed against his skin dried up the sweat just as fast. It made him feel nasty but one of the barbers was rounding up with a customer.

He gestured for Kyle to come over and have a seat, Kyle did just that.

The man was covered in tattoos from head to toe, he looked like the cliche thug you would see in a movie. He belonged in a biker gang, not a salon.

"What hairstyle do you want, buddy?" He asked but Kyle had nothing in mind.

"You know what? Surprise me," But if Kyle would come to regret this decision was to be seen.

Chapter 49 - 49: Parasite
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Kyle was all done with his hairstyle, he prayed to God that this man wouldn't butcher his hair like an executioner.

How are you going to be a barber when you are bald? You do not have any hair to style so how do you style others?

Kyle wondered but the result of his hairstyle changed his mind the moment he caught a glimpse of it in the mirror.

"...And all done," The bald barber said and Kyle couldn't believe what he was looking at.

He looked handsome as fuck, his lifeless face hidden by the strands of hair that often dangled were nowhere to be seen, bringing his full face into view.

It was an undercut, the sides of his hair were shaven, giving him a clean look as it amplified his green eyes.

"Goddamn! I look gorgeous!" Kyle exclaimed, his reaction brought a smile to the barber's face.

An appreciation for his craft was something that he would always appreciate.

"What's your name, big fella?" Kyle asked, he no longer was intimidated by his appearance and as this man had just created something divine on his head.

"My name? I'm called Big Bob," Bob introduced himself but paused momentarily because he didn't know why Kyle wanted his name.

"And just so you know, I don't swing that way," Bob informed Kyle as politely as he could.

"Oh! Hahaha!" Kyle laughed out loud upon realizing how he must have come off for Bob to have this impression.

"It is a pity, I'm sure you would have been a catch but I'm not interested in men either," Kyle pointed out to clear up any misunderstanding.

"Are you interested in being my barber? I'm not a fan of having the service of multiple people when it comes to things like this," Kyle requested. There was no way that Bob would say no to an opportunity to make more money.

"Sure thing man," Bob said, handing Kyle his contact information. Bob was a lot friendlier than he had anticipated.

Kyle left the salon and felt like a new man, he could feel every fibre of his being bustling with energy.

He was never an ugly individual, if anything, he was on the attractive side but his unkempt look was the deterring factor.

But he got all the right attention now, people were looking at him in awe as they found him attractive.

Kyle smiled inwardly, his life was beginning to take shape slowly and the nightmare he had slowly faded from his immediate train of thought.

-

Jane was closing from work, she had carried out her shift and it was time to head back home.

Kyle made her the star of the day, everyone was grateful to her because she was the reason they all got new phones but this was a clever way to make everyone like Jane if they didn't already.

This would put her on good them but also make them supportive of her relationship with him.

The whispers were powerful and what better whispers to fill her ears than people telling her how lucky she was to have him? Jane still couldn't believe he had come to see her.

"Hey…" A voice called out behind her, Jane turned to see Mike standing behind the door she just exited.

"Hey Mike, are you headed out too?" Jane asked, that couldn't be the case as Mike worked longer hours.

"No, I just wanted to help you hail down a cab," Mike said.

"... Just like old times," He made sure to add, Mike was the kind of guy who acted like a parasite, hanging around and hoping that a rejection would turn to acceptance after a series of heartbreaks from the lady he fancied. She would see all men are bad and come running to him.

The long game, that was what Mike was playing.

"I don't think there is any need for that, thank you, Mike," Jane declined with a smile but this was the first time she was doing this.

Mike gritted his teeth, he knew it was because of that man.

That had to be the reason for this sudden change in character but he wasn't going to back down.

"C'mon, I insist," Mike said, proceeding to exit the building and stand by the side of the road.

This was harmless so Jane conceded, she didn't mind him doing this for her but she knew it would be unfair for her to keep expecting certain things from him even after rejecting him.

Jane walked up to the roadside to meet him, there were rarely any empty taxis by this time of the day as someone was already in them.

"I appreciate this Mike," Jane was quick to express her gratitude.

"It is fine!" Mike downplayed it but that was the end of the conversation, Jane didn't attempt to strike up any small talk leaving Mike to do the honour.

"So…" Mike began, capturing Jane's attention.

"That guy… I haven't seen him before, is he your boyfriend?" Mike tried to hide his jealousy as best as he could and it worked, he managed to present this as a normal question.

Jane's face flushed red the moment she heard the word boyfriend, she never knew what Kyle was to her but she knew that she liked him.

They hadn't done anything about labels and she didn't want to spook him either but the thought of Kyle being hers turned her brain to mush.

"H-He is not! At least I don't think he is," Jane responded but Mike hated this.

He hated that another man brought out this side of her and not him.

"You don't think? I would think people know when they're dating," Mike responded, masking the question behind a series of fake laughter.

Jane already disconnected from the conversation, stuck in her head because she was thinking about how Kyle would look being her boyfriend.

But Mike got his answer, they weren't dating yet and that meant that it was fair game.

"I still have a chance!" Mike thought to himself.

Chapter 50 - 50: The White Whale Movie.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Kyle was done with his haircut but he didn't want to go back home straight away because he needed the fresh air.

He sat in a park and simply took in his environment, appreciating everything that came with being alive because attempting suicide was no easy feat.

It took a different level of bravery and that still affected Kyle. He decided to end it all only to find something to live for but this emptiness still existed within him.

A part of him that longed for human connection.

Maybe that's why the system introduced a harem category. Did it understand his innermost desires, he had done things he wouldn't have done otherwise thanks to the affection bar and that was why he felt closer to Jane.

Without that affection bar, he wouldn't be inclined to be so direct and the affection bar in real time would have been dangling at around 20% or thereabout.

Jane wouldn't be as taken as she was with him, and neither would Calista, even though the latter was more of an experiment than actual interest.

Kyle threw back his head, staring at the sky but knew this was just the start.

He had so much to do, he went from having no dreams to having one people would call unrealistic.

Money commanded attention but status commanded respect.

He couldn't have any doubts whatsoever but he began to understand the system only adds women he's attracted to but also if they have feelings for him.

Kyle simply had to control how he interacted with these women, he had to do so carefully.

His legs were already exhausted, he wasn't exactly in peak human condition.

"Perhaps I do need to exercise…" Kyle pondered on Aiysha's offer but he couldn't risk interacting with her more than he needed to.

There were other gyms around and there was no way that Aiysha would know if he decided to apply to a different gym with another trainer but what if she does find out?

"Aargh!" Kyle muttered to himself.

This was becoming a lot more complicated than he anticipated.

He knew the influences wives had over their husbands and that was the last thing Kyle needed due to Mr. Jones not just being his landlord anymore but a business partner as well.

Kyle needed a distraction, he had spoken to Calista a little too much recently and knew that too much communication would do more harm than good.

He had just spoken with Jane and knew he needed to give her space to miss him.

But then he remembered, he had a new lady on his radar. He had Cassandra even though she hadn't given him any green light.

Kyle knew trying to get would be fun, that was the thing with most married couples.

After a few years, the passion and romance die and what better way to reignite that dying flame in one's heart than with a stranger that reminds them of the desire long lost?

Kyle took out his phone but he saw a missed call from Isabella.

"Huh?" Kyle thought to himself not because of the missed call but because he didn't hear it ring.

He shot Isabella a call, hoping it wasn't anything important and it didn't take long for her to pick up.

Kyle knew if he wasn't who he was, a lady like Isabella would never have bothered picking up the phone.

[[Good evening sir…]] Isabella began the conversation.

"Hello Isabella, how're you? Is everything alright over there?" Kyle inquired, he had told her to inform him if she needed anything previously and as such, entertained that as a possibility for this conversation.

[[I'm great sir and everything is better than ever!]] Isabella reassured him before continuing.

[[I have called to inform you that Calista Wave has secured the role.]] Isabella informed him.

"You did everything I told you to?" Kyle questioned.

[[That is right sir, I have told the Distort Company that "The White Whale" film is being produced that we were willing to cover 15% of production cost if they used one of ours as their main cast.]] Isabella informed him.

Kyle smiled to himself, Calista's life revolved around his fingers and he could easily bring it all crashing due with a snap of his finger.

Kyle knew that he needed to start small and gradually gain contacts that opened the door for him to enter the doors of Hollywood.

"Thank you for this, Isabella," Kyle responded.

Calista didn't retain her role because Lucas decided to spare her, Lucas knew that Calista being chosen was a directive the higher-ups gave him.

[[The $15,000,000 has also been transferred from the Agency's primary funds to conclude the deal. Would that be all, sir?]] Isabella questioned.

She was keeping a professional tone but she wanted to know who Calista was for him to go the extra mile for.

"That will be all. Thank you, Isabella. We need to get as many actors or actresses under our Agency out there so others will be inclined to register under us," Kyle knew how actions came off as strange to her but this cover story did its job in giving her an answer.

"And what better way than to show the world that even someone like Calista could get a lead role in a movie?" Kyle dropped a rhetorical question but Isabella got the gist of it.

Kyle had picked Calista due to her poor track and used her as an example to show what the Agency is capable of even if it was a facade, it was a great marketing tool.

She has underestimated him, swallowing up the lie because it was a good one in all honesty.

[[That is genius! I can't believe I never thought of this!]] Isabella exclaimed

"Good thing I'm here, get some rest Isabella," Kyle concluded.

[[I will sir, thank you for giving me a chance to show you my vision,]] Isabella showed her gratitude that Kyle retained and gave her full control of his company.

"You're too modest. I will talk to you later," Kyle responded, hanging up the phone.

If by some miracle, his account was discovered, this amount could be explained.

Kyle was aware that everything had a loophole and he wasn't going to trust that his system didn't have one either even though it might very well be.

He took out his phone and searched for Cassandra, he remembered his conversation with Jones about marriage.

Kyle knew that he could make Cassandra betray her vows, it wouldn't be an impossible task.

This was the text message that Kyle sent, it was simple yet playful so there wouldn't be any misinterpretation.

[[Cassandra: ദ്ദി(ᵔOᵔ)]]

[[Kyle: Someone has a naughty mind, all I suggested was that we meet up but it looks like you have already begun to undress me. If anything, I should be the one uncomfortable. THIS IS SEXUAL HARASSMENT!]

Once again, he didn't get an immediate reply and this was because Cassandra was thinking about it.

[[Cassandra: I guess lunch couldn't hurt, how about tomorrow?]]

A smirk creased Kyle's face, she had taken the bait.

Kyle knew by the way she dressed that she must have some sort of body dysmorphia, she was all covered up despite the scorching hot sun.

There was something about women when it came to their insecurity, you had to compliment that particular insecurity before they told you it was an insecurity.

This would tell them subconsciously that you liked what they hated about themselves, which meant they were more inclined to do as they were told regarding that specific thing.

"Validation…" Kyle muttered under his breath.

[[Cassandra: Oh my God! I don't look like a supermodel if that's what you're thinking.]] Cassandra gushed but Kyle knew this was her way of seeking reassurance

[[Kyle: If anything, you set the standard for them. I will pick you up by noon.]]

This was the end of the conversation, Kyle gave her something to think about because now, she would be looking to impress him more than ever.

Chapter 52 - 52: Introducing Collins.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Kyle gave the man a call and usually, they would set a time for an appointment but they agreed to see Kyle immediately as it was an extension of their desperation.

Kyle met them up at a site but he knew there was a chance that this was a set-up and they were waiting around the corner to rob him.

But that was highly unlikely, Kyle used an Uber to get to his destination because he had left his car back home and had no regrets.

Kyle didn't mind this and arrived in less than twenty minutes.

He left the car and noticed that the site had all kinds of fancy construction trucks around, this was either to show them that they were the real deal.

There were no buildings on site, just an RV in the middle.

Kyle waited outside for a second, even the Uber he came with questioned if he would be alright and lingered around for a bit longer to confirm if he would be safe.

This was an act of kindness but Kyle assured him he would be fine because whoever was in that RV was expecting him so it didn't take long for them to exit the vehicle.

It was a man in his late-forties but his body was built like a Greek God that was summoned from Oympus to show mankind the next step of evolution.

His grey hair betrayed him despite his face doing anything but look his age.

"Hello there. You're Kyle? The one I spoke with on the phone?" The man inquired.

Kyle was expecting someone his age and was surprised to see a man this advanced in years standing before him but this made his choice of workplace even more questionable.

There had to be a reason his body was like this and that was all the heavy lifting that came with construction, Kyle immediately knew he was the real deal.

"That is correct. And you're Collins, right?" Kyle responded, approaching the older man.

Collins nodded to confirm this, gesturing for Kyle to enter his RV, Kyle wasn't sure if this was his workplace or where he lived but he was hesitant to enter.

What if he never came out or worse, was taken hostage because if he screamed at the top of his lungs, no one would hear him this far out but there was no way he would leave a witness if he planned to do something.

Kyle shook his head at the absurd thought, he didn't know how it even made its way into his mind.

He entered the RV and to his utmost surprise.

The RV was decorated and set up like a proper office, it wasn't anything cheap either which further confirmed his legitimacy even though his choice of workplace was strange, no doubt.

"I see James wasn't kidding," Collins said sternly, gesturing for Kyle to sit which he did while he sat opposite him with a table separating them.

" Good, if James has briefed you then you know exactly why I am here," Kyle said, glad he could skip all the formalities and get straight to the point.

The anchor wasn't going to be him, it was going to be her career, the career he was going to help her build with his influence.

"N-Not exactly," Laura responded but Calista saw her teeth, they looked like they were rotting.

"What the hell?" Calista thought, this was a sad sight and she realized at that moment that she must have fallen victim to the dark side of this life they all chased.

In no time, two bulky men approached her and asked her to leave.

Laura served as a grim reminder of what she would become if she wasn't successful in this endeavour.

Chapter 72 - 72: Makeover.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Kyle made preparations  for his meeting that night, he returned home without Calista seeing him, there was another exit in which he used to leave the building.

It was time to get him a proper suit. Kyle went to one of the more elegant stores because he had to look expensive to command the respect he intended.

It took over an hour to get this sorted but Kyle ended up buying more than five suits for the future.

He didn't want to come to the shop each time he needed something so bought it in bulk along with some other corporate clothing that he might need.

He had no sense of fashion nor did he know which would be appropriate to wear in these kinds of meetings.

Kyle got into his car and headed home but he hoped not to run into Aiysha. He was beginning to dread their interactions because he didn't know how long it would be before that line was crossed.

That was one thing he couldn't do to Jones, it wouldn't be an exaggeration to say that Jones had saved his life but then again, wouldn't he be doing him a favor if his wife does turn out to be unfaithful?

Because if she was then it would be safe that she did these sorts of things with her clients or maybe this was his porn brain influencing him.

Kyle arrived there but he saw Jones' car parked in the driveway, he didn't know why but he felt a sense of relief wash over him.

"Wait, Jones should have an idea," Kyle thought to himself but he wasn't going to knock at his door because he didn't know if Aiysha was home or not.

The best course of action would be to do so over a phone call, he had his number after all.

Kyle went back home and took a quick shower, he hoped the water wouldn't stop midway.

Fortunately for him, it didn't and this would be a good chance to finalize the quota for the building.

He gave Jones the go ahead to get the proceedings done in terms of involving a lawyer to draw up a contract.

"Goddamn! I actually do look good," Kyle muttered to himself, the eyebags were disappearing and he was no longer looking like a haggard old man.

No one would be able to guess what his life was like just a few days ago because he looked like a different man.

Kyle was happy with the way his life was turning out but there was one missing component, his body wasn't to his desired effect.

He wanted a little muscle, enough so he would have the physical power to not only defend himself but Jane.

It would be embarrassing if he confronted Mike and Mike ended up beating the shit out of him, that was a scenario that he knew must not come to pass.

Finally, he called Mr. Jones and it didn't even ring twice before Jones picked up his phone.

[[Jones here,]] Jones began the conversation.

"Kyle here, are you home? I could use your help," Kyle requested, getting right to the point.

[[I'm home… Is this a sex thing?]] Jones asked after a brief pause.

"What the hell? If this was a sex thing, I would have the decency to take you out first," Kyle fired back but the fact that he indulged Jones to this extent was proof of their growing relationship.

[[I'm allergic to garlic.]] Jones responded with an awkward silence following shortly after.

"I AM NOT TAKING YOU OUT!" Kyle screamed.

[[Excuse me for thinking I'm worth some dinner.]] Jones wasn't backing down but Kyle knew he had to take a deep breath or Jones would win.

"Fine, I will get you some dinner," Kyle conceded.

[[Ah! I knew this was a sex thing!]] Jones barked and at the moment, Kyle was wondering why he didn't just go to his house and drag his ass out.

"JUST GET HERE!" Kyle shouted. Once upon a time, he couldn't even think of talking to Jones like this but this new form of communication wasn't because of his sudden increase in status but rather, their relationship evolving to this level.

Kyle didn't have to wait for long because a second after, there was a knock on his door.

He heaved a sigh of relief as he walked towards the door with a towel tied firmly around his waist.

Kyle paused for a second, he knew if he opened the door wearing this then Jones was definitely going to talk about the sex thing.

He grumbled before breaking away into his room to grab a trouser.

He finally returned to open the door and Jones was waiting, Kyle wanted to ask why he wasn't at work but her knew that this conversation could be had at a later time.

He had a few more hours until his meeting and that took priority right now.

"Is there something wrong?" Jones demeanour changed immediately when they were face-to-face.

No one would guess the conversation they just had moments ago.

"Yes, I need your help to pick out a suit. I have a very important meeting and I didn't know who else to call…" Kyle got right to it.

Jones could tell this was important by the way he spoke but also felt flattered that he was Kyle's go to person. It meant that Kyle trusted his judgement for something this important.

Without saying another word, Kyle brought the clothes he had bought and returned to show Jones.

Jones couldn't help but admire what he had done with the place and this could potentially be how every room in this building looked.

But he wasn't going to let his mind wander, they had to handle what was at hand right now.

"I need your help, which do you think will be appropriate for a meeting?" Kyle asked, rubbing his lower chin.

"I need to know what setting the meeting would be held in." Jones responded, he was taking this seriously and Kyle was relieved with this reaction.

He kenw he could let Jones in on his business, he trusted him funny enough.

"It is a potential partnership with a very big entertainment company but it is being held at night which is a bit weird," Kyle responded, taking a seat across him after laying the suits across the table between them.

"A meeting at night with a big entertainment company? You don't need to dress too serious but you also can't dress too casual. You need to show your sense of fashion, it would help in how you're perceived but if you wear a suit at night? I personally would think you were a person that played by the books.

If it is a big corporation, you can't give them such a narrow-minded perception so suits are a no go… But a blazer in place of a suit would do you good. I also noticed you have no accessories, I never see you with any jewelries, you should probably get some, " Jones have an in-depth analysis of what he saw.

Kyle was surprised that he could see things in this light because these were thoughts he didn't even know were possible.

Kyle realized that Jones was spot on.

There was no way a big entertainment got there by playing by the rules so if he came dressed like a lawyer, they might take it as a sign of his ability to be inflexible but wearing a blazer would make it harder to get a read on him but he had no jewelries.

That was the only problem, Kyle never bothered with these things but if Jones thought it would help then he saw no harm in doing it.

"I don't have any jewelries and I don't even know what would be appropriate to wear," Kyle confessed.

" You don't need to worry about that but first, how do you feel about piercing your ears?" Jones asked and Kyle raised a brow at this suggestion.

"Huh?" Kyle was confused.

Jones was taking an interest in this a lot more than he thought he would. It was like he wanted to use Kyle as some a guinea pig for an unrealized dream.

Kyle already came this far and saw no reason why he needed to back out now.

-

A few hours later, Kyle looked like a completely different man and he was stunned that Jones had such a skillset under his belt because he looked like your everyday dad yet he managed to make Kyle into a godlike specimen.

But Kyle had also gotten a piercing in his respective ears with a green dye on one side of his face but only on the tip.

But the moment of truth was in a few hours, Kyle crossed his fingers and hoped that this would be enough.

Chapter 73 - 73: The Owner Of Distort?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Kyle was ready for his meeting. He couldn't believe what Jones had done, but he was grateful because he couldn't have done this himself.

Kyle was aware of the effects this might have, whether this look worked or not but before Jones left, he told him something.

"The people you're going to meet aren't attempting to host a meeting but size you out, entertainment industry also means your fashion sense matters. Do not be intimidated and also don't be serious,"

This made perfect sense and Kyle knew that this philosophy was sound but it was time.

He had approximately $206,000,000 in his account, it was depleting at a rapid rate but he wasn't spending on frivolous things.

Everything he spent on was an investment, it wasn't just being spent on useless things.

Kyle hopped into his car, Jones had also helped with a perfume that he thought could help.

This was the moment of truth, night had fallen and Kyle tore through the streets of Los Angeles.

Kyle was feeling nervous, he knew that these people had a way of seeing if someone belonged to their social class or not.

It took a couple of minutes for him to arrive at his destination. He left his home 30 minutes early in case there was traffic but luckily, the road was clear.

He parked the car outside the location but he was surprised by what he saw, the name of the location had thrown him off so he assumed this was a normal hotel but to his utmost surprise, it was a nightclub.

The relief on Kyle's face was evident because he knew that Jones had saved his life.

Imagine wearing a suit to a nightclub.

The time for meeting now made sense as well and Kyle got a lot of stares because Kyle looked like someone that belonged on the big screen and they weren't sure if he was a celebrity or not.

Kyle was feeling like a god but he noticed that there was a bouncer at the door checking names.

He didn't know if he should just walk past him or stand in line like everyone else.

He didn't have much of a social status after all but the person he was meeting was supposedly important hence his dilemma.

"Mister Kyle?" A voice called out behind him, startling him.

Kyle jerked his head back to see a lady standing there but if she knew his name, then it had to be her.

No, she wasn't dressed like someone who held that sort of power nor did her presence command much authority.

"Hello there, do you sneak up on everyone?" Kyle responded but the woman didn't show much of a reaction to his joke except for a polite smile.

"Mr. Nakamura will see you," The lady responded, she had on high heels but even with that, she was standing at 5 '1. She was small but Kyle could tell she was originally from America because of her little accent.

"Isn't it customary to introduce yourself?" Kyle requested but all she did was gesture for him to follow her.

She wasn't polite but she told Kyle that she didn't think much of him and this was a direct reflection of how Nakamura also felt.

She was a reflection of him but Kyle knew this would have to change, he checked the owner of Distort online but there was no Nakamura so he didn't know just how important he was.

Kyle knew he might have to kiss ass but there was no way he was doing so with someone as insignificant as an assistant.

"You know what? I think I have changed my mind," Kyle said, staying in place.

He had to make a strong first impression and he knew that this might be a test to measure him.

Jones did hint at something like this happening and realized that he might have to call their bluff.

She had a choice, to keep working or return but the moment she stopped, Kyle had his answer.

The lady returned to where he was but her forced smile was nowhere to be seen.

"Mr. Nakamura is a very busy man. Can you please follow me, Mister Kyle?" The Asian lady said but Kyle was unimpressed by her behavior.

"So am I, if you're done wasting my time. I will be on my way, I think I will take my business somewhere else," Kyle responded sternly.

"Huh? You are not a representative?" The lady was surprised by his statement. The truth was, no one knew who Kyle was.

They all assumed he was just a face, companies did that a lot to protect the identity of the one who really runs the place, just like Nakamura.

On the internet, the company didn't belong to him but that wasn't the case in reality.

"I am the owner. You called me here for a meeting and treated me with this much disrespect?" Kyle was disgusted by this.

The lady's body language changed the moment this information was relayed to her and she bowed to apologize for this transgression.

Kyle understood where the disrespect stemmed from but he found it strange that this was their character at the core.

"The rich looking down on the poor, how typical," Kyle thought to himself but this was how the world worked.

"My name is Yuki and I'm Mr. Nakamura's personal assistant. I was told to wait for you and usher you inside. My character is not a reflection of Mr. Nakamura. I'm sorry, I didn't know who you were," Yuki responded with her head still bowed.

Yuki was petite but this also included her features, Kyle didn't find her attractive even though she was cute.

"It is fine, just take me to him, Yuki," Kyle responded. Kyle was eager to make a good first impression but in the end, it was Yuki that made a bad one.

If this was how they treated someone supposedly inferior to them, he had no qualms about using them to get to where he wanted to go.

"H-He is so young…" Yuki was surprised by his age, this was the only reason she thought he was a representative. If he was older, there was no way she would have made this mistake.

He walked behind her and right past the bouncer, once again noticing the intimidating aura that his body exuded.

[[Thank you Kyle… For everything, I don't know how you found Jane but I'm glad that it was you who found her.]] Ella showed her gratitude.

Kyle smiled, he didn't know that his relationship with Jane held such significance but then again, it was because of him that this was possible.

Ella had a shot at a musical career because of him and this was something that she wasn't going to take for granted.

"Thank you, Ella, this means a lot coming from you. I have to go now, I can't have her wondering where I ran off to," Kyle informed Ella and she chuckled at the other end.

[[You're right, she is a klutz after all.]] Ella teased her best friend even though it could be interpreted differently. There was no reason for her to be jealous of her friend when she was benefitting from the relationship as well.

The phone call came to an end but the smile on Kyle's face slowly disappeared.

He knew that Mike was a nuisance but knew he had to be more careful around him.

There was no telling what he would do to Jane but he wasn't going to give him a chance to hurt her.

Kyle heaved a frustrated sigh because he knew he had to take care of Mike but he saw how strong Mike looked.

If it did escalate into a physical brawl, he would be beaten to a fucking pulp but they had to be other means a man of his status could take care of Mike.

"Mike, I think it is time for you to go," Kyle thought to himself, placing his phone in his pocket but not before checking if Cassandra had unblocked him.

He didn't know why he was checking to begin with considering it was for the best that he was blocked and it didn't matter either way because she hadn't.

Kyle found himself wanting that forbidden fruit now more than ever.
