Chapter 95 - 95: Dark Temptation.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Kyle was taken aback by the information told to him by Ella because this was something he felt he should have been told by Jane. But then again, it wasn't his business in hindsight seeing as they didn't have any defined relationship.

She presented Mike as someone harmless but that was far from the case.

Kyle wasn't going to let him have his way and he returned to his room with Jane still sleeping or at least that was the illusion she gave.

Her phone was on the table, charging but Kyle noticed that she had no passcode on it.

A lesser man would have gone through her phone to see the kind of conversation that she was having with Mike but Kyle wasn't threatened by him.

Kyle knew that Mike was hanging around like some pathetic dog hoping for a piece of the cake but Jane wasn't stupid enough not to see through this or at least he hoped.

Jane adjusted on the bed and Kyle realized that she was awake or at least, about to wake up.

He walked up to the side of her bed but this showed how deceptive Kyle was.

It looked like she was asleep but Kyle knew that she was in that semi-sleep state and could hear the words that were about to come out of his lips.

"God, you're beautiful," Kyle said to her but this was one of those things you'd see in romance movies where the man is admiring the beauty of his sleeping partner.

Kyle knew he had to play into that romantic notion and the moment he looked away, a smile creased Jane's face.

However, the doorbell soon rang and Kyle walked out to see who it was considering that he didn't have visitors.

But there was a chance that it was Calista, she knew where he lived however she should know better than to visit unannounced.

Jones wasn't around too so it couldn't be him considering he was just coming from outside.

So that narrowed it down to one more person, that was Aiysha, this was the last thing that he wanted to see right now.

Aiysha should be at work, she had no reason to stay home but he didn't know about her schedule.

Kyle checked the peephole and saw it was indeed Aiysha, he kissed his teeth in irritation because she had seen him with Calista so seeing him with Jane would shape how she viewed him.

The last he needed was for her to see him as some sort of playboy or it risked destroying the boundaries he was trying his best to set.

Kyle looked back and saw that she wouldn't even be able to see Jane should she get in so there was no need to worry.

He opened his door and noticed that Aiysha was distracted by her phone for a few seconds before even realizing that Kyle had opened the door.

She was used to the creaking door that was once there and was still adjusting to this change.

"Oh! Hi Kyle, I can't believe I didn't even know you opened the door," Aiysha greeted, putting her phone away immediately.

"It happens to the best of us," Kyle downplayed her embarrassment but he wanted to know why she was here.

"Well, can I come in or is there some lady in there with you?" Aiysha teased him but Kyle knew he couldn't encourage such behavior.

"Is there something you need? I'm quite busy now," Kyle responded; shutting down her attempt at humour.

Aiysha's smile slowly disappeared from her face as she awkwardly cleared her throat.

Kyle hated doing this to her but he would rather hurt her feelings than betray Jones.

"Oh, I'm sorry for the inconvenience but there is something I have been wanting to discuss with you, is now a good time?" Aiysha asked.

Kyle couldn't help but notice that she always wore revealing clothes to show off her toned body and he had this overwhelming urge to rip them off her.

"Now isn't the right time but I will give you a call when my schedule clears up," Kyle responded but he noticed that Aiysha was trying to look past him.

"What is her problem?" Kyle thought to himself, what he does with his life should be none of her business so why was she suddenly getting nosey?

"That will work for me," Aiysha responded with a polite smile, Kyle averting his gaze.

Those damn fucking eyes, Kyle hated that he couldn't maintain her contact with her for too long.

Kyle's eyes couldn't help but look at her ass, it was bouncing all over the place despite the leggings she had on seemingly restricting the motion.

But this was where he made a mistake because Aiysha looked back and caught him staring at her ass. Kyle noticed that his eyes had lingered for a little too long and quickly looked away before shutting the door behind him.

"Fucking hell Kyle! Get it together!" Kyle thought to himself.

He had to get his thoughts under control but he looked back and saw that Jane was all dressed up.

"Hey sleepyhead, welcome back to the land of the living," Kyle greeted her and Jane flashed an innocent smile in response.

"Hi…" Jane muttered but she was flushed red, recalling what had happened a few hours ago.

She couldn't believe that Kyle had done that to her and needed to return to her personal space to process everything that had happened.

Kyle could tell she was ready to leave but he couldn't let her yet because he didn't want Aiysha to see her.

"Are you ready to go?" Kyle asked and Jane nodded to confirm this was the case but Kyle shook his head.

"That won't do, allow me to make you dinner," Kyle requested, his conversation with Ella confirmed that food was her weakness.

There was no way she would say no and Kyle was right because Jane accepted in a heartbeat.

Chapter 96 - 96: New Or Old Feature?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The day came to a natural end with Jane leaving much later in the evening, Kyle offered to drop her off at her place but Jane declined.

There was no need for him to go to such lengths after everything he had done for her.

She called an Uber and Kyle said his goodbye with a soft kiss on the lips before sending her on her way but Kyle was relieved that she was gone for some reason.

He needed time to recharge, his mental capacity wouldn't be able to handle it as he was already overstimulated.

Kyle was glad with how his day panned out, he noticed that Calista hadn't been in contact but he wasn't losing her so there was no need to worry about this lack of communication.

He had the power to get rid of anyone in his harem and knew he couldn't waste it.

Kyle was saving it for a truly terrible situation, Calista was a bitch but she was good in bed.

He was worried about her which was weird, Kyle couldn't help but feel bad about it.

"System, what are your other functions? Can you help me out here?" Kyle knew there had to be more to his system if it had the power to influence reality.

There was something that soon appeared on his cyan screen to show that his system could help out with his predicament.

[Gathering information…]

[Loading 55%!]

[Loading 89%!]

[Loading 99%!]

[Information gathering complete!]

[Would you like to see the information regarding Cassandra?

[YES][NO]?

"Yes," Kyle responded to his system without hesitation because she had gone quiet and off the radar, all he needed to know was if she was alright or not.

[Displaying information…]

[Name: Cassandra Newman…]

[Age: 33…]

[Status: Alive…]

[Relationship Status: Unknown…]

"Relationship status, unknown? That is strange…" Kyle thought to himself, doubting his system's accuracy but he was surprised that it was able to gather this much information about her.

Kyle knew that he needed more information about how his system got this information in the first place and that was what he asked.

[The host gathers information on his partners through copulation.]] The cyan screen informed him about this important piece of information.

"So how did you gather information about Jane when she entered my harem?" Kyle questioned with a raised brow.

[The host had activated the affection bar of said person but that only grants access to basic information. However, the more times you copulate, the more information you gather.]

Kyle realized that this made sense, that was why it was able to discern her age but he wanted to know how much information it could provide.

Kyle also wanted to know why he was just finding out about this feature as well, it felt like it just spawned out of thin air.

But he knew that asking it would be a waste of time as it would only give a generic response.

"Display the information about Calista Wave…" Kyle realized that this could save him a lot of headaches if he could find a way to harness this skill.

He thought the system was all about money but apparently, it was a combination of multiple skills that made up its base ability.

[Gathering information…]

[Loading 23%!]

[Loading 78%!]

[Loading 99%!]

[Information gathering complete!]

[Would you like to see the information regarding Calista Wave?]

[YES][NO]?

Kyle pressed [YES] and prayed to God that he wouldn't regret this decision, this was the equivalent of going through your girl's phone but this was on a more personal level.

Kyle couldn't believe he was doing this but he needed to know the full potential of this system.

[Displaying information…]

[Name: Calista Wave…]

[Age: 21…]

[Blood Type: AA…]

[Occupation: Model, Actress, Singer…]

[Body Count: 5…]

[Status: Alive…]

[Affection bar: 100%...]

[Relationship Status: Taken…]

[Current Location: XXX-XXX-XXX…]

Kyle's jaw dropped, he couldn't believe he could not only see her current location but her body count as well. It was way lower than he would have expected considering the kind of lady she was.

He expected her to have over at least twenty yet it was just five? That was absurd but Kyle knew he had no reason to doubt his system even if he doubted the information displayed.

His eyes darted around her location and he realized that he could keep tabs on her at all times even though it was probably because she was in his harem.

This was one of the most convenient skills because it would allow him to protect them should the need arise. Kyle realized that the system was introducing new skills that encouraged him to push the boundaries of his relationships with these women.

The thing about this system was that it didn't force Kyle to do anything he didn't want to.

There was no penalty for refusing to participate in these activities and even with a 10X rebate system, it was enough to set him up for life.

But no one would be satisfied with that if there was a possibility to make their life even easier.

Kyle knew that with the information that Ella had revealed about Mike, it wouldn't hurt to have vital information about Jane but there was only one way that was happening.

He had to take her virginity but that might not be enough to save her from Mike should he attempt to do something to her.

Kyle did all of this while standing outside, staring blankly ahead like he was trapped in some sort of illusion done by Itachi.

"Are you okay?" The voice of a child asked, Kyle looked to his left and realized it was Clarissa.

"Of course I am, why would you say that?" Kyle questioned with an awkward smile.

"You have the same look as Papa when he's alone," Clarissa said prompting Kyle to recoil at that statement.

"Huh?" He thought to himself but the truth was that Kyle's life had turned around for the better.

That emptiness in his heart remained but the concerning thing about this was that Clarissa saw through him with a single glance.

Chapter 97 - 97: Uncle Kyle.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Kyle didn't know what Clarissa was doing alone outside or what she meant with her earlier statement but she was a kid.

Kyle knew he had to be overthinking this situation, there was no way that a kid could see through him when no one else could but that was only because his mask had slipped.

"What are you doing out here either way, shouldn't you be at home?" Kyle responded but Clarissa sat at the entrance without saying a word.

"Earth to Clarissa…" Kyle responded but he could tell that she was sad and needed someone to talk to even though she didn't specifically search for him.

Kyle sat beside her and noticed that she was staring into the distance, as she would rather be anywhere but here.

Kyle always assumed Jones was a happy man with the perfect family but the words that came out of Clarissa's mouth implied otherwise.

"Mommy and Daddy are fighting again," Clarissa confessed, burying her head between her legs.

"Again?" Kyle thought. This was the first time that he heard they fought.

He knew that every couple fought, that much was certain because a relationship without conflict isn't a healthy one.

Kyle knew he had to be careful with what he said because this was a child and he wasn't aware of what she had been exposed to.

"Did your parents fight a lot, Mister Kyle?" Clarissa asked, momentarily lifting her head to look at him.

Kyle hated talking about his family but this wasn't about him, it was about Clarissa.

"I think people fight when they care about each other…" Kyle started, staring off into the distance.

"What do you mean? Fighting is bad," Clarissa responded but Kyle expected that much.

Children are taught things in such a black-and-white manner that he couldn't blame her for her view.

"Hahahah! I understand why you might think that but tell me, Clarissa, don't you fight too?" Kyle questioned her to which Clarissa gave the question some thought before nodding her head.

"Do you hate her?" Kyle questioned and she immediately shook her head to refute this.

"That's how it is for your mommy and daddy," Kyle responded and he could tell that this seemed to lighten up her mood because she paused for a moment before smiling.

"Did you ever fight with your mommy and daddy?" Clarissa asked a personal question but Kyle took a second longer than he had to as a smile creased his face.

"Of course I do," Kyle responded. Before the conversation could go any further. Kyle was thankful for the noise he heard behind him.

He heard footsteps behind him and turned, it was Jones with an exhausted look on his face.

"C'mon Clarissa," Jones called out to her.

" Thank you, Mister Kyle," Clarissa showed her gratitude this time without having to be told.

Jones patted her head as she walked past him and Kyle could tell that he wanted to apologize for the inconvenience but Kyle waved it off.

Jones heaved a sigh before returning to his apartment.

"Did I ever fight with my parents…?" Kyle thought to himself as the smile slowly faded from his face.

Kyle couldn't remember much of his childhood which was ironic considering the skill the system had grabbed him as he was now cursed to know everything.

He didn't know if the ability was given to him because of this but why couldn't he remember most of his childhood?

Kyle was brought back to reality with a text message informing him that Jane had arrived home safely.

But for some reason, he didn't want to return to his apartment.

He just sat on the floor, taking in his environment and knew he had to make a decision. He couldn't continue living this way and talking to Clarissa told him that there was no way he could destroy this family.

He had to leave this place for his sanity, out of sight would mean out of mind.

Kyle soon returned to his apartment while the sun began to set.

He could still smell Jane in his house but would he have become this close to her without the system?

Every meaningful decision he took was influenced by his system in one way or another like he was some sort of puppet.

Kyle didn't care about any of it and knew he would find what to fill the void with when he conquered the world.

In a matter of moments, Kyle drifted off into a deep slumber.

-

"Again?" Kyle thought to himself, waking up somewhere unfamiliar.

He noticed that he was no longer in the park but a room littered with toys. The walls were painted pitch black; a flickering light bulb in the middle of the barely illuminated room.

Kyle could hear the faint sounds of screaming and shouting outside of the room; the voices were too muffled for him to make out the words.

He stretched his hand to reach for the doorknob only to realize that he was in the body of a past variant of himself.

Then carvings dipped in blood appeared on the door with the words.

"DO NOT OPEN!"

Kyle didn't know what this meant but wasn't about to listen to this sign. He knew this was a nightmare so there was no way that any harm could befall him.

He grabbed the doorknob and the sounds outside came to a halt however that was soon replaced with someone dashing towards the door.

Kyle didn't know what was happening but he felt an immense sense of urgency.

A loud bang was heard on the door like someone was tossed into it by the winds generated by a hurricane. But it didn't just happen once, it kept happening again and again until the door began to crack and give way.

Kyle backed away, his heart thumping; all he heard in response was a loud shriek that jolted him awake.

Kyle's body was trembling, his hands were shaking but the most worrying of this was his rabid heartbeat.

Kyle was having a panic attack.

Chapter 98 - 98: Another Week Later.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Ever since that day, a week had passed and renovation to the entire apartment had begun.

Kyle was glad this was underway because it was the last piece of his missing puzzle.

His nightclub was all set up; Kyle had set the launch for later that day with a whopping 50% discount for the first month.

He knew that this would upset the business revenue of his competitors but that much was to be expected.

It wasn't anything personal, it was simply business even though he knew this would most likely make him enemies.

He was ready to take that step, Kyle's wardrobe had completely changed.

He was no longer looking like a bum but people could tell with a single look that he was someone to prominence.

Ella had managed to secure a gig within his club but she had no idea he was the owner.

Kyle kept these things to himself but Isabella was proving to be invaluable.

She knew how to be discreet and this club could act as a good way for his artists to gain recognition.

Especially if he restricted the acts to artists signed directly under his Agency.

This would not only spread awareness but make it known to talents that signing under them guaranteed a fixed set of income unlike signing with a major credit industry.

Kyle still hasn't seen Calista but that was only due to her schedule however they spoke quite a lot.

He could tell that Calista was taken with him because the conversation was once tilted towards money had evolved to her simply talking about her day.

She told him everything that happened with Lucas Rossini and Kyle realized that this man might be a problem.

He might not be the most famous of Producers/Directors but there was no mistaking his talent. He was the ideal man for this work and any scene he added was for the good of the movie considering it would be on his resume.

Kyle viewed Calista differently when he saw that her body count was below 5, she wasn't ran through.

Even though she was only 21, in this day and age, those numbers were impressive, especially for a lady with her looks.

Kyle had lost all contact with Cassandra even though he got a strange call earlier that week but the person at the other end didn't say a word but he could hear the sound of a baby in the background.

Kyle didn't know what to think about this but didn't care too much. If she was having trouble with her marriage and needed an escape, he was more than willing to do so.

However, Kyle knew he needed the distraction to be more permanent.

If she wasn't happy in her marriage, he was going to take her away from her husband.

He didn't understand why his system was unsure about her relationship status but this meant that it must be complicated to some extent.

Kyle realized why he was so attached to her and the milk factor on her chest because, after a series of nightmares, Kyle was beginning to regain some of the memories he had repressed for so long.

Kyle knew everything he sought was behind that bloodied door and why his disgust for marriages existed in the first place. It was all behind that door.

He never could get past that door because he always woke up when the threat came.

This exacerbated his panic attacks but it wasn't anything he couldn't handle.

He stopped avoiding Aiysha and consciously made an effort to stop viewing her the way he had been conditioned to by his primal instinct.

However, he began to notice something that he had never paid much attention to before.

Jones and Aiysha were slowly drifting apart, the public affection that they showed each other without shame once upon a time had slowly dissipated.

They tried to put up an act for their children but there was some tension there.

Kyle knew that it wasn't in his place to come between them or try to fix whatever might be broken.

That was something they had to do themselves but he didn't want the kids to be caught in the middle.

They weren't his responsibility regardless and he only did as much as Jones and Aiysha allowed.

A lot of stuff happened in the space of a week, Jane was getting a second job because their bills were beginning to back up.

She refused for Kyle to cover everything, this included clearing their student loans that helped them through school.

Kyle didn't understand why she did this, why she chose the harsher path when she could have everything she wanted if she just asked him.

He knew that Calista would have no problem demanding anything she wanted because Kyle had shown her what he could do for her financially.

But she wanted a sense of individuality and Kyle had no problem because it eliminated the spirit of complacency. She knew the value of money so she was more prone to be appreciative for the little things.

Kyle liked this about her even though he knew he would have to take on that responsibility eventually but his view regarding his harem had changed.

If he could monopolize beautiful women for himself, there was no need for him to screw them only once when he could keep them around to rotate as he deemed fit depending on his mood.

A menu exclusive to him, the time for his club unveiling was near, Kyle had spent over $1,000,000 to sponsor the promotion so they received nothing but the best.

This was why L. A had been buzzing and news got out about this opening, one more club to be degenerated in.

Kyle wore a blazer and that was all he needed to get ready for, he looked like an Italian mobster with how he was dressed but it had an air of elegance around it.

Kyle stepped out as the sky darkened, this was where things got interesting.

Chapter 99 - 99: The Grand Opening!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

There was no way that people wouldn't want to give this place a try when their favourite artist had nothing but praise for the people here.

Kyle knew he had to make sure the security was tight too, this was one of the most important things because he had no idea what could happen on the scene.

The last thing he needed was for his club to become a murder scene on the first day because people tend to get violent with a few drinks in them.

Kyle got into the club but the line that had formed outside was jaw-dropping.

He already expected a huge crowd but this exceeded all expectations.

He met Nate inside along with some other people who were temporarily, just for tonight.

Kyle had every intention to retain anyone that did a good job, this acted as a job interview but he gave Nate the liberty to choose who they retained.

Nate was the manager and Kyle made sure he had a certain level of control to make key decisions. This was no doubt a risk as he would have been safer bringing in an experienced manager to handle things rather than gambling on Nate.

Nate, however, was trustworthy and this was one of those things you could learn from the judge.

Kyle was happy, he couldn't contain his smile because he didn't know this thing would be possible.

This dream, this right here was his imagination manifested into reality.

"Money is truly the wheel that makes the world go round," Kyle muttered under his breath but the loud music blasting around them was enough to drown out any hushed voices.

"ARE YOU PEOPLE READY!?" Kyle screamed at the top of his lungs, his voice managing to drown out the music blasting as it coincidentally hit a low decibel at the moment.

The workers were pumped because they knew this might be a chance for them to get a decent job that didn't pay horseshit.

They all matched Kyle's energy and this was the most emotion Nate had seen from Kyle.

He was a happy man right now and nothing was going to stop him.

"Yeah? Is something wrong?" Kyle questioned, sensing his concern.

The two hefty men stationed at the entrance ready to spring into action should the situation call for it.

"Is her name Jane by any chance?" Kyle questioned with a knowing smile.

There was no way that Jane wouldn't come here to support her best friend on one of the biggest nights of her musical career.

"How did you know? You were expecting her?" Nate questioned but Kyle didn't give a verbal response.

He checked his phone and just as he expected, Jane had told him that she was coming to this club. Kyle named it "The Devil's Treat"

A catchy name to say the least but he was aware of what it could be interpreted as considering the religious nature of the world.

There was a message attached that Ella had only told her last minute and that was why she was just informing him.

Kyle had no reason to doubt her because this would be pointless if it was one considering Kyle had no way to know her whereabouts.

He understood the importance of open communication and Jane remained the greenest flag he had seen so far.

Ella only informed her of the gig and how late she would be but Jane quickly threw on something when she found out how big of a deal this was.

"Let her in and make sure she gets the V.I.P. section," Kyle instructed Nate.

Nate was surprised but it was clear that he knew her and boy, was she a cutie.

"Fine! I will do it," Nate responded but before he left, he was given a stern warning.

"Oh and Nate… She's mine," That was all Kyle said, this should be enough to dissuade him from making any moves on her but Nate wasn't even that kind of person to begin with.

"As you wish, boss," Nate responded with a thumbs up.

Kyle knew he had to make an appearance and this would be the night that his dream became a reality.

The club was officially opened and the crowd poured in, Kyle made sure the area Jane was in was restricted solely to her.

No one else could access the area regardless of their status, they would no doubt lose revenue in the hundreds of thousands but that was inconsequential.

This was going to be a long night and Kyle planned to enjoy every single moment.

The restaurant was in the next room and with the soundproof walls, it could effectively separate these two lifestyles.

"Let the party begin."

Chapter 100 - 100: Like Moth To Flames.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Live performances, bring the artists closer to the crowd. However, he knew that a DJ might be needed should something happen to the artists meant to perform so he had one on standby.

Kyle watched the bright smile on her face, Ella couldn't make out the crowd thanks to the flickering lights but her immediate vicinity didn't suffer this same fate, allowing her to focus on her performance.

Ella sang her heart out and the audience was dancing, she didn't opt for a heavy metal track and chose to sing something a lot softer.

This was a different experience and with everything 50% off, bottles were emptied out at an astonishing rate. Everything was going and the stock was depleted, even the meals in the restaurant section were nearing depletion.

This was such a busy scene but Kyle was going to make his move, Jane was surprised that she had this place all to herself.

Ella was performing here but she wasn't that important to warrant such treatment with a security.

Stationed at her booth. A drink was offered but Jane didn't touch it, she took out her phone and recorded Ella singing her heart out.

Tears soon welled up in her eyes, she couldn't believe that Ella had reached this stage in her career but the emotions in her voice were.

She could tell her friend was hurting and letting it all out, it could be felt with every word that escaped her lips.

Jane knew this was more than a song for Ella, this was her soul being shared with everyone but of course, she was the only one that caught on to this.

Everyone else was too focused on drinking and partying to care about such a thing.

If Mike was willing to go this far to get him away from Jane then he was right. Jane was in danger but the threat was him. There was no way in hell Kyle was about to leave Jane with this monster even if it meant him becoming a vegetable, he was going to take Mike down with him.

"Hurry up! " Kyle thought to himself right before the men charged at him and Mike acted as an observer with a grotesque grin on his face.

-

Nate was working when he suddenly received a notification from Kyle out of the blue.

Kyle never sent him a message and always called him when they had something to talk about so this was out of character for him.

He opened the message and saw that Kyle had simply shared his location.

He was out of the store and in what people would deem the bad part of Los Angeles.

Nate had to look at his phone closely to see if his eyes were deceiving him because Kyle was here only minutes ago.

"This is strange," Nate thought to himself because he knew that the only reason Kyle would share his location would be because he was either in danger or wanted him to come and find him.

Nate ran to the compartment that Kyle had entered.

"Where is Kyle!?" Nate asked, the urgency in his voice palpable.

"He left with a man not long ago," Antoine's daughter responded, oblivious to the situation at hand.

Nate realized that Kyle might be in danger and there was no way he was going to let something happen to him because Kyle wasn't only a man he had grown to respect but his direct source of income.

Nate hurried and dialed 911.

Unlike Kyle, Nate had a little fighting experience and could hold his own should a fight break out.

Nate gave the dispatcher all the information they needed while also sharing the live location that Kyle had shared with him.

Nate knew that Kyle would have called him if he could talk so the fact that he didn't even bother trying to reach him might be because he couldn't.

The last thing he would want was to call Kyle and they ask him to get rid of the phone.

Nate hurried to the location himself, shut the shop behind him, hopped on his bicycle and cycled in that direction.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 113 - 113: Bang!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Kyle knew he had to buy time but the men approaching him had a single mission and that was to turn him into a brain-dead vegetable.

But then he remembered he had money, that was the ultimate power in the end.

"Are you sure you want to attack me when I can change your lives?" Kyle offered, his composure gave them the impression that he wasn't scared.

This helped in his stance as he noticed that they reacted to this, momentarily stopping in their tracks with a raised brow.

"What the fuck does this little bitch mean?" One of the men asked.

"$10,000,000? $20,000,000? Is it $50,000,000? Each?" Kyle questioned because he found a language that they finally responded to.

"There is no way he has that kind of money. Are you people buying it?" Mike dismissed it because he truly believed that Kyle was bluffing.

There was no way this man had that much sitting in his bank account, it was ridiculous.

The men continued to walk towards him, Kyle simply laughed because the men continued walking towards him with the bat in hand.

"Let's make this fun, how about I put a bounty on Mikey-boy there and the first person to bash his head in gets $100,000,000? And if I don't pay up, you could just kill me," Kyle shrugged.

The men became hesitant and Mike could no longer tell if Kyle was bluffing or not.

"Prove it to us you shit fuck!" The second man of the three barked but they all had one thing in common and that was their foul mouth.

The last thing Kyle expected from them was etiquette and knew they would need proof.

He took out his phone and tossed it towards them, they caught it and the men all gathered to see what he was trying to show them.

"Holy fucking hell!? Is that real!?" The third man out of the three questioned and Mike was beginning to sense that he might be in danger.

"Wait! Did you mean that shit about giving $100,000,000 to who bashes that fucker's head in?" The first man asked to which Kye simply nodded.

Kyle had sent his location to Nate the moment he sensed something was off but only thought to buy his way out of it as a last resort.

He wanted to buy time but he didn't know they would go for it.

"I'm sorry Mike, I know you will understand," They said as they handed Kyle back his phone while walking past him, headed right towards Mike.

"I knew something like this might happen, you cunts are fucking useless! I can't rely on anybody! I hope the money was worth your lives," Mike muttered.

The men charged towards him but three loud bangs rang out and they all dropped to the ground.

"W-Wai-!" One of the men tried to plead but Mike put a bullet right through his skull without flinching.

"What the fuck!? Did he just fucking kill them?" Kyle thought to himself.

He wasn't aware that Mike was a fucking killer or he wouldn't have attempted this because he now had the nozzle of a gun pointed right at him.

"I didn't want to kill you, Kyle. I know it may be hard to believe but I liked you," Mike blurted out.

"So what, are you going to kill me?" Kyle asked.

"Even in the face of death! You look so cool! At least beg or something for fuck's sake!" Mike barked with a look of disbelief.

"You want me to beg? Is that it? You want me to beg you to spare my life?" Kyle scoffed.

In reality, it was taking everything in him not to piss himself but once again, he couldn't give him that satisfaction.

He was beginning to realize that Mike felt small and he needed to feel superior to him before executing him.

Kyle knew he needed to stall for as long as possible.

"Get on your fucking knees!" Mike barked at him, Kyle noticed that the men he had killed.

He shot two with perfect accuracy, hitting them right in the head.

These were moving targets so he had experience with a gun and there was no way he could take him off guard either.

Kyle knew he would be shot in the head before he could take a step and there was no universe where he was faster than a bullet.

Kyle got on his knees, he couldn't agitate him even more or he might lose his cool and just put a bullet in his head.

"Now beg me to spare your miserable life," Mike screamed, waving the gun at him.

"Do you know what Jane said about you?" Kyle began and he knew there was no way he wouldn't want to know. That was the power of a curious mind.

"What the fuck are you talking about?" Mike asked.

"You know exactly what I'm talking about. She told me how she feels about you," Kyle said with a satisfied smile.

"I hope you don't think stalling for time will save you-!" Kyle knew he had to tell him something that would keep his mouth shut and he knew just what to say..

"I know about the girl you raped… Oh, Mike, the little rapist," Kyle teased him but with this information, there was no way for him to know if someone didn't tell him and he assumed that someone was Jane.

"Who the fuck told you that!?" Mike was losing his cool but this was what Kyle was hoping for, he needed an opening.

"Who else? Jane and guess what? She believed the accusations," Kyle said with a satisfied smile.

"You see? Killing me or not makes no difference, you will never get Jane!" Kyle mocked him.

"SHUT UP!" Mike screamed at the top of his lungs but an opening presented itself at this moment. He reached for the gun but a loud bang echoed in the distance with blood spilling onto the ground but whose?

Chapter 114 - 114: A Fatal Wound!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The gunshots echoed, and Nate, who was close to the area, heard them. This was when he understood the urgency of the situation.

Nate stopped the moment he did because there was a chance that these were the police.

The last thing he would want was to be caught in the crossfire but he hadn't heard any sirens.

It would make sense that the police arrived first considering how dangerous this area was

There was no way the police car wouldn't be nearby, and if they got the amber alert, then Nate had done all he could, but he had to trust the cops to do their job now.

He checked Kyle's location and noticed he was no longer moving.

He was stationary but Nate knew that Kyle should be able to survive long enough for the police to get to him.

He had to get off the road and this was why he opted to take shortcuts, he wasn't a stranger to this terrain either.

He had been here when he was younger even though the area wasn't in this state back then as it had become a den for criminals.

He saw multiple homeless people strung out on drugs and alcohol littered everywhere on the road.

This allowed him to move faster and undetected, he hoped Kyle was okay because he knew he wouldn't be able to forgive himself if he wasn't.

It took a little over a minute for Nate to close in on the target and in no time, he arrived at the location but there was nothing in sight.

Nate's body recoiled the moment he saw three dead bodies, there was no sign of a struggle because he didn't know that the police would attempt to go for the kill shot against people holding bats.

But he wasn't surprised, this was a common theme in America, and the value of human life was equivalent to a trending meme.

Nate wanted to run away but this was where Kyle's location ended so there was no doubt he was here but where?

Nate couldn't establish any visuals on him but managed to find his phone on the floor but what was more worrying was the trail of blood along the floor.

There was no sign of the police, if the police were the ones responsible, there was no way in hell they wouldn't secure the area.

That could only mean one thing, this wasn't done by the police.

Nate gulped; realizing that he might have bitten off more than he could chew but why was it taking the police so long to get here?

Nate decided to follow the blood trail because he knew time was the enemy.

"Kyle, what the hell have you gotten yourself into!?" Nate questioned, picking up one of the metal bars on the floor just in case he would need it, parking his bicycle some distance away.

Nate followed the blood trail into a rundown house, he didn't like the way things were looking but who did the blood belong to? But who would find them first? Nate or the police?

A few minutes prior to Nate arriving at the scene, a gunshot echoed the moment Kyle reached for the gun but Mike had missed.

He involuntarily pulled the trigger upon being startled by Kyle, this allowed Kyle to tackle him to the ground, inadvertently knocking the gun out of his hand.

They struggled on the ground for dominance, Kyle managed to land a few punches to his face but Mike was much stronger than him so there was only so much he could do in this situation.

It was only a matter of time before Mike regained control of the situation, knocking Kyle off him.

Kyle rolled on the floor but was closer to the gun than Mike.

Kyle dashed towards the gun, he didn't know if he was capable of killing a man but was about to find out.

But Kyle underestimated Mike, Mike displayed speed that shouldn't be possible for his body size.

He sprung to his feet and dashed towards the gun, Kyle got to it first and pointed it in Mike's direction but Mike slammed his foot right into Kyle's face, fracturing his jaw and sending him reeling.

This force nearly knocked him out but Kyle held on to his sanity as best as he could.

He knew that Mike was the real deal and needed to get out of here

He was no match for this monster and that was when he chose flight because he couldn't even reach his phone at this point.

There was a sharp pain in his rib and upon closer observations, he saw that Mike had a bloodied knife in his right hand.

Kyle clutched his abdomen and blood was spilling out of it but he still had the adrenaline coursing through his veins. He needed to get out before it subsided.

At that moment, he knew he had been stabbed but the adrenaline running through his body didn't let him feel the pain and Kyle took off.

He ran in a zig-zag formation and this made it harder for Mike to hit his mark, he fired a bullet or two before running out but none of those bullets hit their mark as it narrowly missed Kyle.

Before long, Kyle was out of sight, taking a sharp turn but Mike wasn't about to let him get away.

He had seen too much and if he reported to the authorities, there was no doubt that he had the influence to force these cops into action.

"You can't run forever, you fucker! You once told me you would put a bullet between my eyes. Isn't it funny Kyle? That I'm the one about to do that," Mike shouted.

He knew Kyle couldn't go far and the trail of blood on the ground gave away his location no matter how fast he ran.

He would eventually succumb to his wound and the game of cat and mouse began.

Chapter 115 - 115: Dead Or Alive? Where Is Kyle!?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Jane was getting worried, it had been over thirty minutes without hearing from him.

Kyle wasn't one to go silent for this long so there had to be a reason for this.

She tried to call him but for some reason, it didn't go through and she couldn't understand why.

It went straight to voicemail but this shouldn't be the case because the phone was being tracked or was it?

The answer was no, Kyle's phone had gone off shortly after Nate had left the area.

This wasn't because of a low battery either so there was no way for Nate to have anticipated it.

Jane was worried and had no other way to contact Kyle, she didn't know any of his friends that she could reach to check in on him.

And there was only one person she could call to support her in this situation, that was Ella.

"Pick up! Pick up!" Jane muttered to herself, pacing around the house.

[[Hello Jane, are you alright?]]

"No! I'm freaking out! I think something bad happened to Kyle," Jane fired immediately.

She didn't hold back because she was aware that this was out of character for him and she couldn't explain it either but her gut feeling told her that he was in trouble.

[[What do you mean he is in trouble? What's going on Jane!?]] Ella was concerned.

She grew to like Kyle and there was no reason why she would be unaffected by any misfortune that befell him.

"He was supposed to meet me but I haven't heard anything from him and his phone isn't going through. I can't shake it but I feel something is wrong," Jane responded.

[[Or his phone could be dead? You do this a lot Jane…]] Ella called her out.

This was one of Jane's negative traits when she liked someone, she became affected by every single thing that they did.

She couldn't help her overthinking and Ella had to stop her friend's mind from spiraling out of control.

"You're right, he is probably fine…" Jane responded, taking a deep breath to calm her mind.

[[Now what do you plan to do with Kyle today?]] Ella diverted the conversation from her friend's fear while still keeping Kyle as the topic of the conversation.

"I don't know… I haven't thought about it…" Jane confessed.

And now that she was thinking about it, it gave her anxiety because the last thing she would want Kyle to think was she was boring.

She had to entertain him like he managed to entertain her, what would she even cook?

Would she be able to cook as good as him?

She couldn't use the same method he used and Ella could tell by her silence that she was overthinking it.

[[No need to overthink it, the best moments are those that happen spontaneously.]] Ella reassured her.

"You're right. Thank you so much, Ella," Jane showed her gratitude before hanging up the phone.

She still couldn't shake the feeling that something was wrong and little did she know, she was right.

-

Nate had done the smart thing, he took Kyle's phone with him so the police would track him directly through the GPS.

But the phone died and by doing this, Nate had inadvertently thrown off the police tracking him because they had to use a different path to intercept him.

The blood trail would have been found if the phone had been left there, the police couldn't reach there with their vehicles and would have had to use their foot but they would have been able to track the blood trail.

But by Nate taking the phone, the police no longer had any reason to come to that specific location and simply took a different route to intercept this signal.

And that would throw them off the blood trail, the place the signal was lost was during the time that Nate had taken a wrong turn.

This meant that there was no police coming, at least not for a while.

-

Nate found the end of the trail, it was within a building that looked abandoned, he was frightened and his heart was thumping as well.

Nate knew the police were coming but didn't know how long it would take for them to come here.

This worried him but even in the movies, the police never came on time.

They only came when the chaos was all over but Nate was hoping that this wouldn't be the case.

He was confident in his fighting ability but if there was a gun involved then the chances that he would be successful was close to zero unless he could somehow catch the assailant with a sneak attack.

That was his only hope of coming out of this situation alive, he pushed the door open and the place was dark.

Nate could barely see the trail, the blood was being absorbed by the dusty ground.

All he was met with was darkness and silence.

"... Kyle?" Nate called out in a whisper, taking out his phone and triggering the flashlight but he didn't need to search for long because he heard a painful grunt coming from behind a door that was barely standing.

"Kyle?" Nate called out once again, clutching his bat with one hand because he didn't know who or what was behind that door.

He called Kyle but he wasn't getting any response, he tried to open the door but realized it was locked from within.

"Huh…? Nate muttered the moment his feet came into contact with something damp.

At that moment, he knew whoever was behind that door would give him the answer.

Nate tried to force it open but to no avail. Luckily, he had a metal baseball bat and the old wooden door must have taken a beat just moments ago because it looked like it was on the verge of breaking.

Nate began to pound away at the door with the bat until the door gave way and what he saw next was horrid.

It was Kyle curled up in a puddle of his blood.

Chapter 116 - 116: He Is... Dead?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Kyle had been stabbed but the good news was that he was still breathing.

He managed to get away from Mike by locking himself behind this door and with Mike running out of bullets.

He had to break through the door to get to Kyle but he couldn't get through.

He saw that Kyle was bleeding out either way so there was a near 2% chance he would survive but this was only because he didn't know that Kyle had taken precautionary measures already.

Kyle's quick thinking might have potentially saved his life but he wasn't out of the woods yet.

Nate was terrified, he quickly took out his phone and dialed 911 once again to ask for an ambulance.

He didn't care about the police anymore but the police nearby received this alert.

They knew it had to be related to the case they had been sent to handle.

This allowed them to reach the location a lot sooner.

Nate noted that Kyle had lost a lot of blood, it was a miracle he was still conscious.

Kyle's body was getting cold and something told him that if he allowed him to sleep, he wasn't going to wake up.

"Kyle! Stay with me!" Nate barked at him, but he had to be careful.

He didn't know why but the thought of someone attempting to take his life was frightening.

Nate couldn't believe that someone would go through this length, he didn't know Kyle all that but murder was crossing the line.

If Kyle hadn't locked himself here, he would have been slaughtered, the only thing that saved him was the door.

Nate didn't know if he was to move Kyle from his location or leave him.

He didn't know the extent of his injury but he saw where Kyle's limb hand was.

He quickly raised his shirt and noticed it was a stab wound.

Nate immediately removed his shirt and pressed it against the wound to apply as much pressure as he could.

Kyle didn't even react to the pain anymore because his body was numb.

But the sirens outside told Nate that it was only a matter of time.

"IN HERE!!!" Nate screamed at the top of his lungs and they rushed in.

The police officers on the scene immediately spread out to secure the area.

They had to make sure that the threat wasn't around.

The police officers heard faint gunshots but they were too far away to pinpoint it.

And with this area being a haven for crime, this wasn't a strange occurrence.

Police officers tended to stay out of their place unless they were specifically called.

An officer pulled Nate away from Kyle, taking over the first aid.

It was only a matter of time before his body went into shock, he had lost too much blood.

"Stay with me sir," The officer tried his best to reassure him in the calmest voice he could muster.

Another set of sirens echoed outside, these were the ambulances and they had arrived faster than the police did.

It showed that the dispatcher had already sent an ambulance their way during Nate's first call.

The second call simply helped them confirm his current location, this was the ultimate reason why they had arrived so fast.

They quickly placed an oxygen mask around his face before putting him on a stretcher and carting him away.

Nate tried to follow him but the police officers needed more information at that moment.

They also needed to allow the paramedics to do their jobs and they wouldn't be able to do that with Nate in the ambulance being a nuisance.

But the big question was, would Kyle make it?

That question was answered moments later as his heart flatlined.

"We have lost him! Begin chest compressions!" One of the paramedics commanded and the desperate attempt to save his life began.

If Kyle died, there would be no way to tie Mike to the murders unless Antoine had taken a good look at him.

He was the only loose end.

-

Calista's stand showed her that she did have a say in the way the movie plays out as long as it deviated from the original plotline.

This included the kissing scene, which was returned to the script they had presented.

She noticed that Lorenzo looked relieved as well which was surprising, she expected him to jump at the chance to have that kind of access to her.

But this wasn't a problem, she didn't see it as a bad thing– quite the opposite.

"I'm so relieved you spoke up, I could never do that," Lorenzo confessed the moment that Calista was having this thought, it was almost like he read her mind.

The other cast looked at her with a sign of respect as well because it wasn't only her script that had been perverted.

This single brave act would ultimately shape how she was perceived in this studio.

"I didn't do much, I just said what we were all thinking," Calista tried her best to be humble.

"We thought it and you said it," Lorenzo responded.

"I don't think my boyfriend would appreciate me locking lips with a woman as beautiful as you on my first major movie," Lorenzo added and there it was.

" You're gay? I would never have guessed!" Calista exclaimed, shocked at this sudden revelation but it made sense.

"You expected me to be some sassy drama queen?" Lorenzo questioned, he spoke like a normal person but this allowed Calista to heave a huge sigh of relief.

If he was into men then there was nothing to worry about but she also knew that bisexual people existed so she couldn't be too relaxed.

Hollywood depicted a certain way over the years and that's effeminate.

The man before he was anything but that, he had a masculine air around him but did these people cast him knowing that? But once again, like he could read her thoughts, he responded.

He placed his finger over his lips before whispering.

"It is a secret,"

Chapter 117 - 117: Grim Reaper... Teased.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Nate closed down the section to the building he was responsible for, he didn't know what else to do because business couldn't come to a standstill.

He didn't want anyone to know what had happened until he had to reveal this information.

Nate tried to together if someone suspicious had entered the club last night or if Kyle had any altercation that would make someone want to hurt him.

How did the person manage to get Kyle to follow them?

It had to be someone he knew or Kyle might have raised an alarm.

No. What if the person threatened to shoot up the place and Kyle had no choice but to follow them?

He was a rich man that just opened a club walking around without security.

However, no one could tell he was wealthy simply by looking at him but this was the only thing Nate could do.

Kyle was in the ICU, he wondered if Kyle had any family to call or otherwise.

The hospital would have contacted his next of kin but it had been a few hours and no one had come.

Nate would imagine that they would drop everything and rush towards the hospital the moment they heard their loved one was in danger.

But that wasn't the case, Kyle was fighting for his life and he had no one but Nate.

This robbed Nate the wrong way but there was nothing he could do about it but be present for him.

The fact that he was in the ICU right away, was a reflection of how urgent this situation was.

But a doctor soon came out and he had a grim expression.

Nate hoped that he was simply exhausted rather than having some bad news.

"Are you the one responsible for the patient who came in earlier?" the doctor asked, to which Nate nodded.

He heaved a loud sigh. This was the same doctor who had taken care of Ella on that fateful day.

So in a sense, he knew Kyle.

"I'm happy to say that the patient is stable but has lost too much blood," The man reported.

Nate didn't know if he should be relieved or not, losing too much blood couldn't be a good thing.

"Do you need my blood!?" Nate questioned, the desperation in his voice telling the doctor he would do anything.

"There is no need to worry about that. Our blood bank is taking care of that," The doctor pointed out and he doubted that Nate could donate.

Kyle had one of the rarest blood types, AB- and they knew that if they transfused the limited blood in their bank, they could potentially have a new donor.

But before the conversation could develop any further, Jane and Ella came busting through the day.

Nate recognized both ladies from last night.

"Where is he!? How's he!?" Jane questioned, ignoring everyone around her as she went straight to the familiar doctor.

The self-conscious Jane no longer cares about how she is perceived at that moment.

The doctor had called them because he didn't have anyone to call regarding Kyle when he was initially brought in, Nate only came hours after the call.

"He is fine. Unconscious but fine, the surgery was successful," The doctor reassured her.

"H-How did this happen?" Ella questioned.

They were both out of breath suggesting that they had run here.

"I'm afraid I cannot go into details but it appears the patient was stabbed but it just missed his vital organ, an inch deeper and we would have had a different outcome," The doctor confessed.

However, he reassured them everything would be alright before leaving the vicinity.

"You… You were at the club last night," Jane pointed out.

Unlike Ella, she wasn't aware that Kyle was the owner of the place.

"That is right. I also remember you too…" Nate said, pausing as he stole a glance at Ella.

She was even more beautiful than he remembered but now wasn't the time for such thoughts.

"Kyle and I are… friends," Nate introduced himself but the pause before the last sentence made it sound like he was uncertain about their relationship.

He wondered why Kyle chose him of all people. He trusted him with his life and that wasn't something Nate was ever going to take for granted.

Nate explained the situation to them in great detail before he could complete the story.

Jane hugged upon realizing that this man right here had saved his life.

"Thank you so much!" Jane sobbed. The thought was scary because she could have lost Kyle.

Ella realized, at that moment, that Jane's worry earlier that day must have been about this.

She felt guilty that she dismissed it because what if Kyle had died?

Ella knew she would never forgive herself if that happened but who would want to hurt him?

That was the big question.

-

The assailant, Mike, returned home to clean himself. He didn't know why Jane made him do this.

If only she agreed to go out with him then none of this would have happened but he had removed the problem.

Mike truly believed that he had done the right thing and there was a slim chance that he was already dead.

Mike was certain that he struck a vital organ but Kyle's body jerked back at the last second, this was unknown to Mike or he would have finished the job.

He stabbed and twisted it within to open up the wound, if that knife made contact with a vital organ, there was no way Kyle would survive even with the paramedics in play as it would have been too late.

Mike hummed and danced in front of a mirror with a broad smile on his face

He tossed his clothes inside the washing machine but Kyle's suspicions about Mike were spot on.

Mike was a dangerous man that needed to be stopped but he was even better at covering his tracks as he muttered under his breath.

"One more…"
