Chapter 118 - 118: Ma... Ma? [Trigger Warning!]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Kyle was unconscious but that didn't mean his mind was dead so where was it?

Unlike the nightmares he has been having, Kyle couldn't voluntarily wake up from this.

But unlike his previous nightmares, he was self-aware that he was in a dream.

Kyle had no idea this time, to him, what he was experiencing was as real as it gets.

He was a boy, no more than seven, seated on a deserted table.

The pieces of furniture were worn out, there was no way that this apartment was healthy enough to live in.

Yet a child was on the table with a bowl of cereal right in front of him and the sound of a radio blasting off in the distance.

"You stupid bitch! I told you to get rid of it but you don't fucking listen now look at the mess we are in!" A man barked behind the door.

"Don't speak that way about him! He is your son!" A woman screamed back.

"My son? I'm not even sure that thing is human! Fuck it! I'm done!" The man screamed.

Kyle just sat there, eating his cereal, unmoved by everything he had heard.

This was a frequent occurrence in this household and the fact that he was being blamed for their unfortunate predicament showed that they weren't ready for a child.

Kyle was the repercussion of unprotected sex by two young people who hadn't even figured life out.

One wanted to keep it, the other wanted to get rid of it but unfortunately for the man, there was nothing that he could do about it.

"My choice, my body" This was the beginning of the hatred he had for Kyle because his family was in support of his then-girlfriend so he had no choice but to be a father to Kyle.

The door opened and a woman came into view but her features couldn't be made up.

She was a black silhouette, he couldn't make out any of her features.

The woman was sobbing and once again, Kyle just sat there eating his cereal.

He was unaffected by everything going on around him or would it be more accurate to say that he was dissociating?

"Maybe he is right… Maybe I should have gotten rid of you," The lady said and before Kyle knew it, the cereal in front of him that was swimming in milk soon turned red.

"Huh?" Kyle thought to himself and before he could compose his words, hands shot out from the bowl and dragged him in.

Kyle found himself drowning, he couldn't see or breathe, and he struggled but to no avail.

There was no way that his smaller body could put up much of a fight because of his smaller body.

Kyle managed to come up for air and found himself in a bathtub with a woman's hands around his neck.

"Mama?" These words inadvertently left his lips before being plunged into the water once again.

This was the woman who had defended his existence moments ago so why was she the one trying to kill him?

He could hear her muffled words beneath the water but could not make out what it was she was saying.

"I should have listened to your father! You don't deserve to live!" The female silhouette screamed.

Kyle recognized it, it was the same one he saw in the dark, there was something familiar about it and that was because she was his mother.

Kyle gave up the moment he saw it was his mother, he stopped fighting.

She was right, he hadn't done anything but bring her pain and his body covered in bruises showed that he acted as a tool for her abuse.

It wasn't always this way, his mother once loved him. In a twisted sense, Kyle believed she still did because she still had use for him even if it was to act as an outlet for her abuse.

But it became increasingly worse when his father abandoned them.

She hated Kyle because she began to see everything the man said, she sacrificed her education to raise him.

But he was soon brought up for air with the woman sobbing and apologizing.

"I'm so sorry! Mommy is sorry! I'm sorry!" She pleaded, hugging her soaked son for forgiveness.

However, in response to this, all Kyle did was smile because it confirmed that his mother still had use for him– she still loved him.

-

Jane and Ella stayed there in the hospital with Nate returning to work, he used this opportunity to give Ella his number. Informing her to contact him should the situation change here.

"How're you feeling?" Ella asked Jane but Jane was lost in thought.

"Jane?" Ella called out her name, gently placing her hand on hers to slowly ease her back to reality.

"I-I'm sorry, I was just thinking about…" Jane was visibly worried, she couldn't believe that someone tried to kill Kyle. But the thought of losing him made her heart ache beyond comprehension.

"It is fine… Everything will be fine," Ella assured her but there was no way for her to know this.

She knew Kyle was a rich man so there was no way that he wouldn't make any enemies but how bad must it be to try and take his life in broad daylight?

Jane began to sob, Ella couldn't blame her, she wasn't as close to Kyle as Jane and even she was greatly affected by this.

Ella held Jane in her arms, stroking her hair gently to comfort her as best as she could.

She knew silence was the best course of action right now, all that they needed to do was be there for Kyle when he woke up.

Ella told herself that she would never question Jane's intuition again.

They were glad that the ordeal was over or was it just beginning? That was to be seen because they saw the police walking into the hospital.

"Hm…" Ella thought to herself, she didn't allow Jane to see them because this would only make her overthink but there was no way that they were coming to take Kyle away.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 119 - 119: A New Target? [Trigger Warning!]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Ella and Jane spent the entire day in the hospital, Ella had seen the police and eventually, Jane saw them as well but only after she had calmed down.

Kyle was still unconscious so there wasn't anything the police could do but this attack looked like he was targeted.

It wasn't just a random robbery but an assassination attempt, the fact that Kyle would have ended in a worse state if he hadn't fought back.

He couldn't imagine living the rest of his life as a vegetable and instead, chose to fight.

If he was going to have his life ruined, he wanted to do so by fighting.

Kyle made a decision that saved his life because there was no way he would have seen this coming. But he was alive so that was enough for him.

He thought that Mike was simply a man taken by love but didn't know he was willing to go this far just because his ego was hurt.

Kyle was trapped in a nightmare, reliving the memories he had forced himself to forget, a never-ending loop of abuse and torment.

Kyle's father never laid a hand on him, all the physical abuse was from his mother because the older he got, the more he looked like his dad.

His mother saw him as a constant reminder of the man she had grown to hate and to cope with their hatred, she used Kyle as the placeholder of the abuse she wished she wished she could inflict on him.

It was a never-ending cycle of abuse and this ultimately warped Kyle's perception of what love should be like.

But in the end, his mother couldn't take it anymore and decided to imprint an image that triggered him to repress every memory leading up to that point.

Kyle came home from one of his daily hustles, he assisted his mother with the bills as best as he could but he was just 7, and what he could do was severely limited.

He earned way below the minimum wage but as a kid, he shouldn't even be working at all.

"Mama! I'm back!" Kyle exclaimed, excited by his haul today but he got no response from her.

Kyle assumed she was out and decided to pour himself some cereal and milk but they had run out of milk.

Kyle didn't know what else to do, so he took the plastic jug of milk and walked over to the sink, pouring some water inside and shaking it.

He returned to his chair and poured it into his cereal– it was more water than milk but it had to do.

Kyle ate and slept for a few hours but when he woke up, his mother was nowhere to be seen.

Kyle wasn't in a hurry to find her because she disappeared for days at a time forcing him to take care of himself.

This was no way for a 7-year-old to live but after a week, there was a disgusting smell coming from his mother's room.

Kyle never went there, she forbade him from entering but the smell was so bad that he had no choice.

Kyle broke his mother's rule and entered her room only to see her lifeless body hanging from the ceiling.

She was already decomposing and at that moment, Kyle's memory fractured.

This was the moment that Kyle forced himself to forget every traumatic event he had gone through reshaping his childhood with false memories.

-

Night had fallen and Antonio was leaving the area with his daughter.

Their shift was over and another set of people would take over for the rest of the night.

People personally approved of him because it was his kitchen so they followed his rules.

He was glad that Kyle came into his life because there was no telling what kind of future awaited him if Kyle didn't show up when he did.

Antonio needed to get some sleep and knew that his daughter would be a nuisance without him around.

Antonio didn't have a car, he couldn't afford one but didn't need to because he lived just around the corner.

He thought about these moments as an opportunity to bond with his daughter who would soon be off to college, this was why he preferred walking home rather than hailing a cab even if it took a little longer.

However, this might be a different night because someone was lurking in the darkness.

Antonio would come to regret this decision because the same man who put Kyle in the hospital was ultimately after his life.

This was clearly not Mike's first time taking a life, he was too calm and composed.

He must have done this numerous times but if he was about this life, what was he doing working in an Apple store?

The man and his daughter took multiple turns and they ultimately ran into Mike standing right in front of them.

Mike barged into him and the man quickly apologized, looking Mike right in the eye but the way he looked at him, Antonio had no idea how this was.

Mike could tell by the way he reacted and he had a choice to make.

Mike let him walk past him because any other thing he did, he risked leaving an impression but who remembers the random stranger you bumped into in the road? No one but if that stranger attempted to interact with you, he leaves an impression that allows you to recall that event with greater detail.

Mike realized that going on a killing spree would no doubt attract attention and if this man didn't know who he was, there was no need to harm.

Mike heaved a sigh of relief with a broad smile on his face.

"Get it together Mike, we have gotten rid of that fucker," Mike thought to himself but only if he knew that he failed in his assassination attempt on Kyle.

He would have been far more cautious with how he walked around on the street.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 120 - 120: 3 More...
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Mike felt euphoric, it had been years since he did something like this but the satisfaction he felt when he dug his knife into him made the hair on his body stand.

He would have liked to put a bullet through his head but he couldn't do that unfortunately.

Kyle made sure he ran out of bullets and he didn't have another cartridge to reload his gun with.

Heck, he didn't even know he would need it in the first place but Kyle found a way to wriggle out of the situation but he was glad that he was dead now.

"Jane, I wonder how you are…" Mike said, staring at her name on his phone but he decided to give her a call nonetheless.

Her phone rang a couple of times but there was no answer until the automated voice took him to voice note but Mike wasn't interested in this.

He tried again and this time she picked up but she sounded distraught, Mike assumed that this was because of Kyle's disappearance as there was no way someone of his status disappearing for the entire day wouldn't go unnoticed.

"Are you okay?" Mike wore the mask of a friend but Jane's voice was cracked.

[[I'm not okay, Mike…]] Jane confessed, there was no point in lying about it.

"What's wrong? Do you have a cold or have you been crying?" Mike questioned.

He wanted to confirm that things were going according to plan, if she could talk then there was no way that Kyle's death had reached her or she would have shut down.

[[It is Kyle…]] Jane stopped herself from completing the sentence but before she could complete it, a sign of struggle could be heard there.

"Um… Jane? Are you okay?" Mike questioned.

[[Mike, leave her alone. She's in no state to talk to you…]] Ella barked but she didn't hang up the phone because Jane took it back soon after.

"Ella really doesn't like me, does she?" Mike laughed it off.

[[It isn't like that. We're at the hospital with Kyle so everyone is a little tense…]] Jane confessed.

It was past midnight so what the hell were they doing there but that wasn't the detail that surprised Mike.

"Kyle… is in the hospital?" Mike's voice broke.

He was sure that he had delivered a fatal wound, there was no way for him to survive that attack because it was a fatal blow, wasn't it?

[[I'm sorry! I didn't mean to get you worried but the doctor said he will be fine.]] Jane responded.

She heard his voice break and mistook that for him worrying about Kyle when it was the opposite.

Mike could hear Ella in the background telling her to hang up but Mike had one more question to ask.

"Which hospital are you?" But this was a question he never got the answer to because the phone hung up.

Mike's eyes widened in shock, he couldn't believe that Kyle survived the ordeal because there was only one thing he could do to rectify his mistake.

He had to find Kyle and kill him before he could speak to the police because he was unconscious and in no condition to talk to the cops even though Mke had no idea what state he was in.

Mike had a rough idea of the hospital that Kyle was in and that was the same one that Ella had been admitted to earlier.

But he knew the area would be under surveillance by the police, Kyle was a high profile man after all but Mike wasn't aware that to the police, Kyle was just a normal person but this was the advantage of false perception.

This could very well be the thing that saves his life.

-

The night passed and a new day arrived, Kyle was still unconscious but he was all right.

He might not be awake but his body was responding to treatment and the blood transfusion was a success as well. This was good news because they didn't have too many blood bags to spare.

The surgery had sewed together the torn flesh and the ruptured intensities within.

Jane and Ella spent the entire night there, it was understandable that Jane did this but Ella had no reason to.

She could easily support her friend from a distance so was she here because of Jane or Kyle?

Ella warned Jane to cut down on her communication with Mike because it might be deemed inappropriate to give him access to everything that was going on in her life, she knew that Jane might not see it as a big deal but this was mostly due to her inexperience but this was why she was here– to guide her.

She wasn't going to let Jane ruin what she had with Kyle with someone as inconsequential as Mike.

Ella was becoming stern in her stance and even though Jane didn't like it, she understood where Ella was coming from.

You can't be friends with someone that has shown an interest in you, the friendship ends there and then.

This was either Jane being hopeful or simply being nice but it wasn't helping her relationship with Kyle.

She needed to show Kyle what she was all about and hope that qualifies her to be his girlfriend.

The police officers were still lingering about and Nate came as early as he could to see how Kyle was doing.

"Good morning mate," Ella greeted but the way she pronounced it made it hard for him to pick out the connotation.

"Hi Ella, how're you?" Nate greeted her, she hadn't showered or freshed up yet she still looked so good.

Nate was surprised but before he could be drawn in fully, he looked over at Jane to greet her as well.

He could tell by a single look who was more affected by this, Jane had been crying and it was obvious due to her bloodshot eyes.

But a police officer finally approached them

after a brief conversation with the doctor.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 121 - 121: 3 Days Later.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

It took Kyle three days to wake up from his slumber; in that timeframe. A lot had happened.

The throbbing headache that rocked his head was persistent, it felt like his brain was going into labour and Kyle could barely think straight alongside the pain in his torso.

The nurses gathered around him to make sure that he was alright. They checked his vitals before confirming there was nothing wrong with him.

Kyle could remember everything he dreamt about within those three days but the moment his eyes opened, it flooded in with everything he tried his best to forget.

Kyle just began to sob, no one knew why he was crying or what was going on but the most logical conclusion.

He must have been scared he nearly died but this was all that they concluded.

Kyle wasn't allowed to see anyone just yet until he spoke to the police because they had discovered the bodies there.

There was no telling who was responsible for their deaths because they were killed with a gun while Kyle had a knife wound, not a bullet.

This was the only complication as it could be Kyle that fired those shots to kill these three people even if it was in self-defense. But before he could do it, they managed to stab him but there was no one else there.

There were only three bodies and no murder weapon available and this was why they needed Kyle to piece together the information that they had.

Two police officers walked into his room and sat across his bed but Kyle wasn't uneasy by their presence.

"Mr. Kyle, it is good to see you're well," The male officer began the conversation.

Kyle's eyes darted at the female officer, she had a buzz cut but a very intimidating aura.

"It is good to be alive, I guess…" Kyle responded.

"I'm Officer Oliver and that over there is my partner…" Oliver paused, waiting for his partner to introduce herself.

"I'm Officer Priscilla and we are here to ask you some questions regarding the attack," Priscilla had a deep yet almost hypnotic voice.

If Kyle hadn't looked at her, he would have assumed it was a man he was speaking to, but with the way America was, there was a good chance that Priscilla was Patrick.

"What do I remember…." Kyle muttered out loud. He sounded disoriented like he couldn't recall the events, but that was impossible.

There was nothing that he couldn't remember so this was Kyle acting the fool.

"Anything would be helpful, Kyle," Priscilla encouraged him to speak up.

"I can't remember, it all feels hazy…" Kyle muttered but this was common for victims of such attacks to forget the events that put them in that state.

"I remember going for a walk and someone asking for my help. I don't know why but I followed them. I sort of blacked out after that," Kyle lied but there was no reason for this.

If he told the police officers the truth, they would have moved in to get Mike arrested.

But that would be the end of it, Kyle knew he didn't have enough evidence.

He knew how important evidence was and if he somehow told the police that Mike was responsible and he got off because there was no evidence.

There would be eyes on Mike and this wouldn't allow Kyle to enact his revenge.

He didn't want Mike arrested, he wanted him dead and this was why he lied to the police.

All he needed to do was show them the CCTV footage. He learned from Ryder and installed this in the building.

He used the latest technology, it wasn't an eyesore and people wouldn't even know that there was CCTV, that was the point of it.

This would establish Mike made contact with Kyle that day but it wouldn't be enough to put him at the crime scene.

Or at least, this was what Kyle believed would happen and he knew that the best-case scenario would be Mike ending up in prison.

He didn't want that, that would be too light a punishment but Kyle wasn't a killer either so this was a tricky situation for him.

"Do you remember anything else?" Priscilla asked, jotting down what was being said on her notepad.

Kyle knew lying to a police officer could be a crime if it interfered with the investigation of an active crime scene but that was only if they were caught.

"If you remember anything. Here is my card," Priscilla handed him a card with her contact information.

Kyle took it but had no intention of letting them know that it was Mike who attacked him.

Because if he revealed this information carelessly then there was a good chance that Jane would be affected as well.

"Say, Kyle. Do you own a gun?" Oliver questioned Kyle, this was an important question because if he said no then that would mean that there was a fourth person on the loose.

"No, I have never even held one before," Kyle responded in a dismissive tone.

Oliver shot Priscilla a telling glance because both of them knew what this meant.

He didn't know how far Mike was willing to go but he wasn't going to risk anything whatsoever.

The police officers left his room and Kyle heaved a loud sigh of relief.

.He couldn't believe that things had gotten this complicated but he wanted this to be chalked off as a random attack by strangers that tried to rob him.

But the fact that there were bodies increased the severity of this situation and Kyle's story wasn't adding up.

If they decided to rob him, who killed them? It wasn't illegal to own a firearm/

Was it possible that Kyle had an unlikely hero? That wasn't accurate because the shots Mike had missed damaged the walls in the nearby building.

This meant that someone shot at Kyle and missed, that was the most likely scenario at this point.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 122 - 122: The Man Who Could Not Be Killed.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Jane rushed into the hospital room the moment she was given the clear with Ella trailing behind her.

They wanted to hear all the information that Kyle had to give because there was no telling if this would happen again.

The moment Jane saw Kyle, she jumped on him, forgetting for a brief second that he was recovering from a stab wound.

Jane didn't care at that moment and tightened her grip around him.

"H-Hey…!" Kyle struggled to say amidst the pain from every inch of his body but he soon felt something moist drop on his shoulder.

It was Jane's tears, Kyle smiled, realizing that she must have been scared out of her mind.

Kyle looked at Ella and she couldn't hold in her smile because she too was as scared as Jane but she hid it a lot better than her best friend.

God, you look like shit," Ella said under her breath and Kyle chuckled at this because it caught him off guard.

"ELLA!" Jane called her out, looking back at her friend while in Kyle's arms.

"It is fine, Jane. It is fine," Kyle reassured her because he saw the humour in the statement as she wasn't wrong either way.

He did look like shit but that was what being unconscious for two days does to a person, he was unkempt and looked tired due to the eyebags under his eyes.

It brought him back to the days he was stressed about his daily life with little to no income to get by but the ladies also looked like shit even though he didn't say it.

He knew that they only looked this way because of him, they stayed in the hospital to make sure he was alright when they didn't need to.

Nate was nowhere to be seen, Kyle vaguely remembered hearing his voice before he lost consciousness, Nate was the one who saved his life.

Kyle realized that he was right to keep Nate because there was no way that anyone else would have reached him as fast as Nate did.

By putting himself in that environment, Nate put himself in danger because what would have happened if Mike was still around?

There was no way for Nate to fight Mike, Mike was on another level and he didn't fight to win, he fought to kill.

That gave him the edge as he didn't hold anything back but luckily, none of that happened.

They spoke for a bit but then Jane revealed something that sent chills down Kyle's bones.

"You know, Mike called and he sounded worried when I told him you were in the hospital," Jane informed Kyle but Kyle's body immediately went limp.

He recalled the event that nearly took his life but hearing the man that was responsible calling to check in on him was nothing short of psychotic.

Ella noticed the drastic change in his body language and kissed her teeth in irritation.

Why did Jane feel the need to bring him up when she had told her numerous times that Mike wasn't a topic she should discuss casually with Kyle?

"Mike called?" Kyle questioned and Jane nodded to confirm this because she didn't want to keep anything from Kyle but sometimes, full transparency wasn't the answer.

"Yes! I think you two will get along well," Jane was hopeful but all Kyle did was smile before slowly breaking away from her.

"I bet we will," Kyle said and Ella quickly intercepted the conversation.

"We should probably leave you to rest. Don't go dying on us," Ella said.

The way she spoke to him sounded a lot like they were friends, it brought a smile to his face.

"If I die, who takes care of you both?" Kyle questioned to which Ella chuckled.

"You need to be more careful Kyle, I don't know what happened but we were worried," Ella confessed.

"We?" Kyle found an opportunity to tease her to which Ella rolled her eyes.

"You should head home and freshen up. I know you are both exhausted but I'm alive and well," Kyle reassured them.

He knew that they couldn't stay here forever and needed to get on with their lives because the last thing he would want to be was a liability.

Jane and Ella agreed with him but once again, Kyle didn't let loose any information regarding the attack.

No one knew what happened, all they knew was that he was attacked by some random people after he tried to help them.

As Ella and Jane were walking out, in came Nate with the wildest smile on his face.

"YOU ARE ALIVE!" Nate screamed at the top of his lungs before charging at Kyle like a bull.

"W-Wait!" Kyle knew he had to stop him because, unlike Jane, Nate was sure to cause massive fall damage.

-

"Hey Jane…" Ella called her name as they were in the Uber, returning home.

"Yes Ella, is there a problem?" Jane responded.

"Did you notice Kyle tense up the moment you called Mike's name?" Ella questioned but the look on Jane's face gave her the answer she sought.

"He was surprised that Mike was worried," Jane responded with a sigh.

"I shouldn't have brought that up, should I?" Jane realized that she might have made a mistake because talking about Mike took the attention away from Kyle.

Even though this wasn't her intention, she could see how it could be translated like this.

"That's not it… I know that body language, he was frightened," Ella thought to herself.

She had spent years living with Jane, a girl who was bullied throughout her childhood so she could pick up on these things but why would Kyle be scared of Mike?

Jane knew it wasn't in her place to dig deeper into this as it might do more harm than good.

But her curiosity needed to be satisfied.

"No, you shouldn't, at least not then," Ella confessed.

Ella made sure to give Jane the reality checks she needed because she was the only one who would.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 123 - 123: A Visitor Or..
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Calista was so busy with her acting but even with that, she got worried when she couldn't reach Kyle. There was no way she could and tried visiting him but there was no one home.

This made her restless and it began to affect her job because she couldn't take her mind off him.

"Where are you, Kyle!?" Calista muttered under her breath with her phone in hand.

She was reckless and couldn't believe that she hadn't heard from Kyle for some time now.

She began to question if he had ghosted her, that was the only logical explanation for something this drastic to happen.

Calista hated how affected she was by this but it wasn't in her place to make a scene.

This was always a possibility, she knew that it would happen sooner or later but she got ahead of herself and began to get too comfortable.

This was why she kept it simple but Kyle had managed to break through her defense.

In the end, she wasn't surprised that this was the result.

She actually thought she could keep someone like Kyle even after she had been a bitch to him?

Kyle had shown her a lifestyle that no one else would be able to live up to.

She kept checking her phone and Lorenzo noticed this.

He noted that she was uneasy and decided to talk to her about what could potentially be troubling her.

"Are you alright?" Lorenzo questioned but Calista's attention was on her phone.

Lorenzo's words fell on deaf ears but Calista snapped out of it the moment her phone rang.

She looked at it and saw it was a call from Kyle.

Only then did she look up and see Lorenzo standing there, wondering how long he had been there

Calista picked up the call without hesitation and every thought she had melted away the moment she heard those words.

[[Hey you…]]

Calista had a wide smile on her face and Lorenzo didn't need to be a detective to know that whoever was at the other end of the phone must have been the reason for her unusual behaviour these past couple of days.

"Kyle! Where have you been!?" Calista couldn't hold back but Kyle realized that she must have been worried out of her mind.

However, he couldn't have her worry about him even more so he had to pick between lying and telling the truth.

A lie would have been more effective but Kyle learned a thing from Jane, honesty.

That was the most reassuring thing a person can give another and Kyle told her everything that happened to him these past couple of days– at least the version he told the others.

Calista's jaw dropped and she realized that Kyle could have died with her not knowing any better.

This frightened her and made her realize that she needed to be more involved in Kyle's life.

She got embarrassed that she thought this was about her when he was fighting for his life, it made her feel selfish.

Calista had a scene to act but couldn't focus, she wanted to go to Kyle.

Lorenzo noticed that she needed to go and sighed upon seeing how restless she was.

"AHHHG!" Lorenzo suddenly screamed at the top of his lungs and everyone looked in his direction, startled.

"What's wrong!?" Calista was closest to him.

"Go…" Lorenzo said in a whisper, they were supposed to act a scene together but there was no way for that scene to go on if one of them was unable to.

Lorenzo was pretending to have an episode.

Calista mouthed a "thank you" to him and left the studio to go to Kyle.

-

Kyle was affected by his three-day slumber, it felt like he rediscovered a lost part of himself.

He couldn't shake it off but knew he might have to revisit the home he grew up in.

Everything that Kyle experienced in that nightmare wasn't how he remembered it.

This included his mother's death but these were thoughts that he couldn't dwell on right now.

"Man, fuck my life…" Kyle muttered under his breath.

He realized how easy it was for him to lose everything he had, all it would take was a fatal wound.

But it wasn't the adrenaline in his body that saved him, it was the endurance that the system he has been blessed bestowed upon him when he drew the lottery.

Kyle knew that Mike couldn't come here to finish the job as it was too risky.

If not the CCTV camera but the security, this hospital was in no way cheap especially Kyle, who had been moved into the VVIP section.

That was how the hospital operated, they had a slave for the everyday people and a specialized location for the rich. This setting worked because it meant that they could treat everyone without depriving one of service.

There was a certain fee you pay, Kyle paid that fee the moment the ladies were gone with his black card and was moved to that part of the hospital.

It had heightened security but the most important part of this compartment was the comfort.

It was far more comfortable, the service was more or less the same as the other section.

The doctors and nurses were alert, they treated every life like it mattered, an art that has been lost in other hospitals.

Kyle was ushered into a room and Calista was on her way so they would get all the privacy they would need but something told Kyle he was in for a scolding.

"Mister Kyle, you have a guest," A nurse informed Kyle.

"Oi system, show me Calista's location," Kyle requested and a cyan screen appeared to show that it wasn't her, she was approximately five minutes away.

It couldn't be Jane, Ella, or Nate either.

"Sir?" The nurse asked, waiting for an answer but Kyle knew he had to be careful with his choice because there was o

only one person came to mind.

And that was Mike.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 124 - 124: Bonds.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Kyle was worried about who could be waiting to see him, but he had nothing to lose.

Even if it was Mike, this wasn't a movie—there was no way Mike could do anything to him here and get away with it.

That would be foolish. Kyle was safest here, but there was nothing he could do if he was caught lacking outside.

Mike had already demonstrated that he could take a life without batting an eye, but Kyle wasn't sure he could do the same.

Kyle was frightened by this prospect and knew that it was one thing he wouldn't be able to foresee, as it was his emotions speaking for him.

Kyle gave the nurse permission to bring in the visitor, but his eyes were already darting around the room, searching for something to defend himself with if the situation called for it.

However, he was shocked to see that it was Jones standing at the door.

"Wait, what?" Kyle blurted out, confused by how Jones knew about his situation. There was no reason for him to be aware of it.

"Surprised to see me?" Jones spoke softly. He didn't know Kyle's current mental state, so he chose the gentle approach.

Kyle was still in disbelief. "Of course I am! Are you stalking me now!?" he barked, but quickly winced in pain, clutching his torso.

"Boy, ain't nobody stalking you," Jones shot him down, sitting in the chair available in Kyle's room.

Jones pulled out his phone and showed Kyle something. Kyle was surprised to see that he had unknowingly sent the location not only to Nate but also to Jones.

Jones was one of his most frequent contacts, and in the chaos of the moment, Kyle hadn't taken the time to double-check his messages. But this still didn't explain how Jones had found him.

"Oh… my bad, I didn't mean to bother you," Kyle apologized. Jones had his own problems and responsibilities; the last thing he needed was to worry about another grown adult.

"Bother me? You NOT paying your rent for months bothered me, not this," Jones reassured him, letting him know he wasn't a bother.

What Jones didn't tell Kyle was that he had searched over ten hospitals looking for him, and because Kyle wasn't registered under his real name, there was nothing that could be done.

Nate had thought registering Kyle under a false name would be the safest option. If someone were to try a premeditated murder, registering him under his real name would make it easier for the killer to track him down, so Nate had used his own name instead.

Calista's visit to his home and her admission that she hadn't been in contact with Kyle only reinforced the idea that something had happened to him. The next day, Jones began his search.

The only reason Ella and Jane knew about the situation was because the doctor had a relationship with them.

Kyle was attacked, and Jones was worried not because of business but because Kyle was his friend. Calista had called him right after speaking with Kyle, and they exchanged contact information to stay updated on any developments regarding his disappearance.

Jones looked haggard. Kyle could tell with a glance that he was stressed, and there was no way he looked like this because of his fight with Aiysha.

Kyle knew why, but neither of them said a word. It was nice knowing he had people who would look out for him. In that moment, Kyle knew he wasn't alone.

They sat in comfortable silence, both relieved to see each other, but those feelings went unspoken.

Jones could tell that Kyle was shaken—who wouldn't be after an attempt on their life?

Kyle tried to act like he was okay, but not everyone was fooled by the mask he wore.

Calista soon joined them, surprised to see Jones already there, even though she had told him when she was on her way.

But her attention was on Kyle. She couldn't believe that she nearly lost him.

She just stood there awkwardly.

"I think I forgot my freezer in my car," Jones blurted out. It made no sense, but it was an excuse to leave them alone.

Calista didn't know what to do, even with Jones out of the room.

"Come here," Kyle gestured, and she walked slowly toward him. The closer she got, the heavier her heart became as she realized the gravity of the situation.

The first tear escaped her eyes, but she quickly wiped it away. That didn't stop the flood that followed.

Calista had lost control. The relief she felt seeing Kyle was overshadowed by the fear of losing him.

She slowly buried herself in his arms, and Kyle realized that she probably needed more comfort than he did.

This was the only reason Mike took this chance, lowering her gag.

"You must think I'm a monster," Mike chuckled. Jane was never a good liar and knew that if she did, Mike would see through it– something she couldn't risk.

"No… If anything, I see you more clearly," Jane's voice was soft, she knew it would be stupid to try and talk him down from doing what he planned to do.

If anything, he must have thought about this for far too long for her to dissuade him in minutes but if she did this, she risked showing her true motive.

It wasn't going to end well and she knew it so she had to take a different approach.

"Look at me, Mike," Jane sounded like a mother comforting her child.

Mike reluctantly met her gaze, he expected her to try and talk him down, offering to run away with him so he would spare Kyle.

"Do what you have to do, I will be here waiting for you," Jane responded and Mike's eyes widened in shock.

This was the last thing he expected to hear from her, did Jane understand him?

"W-What the hell do you mean by that?" Mike stuttered, showing a crack in his otherwise ruthless persona.

"You are not a monster, Mike. I have never had someone go this far to show that they care about me. You could have hurt me but you didn't. It might sound crazy but I feel like I'm just meeting you," Jane said softly, it was hard to read her emotions due to her soft-spoken nature but Mike was disturbed by this.

He knew that good girls tend to have a thing for bad boys but never did he think that would apply to Jane.

She spotted a chink in his armour and planned to keep digging away at it but before the conversation could go further, a man walked in.

He had no hair but the tattoos littered not only his head. His face told her that this was a man she wanted no business with.

The way he looked at her, he didn't hide his perverse nature whatsoever and Jane immediately positioned herself behind Mike like a helpless puppy.

Mike liked this; it showed that she saw him as her protector, was this perhaps Stockholm syndrome? This was what Jane was going for and it was the only narrative that would work on Mike.

Mike noticed her unease and the protector instinct in him was subconsciously triggered.

"Step outside with me for a minute…" Mike instructed the man but he made sure to lock the door behind him the moment they did.

He might be one of his associates but Mike didn't trust him enough not to lay a finger on Jane.

Kyle was around the corner and could feel a thumping heart, it was so loud that he became convinced that others could hear it but this was impossible.

He looked up in horror but saw that it was a man that reeked of alcohol and the stench that oozed from his body nearly made Kyle throw up.

Kyle simply moved out of his way and walked right past him.

This place wasn't a haven for Mike and his supposed band of misfits.

It was an abandoned place that had people in dire conditions relocated there because, for one, there were no laws surrounding it.

Kyle chuckled to himself, this gave him a lot more freedom and the fact that Mike had no idea he had made his plan to isolate Jane had failed.

If these people here weren't his minions then it granted Kyle a lot more freedom when it came to movement.

He found a dirty rag nearby alongside a few emptied-out bottles of water.

Kyle tossed the rag over his expensive clothes, he didn't care if the stench ruined it as he could simply buy another. He noticed Mike's car parked nearby and knew that he had to be careful.

He couldn't beat Mike in a fight and Mike had the upper hand considering he had a gun he wasn't afraid to use.

"Should I have called for backup?" Kyle thought to himself but he didn't know what Ella was thinking right now.

She must have tried to call him but his line wasn't going through and if this was the case, Kyle knew she would only worry all the more.

But he asked her for trust and this was the time to test her resolve in this manner.

He was a bald man and that was the only feature that Kyle could make out due to the moon barely illuminating this area.

"You really got yourself a beauty this time," The man praised Mike but his voice was deep, it had bass.

"You cannot touch her. Unfortunately, this one is not for fun," Mike responded and at this moment, Kyle realized that this was something that Mike did often.

He couldn't wrap his head around how someone this sick could get into contact with Jane and Kyle began to wonder what would have happened if he didn't meet Jane at all.

What the fuck did Mike have in store for her? That didn't matter.

"Are you sure? Not even a little look?" The man barked but there was something about Mike that made him quickly correct.

"I-I'm just kidding, no need to be so serious! Fine, I will watch over her," The man conceded.

"Mike is their leader?" Kyle thought to himself, he also noticed that Mike made it clear that Jane wasn't to be touched.

"Where are you going anyway?" The man questioned, wondering why he couldn't watch over her himself if she was so important.

"I have one more person to bring here," Mike informed his colleague but who was referring to?

But one thing was certain, Jane was safe for now but Kyle knew that if he didn't have any mobile network here, that would be the case for Mike as well.

That meant if he wished to reach out to him then he needed to get out of here.

And that was exactly what he did, Mike hopped into his car and drove away.

He had a hostage that evened the playing field and there was no way he was going to let his trump card go.

He knew that Jane would hate him and want nothing to do with him after this but the truth was, Mike didn't plan on letting her go.

"Man, what a fucking buzz kill…" The man muttered but there was something malevolent about him, this man was a bigger threat than Mike and Kyle could feel it.

He grabbed his crotch. No, he was stroking it.

"As if I was going to listen to you either way," He muttered before heading back in.

The only thing stopping him from getting to Jane was the locked door but would that be enough?

That told Jane that he had a plan and she had to trust him.

He used his Total Recall to analyze the state of the gun, he read up about guns when Mike had failed to kill him and knew every single detail in regards to it.

He noticed a minuscule one that implied the gun was not only old but it had a high chance of jamming.

This was a gamble because Kyle wasn't a hundred percent sure but it worked out in the hand.

Mike, however, was no stranger in this situation and took a step back to effectively go out of Kyle's range but Kyle slammed the metal bar on his foot.

Kyle realized the direction it was pointed was Jane, he looked in that direction but saw that Jane had already taken cover.

He grabbed the gun and a battle began to take control of the weapon, Kyle stomped the foot he had just hit to gain the advantage. It ultimately resulted in the gun being flung out of his hand, skidding away from them.

Chapter 133 - 133: Kill Shot.

But she wasn't going to miss this time, Mike was about to kill Kyle and Jane knew she had to do something about it.

This allowed Kyle to kick him off and send him tumbling to the ground.

He looked at Jane and saw that she was trembling, horrified by what she had done.

She couldn't believe that she had shot someone.

The gun dropped to the ground.

"W-What have I done? I killed him?!" Jane was in disbelief, she knew that Mike was a bad person but this wasn't something she pictured herself doing.

" I won't let you burden yourself," Kyle whispered before planting a kiss on her forehead.

He was going to kill him regardless of if Jane had shot him or not.

He truly believed these words and this removed the weight that shackled her heart.

This sage was over in a single night, Jane had no idea how Kyle found her so fast but she knew that not every question needed an answer.

She was content not knowing because it only made her feel safer.

And the man who took her must be responsible for the attack on Kyle.

This was why she decided to remain stationed outside of her place but under such short notice.

There was no way that anyone would come to this location to watch over the house.

Kyle also told her where to find Mike or rather his corpse and Priscilla was on the move right away with her siren blaring in the distance.

The incident didn't make the news but this was partly due to fears of a potential retaliation by whatever gang Mike was associated with.

This was the man they had been looking for as his DNA, most notably, his semen had been found in different crimes.

He was in the system already long before he was taken in for this particular crime but the DNA obtained from those crime scenes never linked back to him. Priscilla knew it had to be him because these crimes were as violent as the ones that he was accused of committing.

He only got off due to a technicality and that was the semen sample not matching the blood they had in the system but Priscilla presented a theory that was immediately shot down by her superior.

And that was the possibility that the suspect had two sets of DNA, this was possible due to a very rare congenital condition called Chimerism.

This was also partly due to the department cutting back on expenses and didn't feel the need to carry out other tests.

Priscilla saw his dead body and instantly knew this was the same man even though he went by a different name now, which was Mike.

This man was called Clarence Thomas and there was no reason for him to change his name.

But with his body in their possession, she was able to extract sufficient DNA and run the appropriate test to confirm the theory she had had for months.

And it came back positive, Clarence had artificial chimerism, it was because he had bone marrow, and this effectively changed his DNA.

He was committing crimes right under the nose of the police and this little abnormality allowed him to commit crimes under the radar for so long.

She was grateful that Kyle managed to put a bullet through his head because this solved and gave a few unsolved cases an ending that would bring the family closure.

She didn't say it but her body language was all over the place.

"Come here…" Kyle gestured with his hand.

Jane couldn't look him in the eyes as she did.

Kyle placed his hands on her hips and pulled her in.

"Are you jealous?" Kyle questioned with a smirk.

Jane began fiddling her fingers, not knowing what to do with them but she gave no verbal response to his question.

"You have nothing to be jealous of, no one compares to my princess," Kyle said but once again, he didn't tell her she was the only one granting him plausible deniability.

Jane didn't care at the moment because he told her what she wanted to hear and that was evident by the big smile on her face.

Chapter 153 - 153: Harem Trouble?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

She wasn't about that life, and there was no way that would change overnight.

Kyle knew it was possible to shape her into that, but it would take time, and his nuts couldn't wait that long.

She couldn't shake the thought that her husband had the upper hand. He was never the type to enter something he didn't think he could win.

This was what had her worried. She thought about calling Kyle, but she had already bothered him enough.

He wasn't her man, and their relationship was based on an agreement.

She didn't want to overwhelm him with her problems so early, and she knew that if she did, Kyle would abandon her.

There was nothing she could do if that happened. They weren't bound by any legal documents, even though the money currently in her account could sort out her life for a few years.

The chances of losing custody were slim, but they weren't zero.

That was what frightened her. And there he was, her husband, walking through the hall.

Unlike her, he looked confident and smiled all the way through, greeting people there. But this only made Cassandra more anxious.

Why did he know so many people? That could only mean one thing: he had either been here before, or these people were his friends.

"Miss Cassandra, is there anyone we could call?" the lawyer asked, but Cassandra didn't understand why she would ask this.

This only caused her to freak out more.

"Call? Why? What's going on?" Cassandra questioned, but the female lawyer gripped her hands firmly.

"You're anxious. We need you calm for the trial. Financial stability is very important in cases like this, but I need you to calm down," the lawyer reassured her, her soothing voice washing over her.

She knew not to involve Kyle; he had already done more for her than necessary.

Asking for his presence would not only be doing too much, but it would also disrupt Kyle's daily routine.

There was nothing left for Cassandra to do but trust her lawyer, Jessica.

She was losing it in the divorce, and this was used as a strong basis for her inability to care for the baby.

Jessica couldn't bring up the bank account balance because it didn't match her wages.

There would be questions about how she got it, and if it showed that a random man sent it, it could be considered manipulation.

Why? There was a chance that he sent it for the sole purpose of presenting it to the court, only to take it back when it was over.

This had been done before, which was why lawyers avoided using this route as a strong defense.

But Jessica also noticed that the judge was biased. There was no doubt that his verdicts favored her ex-husband, which was unusual.

However, the case was adjourned, giving them time to re-strategize.

"Now? I'm afraid you have to show that you've acquired a property to raise your child in. But not only that, I think presenting a partner would help sway the judge and show that you are more than capable of providing not only shelter but a father figure," Jessica said. Her tone, however, didn't sound worried at all.

She wasn't swayed because, despite this hurdle, she was confident that they would win the case.

This was the truth, every word that came out of his mouth.

Even though she hated to admit it, and despite trusting him with all her being.

This was a woman thing, and Jane was slowly becoming territorial over him.

The truth was, she had no idea, but it seemed like the right thing to say.

"Because you're involved. Everything just works out with you…" Jane muttered with a sheepish smile.

"I'm like a superhero, huh?" Kyle relished the thought.

Jane managed to ease his worries, even though one couldn't really call it that.

Kyle wanted to see if Cassandra would back out because a big part of the reason she accepted his terms was because of her child.

He didn't have to wait long, as a text soon came.

Jane knew who it was without asking, but kept her mouth shut.

She liked that Kyle was transparent with her and wasn't about to make him regret that decision.

Kyle's facial expression didn't change, so it was hard to read him.

"Is everything alright?" Jane asked before Kyle put away his phone and sighed.

"It looks like this is going to be more difficult than I thought," Kyle responded.

Ella had told her he had a girlfriend, but after meeting up with him, he was able to give her the reassurance she needed to know she was still of use to him.

This was proving to be a hassle. Calista knew that developing feelings might have been a mistake because Kyle held all the cards right now.

There was nothing she could do about it either. It was too late to pull back because Kyle had already done so.

However, Calista knew she had to step up her game if she had competition.

She wasn't going to lose out—this was the mindset she knew she had to adopt.

She couldn't sulk and moan about how she wasn't the only woman; she only had to worry about holding her current position.

Lorenzo was seated beside her, but she did a good job of masking her current frame of mind.

"Hey, Lorenzo, what do you think about polyamory?" Calista asked out of the blue.

This question caught Lorenzo off guard.

"I believe the human soul wasn't meant to be shared with just one person. A single person can be the soulmate of multiple people," Lorenzo responded.

It made sense that he was polyamorous—he was European—but this response wasn't what she expected.

She had thought he would be disgusted, but his answer made it seem like a normal thing.

She, herself, had seen multiple men simultaneously, but they weren't aware of each other, even though they suspected it.

But Kyle was blatantly waving it in her face. Was this all just a game to make her overthink things?

If he did have a girlfriend, wouldn't that make her his mistress?

Calista needed answers, and there was only one way to get them.

Chapter 154 - 154: The Force

Why did she think this way? It was simple, that was her reality.

It was inconceivable that Kyle would do anything to hurt, a thought that was taboo to her.

She wouldn't want to knowingly soil his reputation.

This told Aiysha that this lady wasn't a relative or anything of that sort.

Unlike the other lady that she encountered, this seemed timid yet polite or she wouldn't have opened the door in the first place.

If he was going to stay here then it wouldn't hurt to be friends with a few of the residents.

She had no idea who this woman was but risked it by inviting her in.

He was taking the restaurant so seriously even though there was no reason to as they would be set for life once this renovation was over.

It would be a hot site considering the rent remained the same for the first year, it was a steal.

The smell in the house implied that she had been here for a while.

"I didn't know Kyle had a girlfriend, not one as cute as you either," Aiysha said, wanting to know what kind of man he was.

Jane's face flushed red, if they weren't a thing, it was clear that Jane liked him with her reaction.

Her ocean-blue eyes looked to the ground, Aiysha helped herself to a seat and placed the document on the table.

"I cannot show you its content as the last thing I need is to make him angry," Aiysha shook her head.

"I think that is best. Ah!" Jane exclaimed out of the blue, startling Aiysha.

"I forgot to ask if you wanted anything to drink, " Jane looked eager to impress.

Aisha couldn't hold her chuckle, this girl was different from the kind of person she thought Kyle would go for.

Jessica was impressed by his deducting skills, this was something she entertained but there was no evidence to support this.

This was why she didn't see it fit to tell her client about this but Kyle had carelessly spilled it out.

She expected Cassandra to panic but she didn't.

She liked driving around in their new car, it made their work a lot easier.

It had more of a presence as well, it turned the heads of a few people because this monster was capable of doing things normal cars only dreamt about.

"Hm…" Priscilla looked at it before picking it up, she waited for the person who called to speak first.

[[Hello officer Priscilla…]]

She instantly recognized the voice, it was no doubt Kyle's voice.

"Kyle? What can I help you with?" Priscilla asked, taking a sip of her smoothie.

[[I think I have a case you might be interested in…]]

She was a lady who truly believed in justice and went the extra step to make sure it was served.

The just-concluded case of Clarence was proof of that and Kyle was about to play on that.

The donation he sent would have softened their perception of him and they would subconsciously want to repay him.

That was human nature and he was going to use to his advantage.

It shouldn't be hard to show that the judge accepted a bribe because Kyle had a plan on how to oust him.

The fact that this man was a prominent figure in society would make this somewhat high-profile even though the higher-ups were sure to try and sweep it under the rug.

The way she carried herself gave her away, most people could tell.

This was a small case in itself but the name would determine the scale of this case.

"Do you even know who this is?" Priscilla asked, Kyle couldn't read what she was feeling right now.

"I was hoping you would…" Kyle responded, something told him that this wasn't the first time that this woman had heard of this man.

The truth was, this man had been on her radar because he was one of the few judges that have given questionable verdicts in the past.

But there was one thing that he was very good at and that was eradicating all connections.

There was nothing to lead on with, he cleaned his tracks better than a hungry man cleans his plate.

Priscilla didn't reveal his name but she didn't need to, Kyle already knew all he needed to know about the man.

"I'm glad to hear that…" Kyle said but Priscilla wanted to confirm one more thing.

"Are you the one that donated those cars to the LAPD?" Priscilla questioned.

She was blunt and went right to the point with no small talk, she wasn't interested in any social interaction that didn't concern her occupation.

"I have no idea what you're talking about or those 10 cars," Kyle fired back.

He knew that she didn't state the number of cars and did this intentionally to help her settle one case.

Priscilla didn't even react, her face remained stoic, not giving him any reaction.

Jones brought the food at that moment and Kyle wanted to ask her why she was eating so light but this was none of his business.

Kyle already knew he couldn't get through to her via normal conversations and she didn't reveal anything about herself.

So there was no way for him to further this interaction without forcing things but maybe he didn't need to.

For once, he might as well enjoy the silence and that was what they both did.

They spent over ten minutes eating, the best part about this was the silence wasn't awkward.

After eating, they just sat there for a couple of more minutes without saying a single word.

Kyle expected her to be up and away the moment they finished but she was not in any hurry to leave.

"Silence is peaceful, isn't it?" Kyle asked but Priscilla didn't answer nor respond which intrigued Kyle all the more.

"I have to go," Priscilla said but Kyle wasn't done with her just yet.

"I haven't even told you about my plan or is this your way of making sure you see me again?" Kyle teased, this was his personality so it wasn't something he could help.

Naturally, not everyone would be a fan of it but was Priscilla one of those people.

She awkwardly returned to the table but there wasn't any hint of embarrassment.

"You had a plan and sat in silence until now?" Priscilla asked with a raised brow.

"Don't you have anything better to do?" She added, astonished by his lack of awareness.

"Hey, I thought we were enjoying each other's company," Kyle chuckled but once again, no reaction from Priscilla.

She felt like an AI that he couldn't get through to but it was refreshing to have someone this professional by his side.

It meant that this was the same treatment others would get, Kyle couldn't explain it but he felt a sense of relief. She wasn't easy and that was good.

Kyle proceeded to tell her his plan in great detail and Priscilla's face remained completely uninterested but her body language said otherwise.

She was intrigued that Kyle could think of something so simple yet devious.

"This could work," That was all she said, pondering on the possibility of success.

Kyle could tell but this was the only way to make sure she thought about him long after meeting him, he wanted to see what it would take to break that shell and hoped Oliver wouldn't be as difficult.

Chapter 156 - 156: Jones Regret
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

That was about it, there was nothing else to press a case but he knew that Priscilla was a lady that went out of her way if she thought someone was guilty.

And this was an unexpected blessing in disguise, one he could utilize to his advantage.

"Who was that? She is scary!" Jones pointed out; he was intimidated by Priscilla because she had this aura of someone that would beat the shit out of him if he looked at her the wrong way.

"A police officer," Kyle said the obvious but Jones raised a brow to this.

"Is that the kind of woman you go after? A woman that can beat you to death?" Jones questioned before sitting right across him.

"I mean, it keeps the relationship exciting, no?" Kyle responded.

"You're one crazy sonuvabitch!" Jones laughed it off.

The employees were looking at how well he got along with Kyle, no one would guess that Kyle was a tenant in his building.

They sounded like they had been friends for years but their relationship only just took a thing despite them knowing each other for quite some time.

"What can I say, if she can't kill you, are you even living?" Kyle said with a chuckle.

He didn't want to drag Jones into his problem, there was no telling what would happen if he did.

It was something that he couldn't risk either way because Jones already had his troubles.

The last thing he needed was getting him mixed with all of this.

Jones kept quiet for a second but that felt like an eternity, he tried to continue but Kyle realized it was about time he checked in on his friend.

"Is everything okay at home?" Kyle asked and Jones hesitated to give a response.

This was unlike him, Jones had quick wit so any hesitation gave Kyle his answer indirectly.

"It is okay, I'm here to listen," Kyle reassured him. He knew the last thing Jones complained about was not being able to keep up with her sex drive.

Kyle wondered if this was the thing causing trouble in their marriage, it might seem insignificant but sexual satisfaction was important.

"Marriage man, it isn't easy…" Jones finally spewed.

"You tell me, I'm not the one married," Kyle shrugged it off prompting Jones to chuckle faintly.

"I know it is hard, that is what makes it worth a damn but everything just seems to set her off these days. I work so hard and I'm a great father but I'm afraid I might not be so great a husband," Jones said.

Any other person would have jumped at him and told him about how great of a husband he is but that would be invalidating his concern. This was why Kyle just stayed and listened, he didn't confirm or deny any allegations that Jones laid on himself because he knew that there must be some truth to it.

A person knew themselves better than anyone else, this included their flaws.

Disregarding a flaw simply because you have seen a flawless version of themselves creates a disconnect and this was something Kyle wasn't about to do.

"Why do you feel that way?" Kyle asked, permitting Jnes to go into details.

"I don't know what else to say, everything's just falling apart," Jones confessed but Kyle knew he was beating around the bush without touching the major subject.

Kyle noted how Jones highlighted his might in other aspects except being a husband.

"Do you think she is cheating on you?" Kyle asked and judging from Jones's lack of a reaction, he knew he hit it right on the head.

"Tell me why you think so," Kyle said but a brief thought flashed through his mind.

"Damn, I sound like a freaking therapist!"

"The late nights, the not picking my calls, the switch up… I wouldn't even blame her," Jones was worn out but the last sentence almost made Kyle lose respect for the older man.

"You wouldn't blame her for cheating on you?" Kyle repeated, wanting to make sure this wasn't a slip of the tongue.

"I know how it sounds but I wasn't kidding when I said I haven't been the best husband," Jones reiterated.

"Tell me why you think this way," Kyle realized this wasn't about him and he had to keep his feelings in check.

"You know, Aiysha was never my first choice when it came to partners when it came to marriage.." Jones began but he felt like Kyle was judging him.

"I know what you're thinking, she is gorgeous! But she hasn't always been that way," Jones pointed out.

"You see, she was a woman I married not because I loved her but because she was a good wife. However, I grew to love her," Jones revealed.

Kyle was beginning to understand why she was so obsessed with the gym, she was once a lady of unhealthy weight. Jones married her that way and then she got her weight under control, turning into drop-dead gorgeous beauty.

"I cheated on her in the past, you see… And I have been trying to make it up to her ever since then," Jones confessed and there was his flaw.

Jones was too perfect a man but Kyle realized that his dedication was linked to his past.

Aiysha got into shape after the affair, she wasn't Jones's first choice and the only reason he married her was because she was a safe option.

He didn't have to worry about anything because of her size, she was obese and this made him complacent.

He cheated on her repeatedly with the girl he did have affection for but she cheated on him after declining to marry him, this brought an end to their relationship.

However, after a few years, they reconnected and she was looking even better than ever.

Things had gotten boring in his marriage and he sought to find some sort of excitement to wake his heart up.

And there she was, Chloe, ready to fill that role, which made Kyle feel alive once again.

But it couldn't last forever because Aiysha caught him but she did forgive him.

Jones ended the affair right away and earned that forgiveness, he begged her and even offered to give her a free pass if this meant she would forgive him.

She blamed herself and realized she had to work on herself so her husband wouldn't look outside.

This was the motivation for her transformation and ever since then, Jones has been the ideal husband but as of recent, his insecurity was getting the better of him.

Aiysha was a woman everyone wanted, she went from a woman no man desired to a woman every man desired.

Jones became worried that she would do to him what he once did to her.

Jones told Kyle in great detail about these events and all Kyle did was listen.

He didn't know Jones was this kind of man but everyone made mistakes and he had done enough to make up for his.

He couldn't judge him because marriages were complex and just because a story starts bad doesn't mean it couldn't end up good.

This made Jnes appear a lot more human to Kyle and realized that their marriage problem wasn't because of Aiysha but Jones.

"Yes, we are," Ella said but Kyle realized that they were on a one-way road with a huge gate at the front.

"The hell…?" Kyle thought to himself. At the end of that gate, multiple buildings made it look like a gated community.

Jane had only been to Ella's house once but she was very young so the perception she had of it as a kid didn't do it justice whatsoever.

Kyle's jaws dropped, tilting his head back as he looked at Ella in disbelief.

"Who the hell are you!?" Kyle asked in shock.

He was even more astonished that she was fighting for her life when she had such wealth under her thumb.

Or at least had, Kyle knew that the situation she found herself in when she was younger couldn't be helped.

But it made her sacrifice all the more impressive but Kyle saw an opportunity here.

He needed to know more, he stopped at the gigantic gate stopping them from accessing the estate.

Kyle hoped that they would be allowed in because in a few seconds, a few heavily armed men approached them, they had assault rifles strapped on them.

"Is this the fucking military base?" Kyle whispered to her, the men didn't equip their guns but their body language showed that they wouldn't hesitate if the situation called for it.

"Hello there partner," A man with a thick southern accent greeted, he was muscular but Kyle could tell he couldn't escape old age.

The saggy skin hanging on his face told him that this man was well into his sixties and must be the leader of this little squadron.

"Hello there sir, I think I have someone here that belongs to you," Kyle was cheeky but noticed that Jane's body had stiffened.

"Can you lower the back window for me, partner?" The man requested to which Kyle complied, it would take a madman to try and rob this place with the amount of security present.

The back window was lowered and the man walked there to see who else was in the vehicle.

"Aren't they expecting us?" Kyle thought to himself but the man soon screamed.

"EVERYONE ON THE GODDAMN GROUND! CODE RED! CODE RED!"

Jane's heart sank but before it could escalate, they heard laughter from the back.

"You old bag! How are you still alive!?" Ella questioned.

"I'm a lot tougher than I look," The man responded before signalling for the gate to be opened.

Ella knew him, he was one of the few people she got along with because she treated him more than security personnel but back then, he didn't hold this position and was a normal security guard so he wasn't treated with much regard.

This was why Ella's treatment stood out for him because she didn't need to do that but chose to.

"My parents should have taken a memo from your book," Ella rolled her eyes and the man's facial expression went cold.

"I'M KIDDING!" Ella had to quickly say when she realized she was using her humour with the wrong person.

"Don't mind her, that is how she copes," Kyle chimed in, grabbing Jane's hand in the process.

This brought her back but Kyle didn't even look at her and Jane felt her raging heart slow.

Kyle drove through the gates and knew that Ella was a different kind of money and was glad that he got into her good books.

If she could reconcile with her sister then there was no doubt that she could have a huge piece of this wealth that came with unimaginable influence.

Influence that money couldn't buy but Kyle was getting ahead of himself and knew he had to calm down.

There were women in uniform, ladies and men he assumed to be maids and butlers waiting at the entrance of the biggest house within this compound.

Kyle realized the gigantic houses beside them were quarters for the staff, if anything he was looking at a building that rivalled the Whitehouse in size.

But if it was only one person living here, it wouldn't make sense to live in such a big house alone but who was he to tell the rich how to live their life?

Kyle parked the car and before he knew it, a man sprung out of nowhere and offered to drive his car to the designated parking spot.

Kyle accepted but not before opening the car door for both Jane and Ella because he thought that Ella was exiled from this household but it looked like there were people who held her in high regard despite that.

This showed the kind of person she was if nothing else and Kyle wanted to show that he was treating her right so in turn, he would gain their favour.

Kyle glanced over at Ella and saw the look on her face, he had only heard bad things about this place but she was nostalgic just being here again.

Kyle knew she needed a moment to take it all in but wondered if her sister was around.

They didn't throw her out or refuse her entry, did this mean that they recognized her as some sort of authority in this household?

"Are we going to go in?" Kyle asked with a raised brow and this brought Ella back from her nostalgic trance.

"Don't mind me! Follow me!" Ella said, dragging Jane by the hand as the two ladies entered the house, leaving Kyle behind.

Kyle noticed the workers were on the younger side despite a few being older but at that age, what could they do?

There was a chance that they filled in the role of a mentor, who else was best equipped to train the younger generation than those who spent their entire life here?

Even though Ella remembered her parents hiring external workers so they could have an idea of how the outside world was.

Kyle shrugged, walking past them and inside the house, a single step within told him of the insurmountable wealth that these people possessed.

Every single thing here looked like they cost a fortune but Kyle had no idea where Ella or Jane were either.

"A house you can get lost in, what insanity…" Kyle mumbled but heard a voice beside him.

"Is there anything we can get for you, sir?" A man asked; he had on a white tuxedo with three maids behind him, waiting for Kyle to say what he wanted.

"Nothing for now, thank you very much but I do have a question," Kyle said and the man gave him his full attention.

"What will that be, sir?" The butler asked but Kyle looked over his shoulder to make sure neither Ella nor Jane was behind him.

Once he confirmed he was in the clear, he proceeded with what he wished to ask the butler.

"Is Ella's sister around?" Kyle questioned but the butler shook his head to deny this.

"I'm afraid Miss Cleopatra is not available but she will be joining us shortly," The man responded.

Kyle was satisfied with this answer and caught a glimpse of the maids standing behind him but averted his gaze just as fast when he heard laughter in the next compartment.

"Found you…" Kyle muttered under his breath in relief.

"Women," Kyle whispered to himself with a quirky smile.

Chapter 189 - 189: A Crack?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Calista had made arrangements to meet with the strange woman who supposedly knew Kyle. She didn't know how to process the situation because, ultimately, this was her betraying Kyle. That was why she hadn't kept him in the loop—there was no telling what could happen if he knew. It could be the end of their relationship, but if she met the woman and nothing happened, there would be no need to tell Kyle, either way.

Calista knew she had little to lose, but she was aware it had to be somewhere where she wasn't well-known. The last thing she wanted was to be recognized and have pictures taken while she was with this person.

She arrived at the location with Lorenzo, the first time they were meeting outside of work, which showed that Lorenzo was interested in deepening their bond outside of the office. This was understandable, as having a colleague in the industry could be helpful, but unlike her, Lorenzo wasn't as famous, despite his good looks.

"You look nervous," Lorenzo said, seated beside her as she watched the entrance of the restaurant with such intent.

"I'm just worried, that's all," Calista confessed, though Lorenzo didn't know the gravity of the situation.

"It will be fine, and if it isn't, we'll deal with it," Lorenzo reassured her, placing his hand on top of hers.

Despite his sexuality, Calista was clearly uncomfortable and removed her hand from his.

"I'll be fine, but thank you for coming here with me," Calista said, appreciative.

Lorenzo didn't take it to heart, as everyone had their different boundaries. If anything, he liked that she reinforced hers, knowing not to do this again.

"Anything for the famous Calista," Lorenzo said with a cheeky smile, but at that moment, someone walked into the restaurant.

She looked like someone who didn't belong. The way her head darted left and right anxiously, clearly searching for someone, was telling. The moment her eyes settled on Calista, she immediately walked out without confronting her.

"What the hell?" Calista blurted out.

"Wait!" Calista shouted before dashing toward her, but this took Lorenzo off guard. He couldn't catch up to her because he had to pay for what they had eaten.

Calista made it outside, but the woman was nowhere to be seen. Calista clicked her tongue in frustration, realizing that the woman must have been frightened out of her mind seeing her with someone else. She had told her to come alone, but Calista hadn't extended the same courtesy.

"Fuck!" Calista cursed aloud, because she hadn't gotten a clear view of the woman's face either.

This was a failure, at least for her, because the woman had only observed her from a distance. Her intention? Unknown.

But one thing was certain, Calista had played right into her hands.
