Kyle entered the room with the rest of the ladies and could hear them giggling, but the moment they saw him, they straightened up.

"Um, I like jokes as well, you know?" Kyle noticed the shift in energy, but the ladies didn't budge.

"It's girl talk. Now, who's ready for a tour?" Ella dismissed his curiosity and quickly changed the subject.

Kyle knew it couldn't be anything harmful to begin with, or Jane wouldn't entertain it. He trusted Jane to put him first. This was the type of person she was, and in a month, he'd gotten to know a side of her that took Ella years to reach.

"Careful now, or we might get lost," Kyle said, but Ella knew he was exaggerating.

"Scared? You can wait in the car," Ella teased, but Kyle just shook his head.

Jane liked how they went back and forth, but Kyle was doing all of this because he knew the world he was about to dive into would give him little to no time to spend with them. He would be busy once the completion of that movie came to pass because that's when the next set of projects would come in.

Depending on the movie's success, Kyle was willing to use his money to bump up the numbers if it had a poor showing at the box office. He planned to buy out multiple cinemas and allow people to view the movie for free for a week.

Imagine a full cinema for a week? The returns would be justified, but Kyle knew he could get into trouble now that his face was out there. There was a face to the name, unfortunately, but this was a good introduction.

There's nothing more appealing to the public than gossip, but Kyle had to focus on Ella. No, he had to see what kind of person her sister was. Whoever she was, she was a powerful woman. She inherited everything their parents worked for all their lives, and the fact that he was going to meet her today meant he would get a piece of that influence.

He was shown around the house, and the more rooms they explored, the more impressed Kyle became. He couldn't believe that one person got to experience all of this alone, but Kyle realized this was the sort of life he sought—a life where he had people do the mundane house chores, but not in a house as big as this.

They had a tennis court at the back, along with a golf course further back. It felt like a small city, and Kyle understood why security was needed.

It took a little over thirty minutes, but the fact that she wasn't done showing them around seemed ridiculous. But Kyle noticed she hadn't taken them to a wing of the house, a place he assumed to be where her parents slept.

It would be understandable why she wouldn't show them there, considering she hadn't processed it in a healthy manner. Kyle knew it was best to keep his mouth shut; there was no point in exacerbating the highly emotional situation.

"Lady Ella, it appears your sister has arrived," the butler said, appearing out of nowhere.

The fact that he wasn't following them around but knew exactly where to find her had Kyle in awe. There was no way this man had learned the act of teleportation, but Kyle was also surprised that they allowed her this much freedom. That was strange for someone supposedly exiled, or did that end with the passing of her parents?

That wouldn't make sense. There had to be a reason why she had this much freedom other than being kind to them. Ella's sister must have granted them access, but it was beginning to sound like their relationship wasn't as strained as Ella would have them believe.

A look at Ella's face changed that perspective. She looked like she had heard the worst news of her life, her hands trembling as if she were phasing out of existence.

"Woah! Calm down!" Kyle exclaimed, even Jane hadn't noticed this as quickly as Kyle because her attention was still on the butler.

"I-I'm fine," Ella tried to dismiss it, but her shaking only got worse. Kyle pulled her into an embrace.

Jane watched this, not knowing what to do herself, but Kyle somehow managed to do it.

"You aren't alone. Don't forget that… You don't have to carry that burden alone anymore," Kyle said, and Ella's trembling soon came to a halt.

Jane looked at Kyle, and for the first time, felt jealousy because Kyle could do what she couldn't. She had known Ella for so long, but Kyle was the one who learned how to comfort her.

Jane just watched as Ella wrapped her arms around him without much hesitation.

"What's going on?" Jane thought to herself. She didn't understand the feelings welling up inside her because this was something she had no problem with previously.

"Are you good?" Kyle asked, and Ella nodded before letting go of him.

Kyle looked at Jane, standing awkwardly, and realized she must be feeling left out. But he knew now wasn't the time to be patronizing.

"I'll be right back," Ella said.

"Do you want me to come with you?" Jane asked, making a conscious effort to still show up for her best friend.

"No, I think it's best if I do it alone. No, I have to do it alone," Ella said before leaving Jane and Kyle alone together.

"Hey, come here," Kyle called over to Jane. He knew she wouldn't want to be selfish and make the situation about her.

Jane walked closer to him, and Kyle wrapped his arm around her waist.

"We finally have some alone time, and I have to say, you look fucking stunning. I'm fighting everything in me not to kiss you," Kyle complimented her.

Jane tried to look away to hide her flustered face, but Kyle wasn't about to make it that easy. He leaned in and kissed her.

This made her mind go blank, and she forgot about what she was worried about just a second ago. Kyle gave her the reassurance she didn't know she needed.

"What kind of person is her sister?" Kyle casually asked Jane. She couldn't blame him for being curious.

"Intense, huh? So a bitch?" Kyle asked and Jane almost choked on her saliva.

She knew Kyle would get the message, but she wasn't eager to say it herself.

Jane had no interest in badmouthing her even though she disliked her for all the things she put Ella through but hate would be too hard a word.

Everyone had their story and Jane was the type of lady who chose to see the good in people even though that person caused her friend unimaginable trauma.

Ultimately, Ella was just a kid, and this was a result of bad parenting. Parents had to fill roles regardless of how busy they were, and they had failed at that.

"Is something wrong?" Jane asked upon noticing Kyle's silence but all Kyle did was smile at her.

"Everything is fine, do you think we will meet her?" Kyle asked.

"I think so, she would want to see us, wouldn't she?" Jane asked.

Ella first came into view but the beauty that came in right after made Kyle readjust his eyes.

"Wait…" Kyle thought to himself, he knew that he had heard that voice somewhere before and there was no doubt that the lady before him was the exact same woman he saw at the party.

The body and every other measurement were identical, Kyle knew this with certainty due to his Total Recall.

The woman's eyes settled on him but Kyle knew she didn't recognize him she didn't care enough to allow him to stick in her head.

"So who is this?" Cleopatra asked with a raised brow and this was his queue to introduce himself but Kyle realized that she knew his name.

There was no way she wouldn't put the pieces together if she heard that name.

This was no doubt the same woman and Kyle smiled at this response, he couldn't give who he was away.

"Jane…" Cleopatra acknowledged her presence and Jane nodded in her direction.

"I was surprised when I got the call that you wanted to see Mom and Dad," Cleopatra said but Ella knew that they couldn't respond to her triggers because once she gets under their skin, it is over.

Cleopatra had a knack for such things but Kyle couldn't believe his fortune.

He had met this woman before and had the advantage because she had no idea who he was.

She lay on the bed, her arms stretched on either side of the bed but she couldn't understand what was happening.

This would have been a lot easier if Klye had been honest with her about his past, she wouldn't need to take this deceptive route either.

She didn't want to do anything that would piss him off so she had to control herself.

She missed Kyle and the way he made her feel but he was a busy man and Ella's phone wasn't going through either.

Calista thought about calling it quits with her because she knew she was playing with fire.

Even though Kyle didn't tell her to stay away, her common sense told her this would be the right thing to do.

"What the hell am I doing?" Calista thought to herself but she soon heard a bang at the front door.

With the presence of a doorbell, this was a strange alternative to alert her of a potential visitor.

This only put her on guard, she wasn't about to risk it either and was ready to dial 911 right away should this person prove to be troublesome.

Calista cautiously walked to the door and noticed that the door was open.

"Did I forget to lock it!?" Calista thought to herself. This was something that happened every now and then because this was a low-crime neighbourhood.

"Whoever is here, leave now!" Calista warned. She had already dialed the police because she couldn't risk it.

If this was some sort of prank then she could play it off but either way, it was better to be safe rather than sorry.

"I don't think I would do that if I were you," A voice said behind her, Calista immediately turned around to see a lady standing behind her.

"You wanted to meet, here I am," The lady said and at approximately this moment, the 911 call went through.

Calista had to make sure she was the only one here before dropping that call.

"If you don't mind, I helped myself to some water," The lady said but she wasn't worried at all despite her having a phone in hand.

"You are here…" Calista said, hanging up the phone but she kept her distance.

"Well then, what do you want to know?" The lady asked with a smile on her face.

Aiysha had a decision to make but she couldn't push Kyle out of her mind.

She didn't know he was that big but this was something that made her nervous.

Aiysha knew she couldn't look at him the same either because that image was forever in her head.

She remained in the gym and saw Tasha doing what she did best, she presented herself to the other male members like she was some sort of cargo.

Aiysha didn't know how she was comfortable doing such a thing but there was something that her relationship allowed.

That was the freedom to explore, Aiysha knew she wasn't necessarily cheating because the boyfriend was aware of these things.

This was strange because it was something she didn't see herself doing, Aiysha knew the moment she let another man touch her.

It was over because the guilt would eat her up but that didn't mean the thought didn't cross her mind.

"Girl! How do you know him!?" Tasha sprung out of nowhere.

"Huh?" Aiysha was confused about what she meant because her presence had not only startled her but jumbled up her thoughts.

"That man, I thought he was going to pin me to the wall when he shut the door on me!" Tasha was so shameless when it came to talking about these things but it made Aiysha uncomfortable talking about Kyle this way.

"Hahaha, he has a girlfriend…" Aiysha warned Tasha.

"Even better," Tasha licked her lips, Aiysha knew she might have made a mistake because Kyle was going to keep coming here which meant that Tasha would have her chance.

"I felt it…" Tasha muttered under her breath, clasping her thighs shut.

"Are you alright?" Tasha asked and Aiysha quickly excused herself.

"Y-You talk too much girl!" Aiysha said before rushing to the locker room, she had no idea what was going on with her but her body was responding in a way she didn't want it to.

Chapter 191 - 191: A Harmless Lie?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Kyle met with Cleopatra but she didn't recognize him and ignored his existence.

She focused solely on Jane and Ella, speaking to them as they walked around the house.

Kyle felt like a fourth wheel but didn't mind this because his eyes were scouting the building carefully, retaining every single detail within.

Ella and Jane were tense. They were scared of Cleopatra, but how could someone so small command such intimidation?

"Cleo, where are Mom and Dad?" Ella asked the all-important question, but Cleopatra stopped in her tracks.

"They are dead, you might have forgotten seeing as you missed their funeral," Cleopatra mocked her.

Kyle noticed that Ella didn't speak out of line and despite Cleopatra being a bitch, Ella was docile around her older sister.

"Of course you would. The daughter who couldn't bear the shame, so she ran away like a coward," Cleopatra said, and Kyle noticed that neither Ella nor Jane was saying anything to her.

He expected the woman to be a lot more level-headed but she was a rude lady. Kyle didn't care much about Ella being insulted because this was her family.

If she chose to be verbally abused, it was on her because she was a grown woman who was accountable for her actions.

Kyle knew trauma had a role to play but for how long could one blame trauma for their unwillingness to overcome it?

That was her responsibility, this was her journey and Kyle wasn't going to intervene.

The only reason he would was if this woman dared to insult Jane.

He would have no choice but to step in and put an immediate stop to it.

"Jane, why do you follow her around?" Cleopatra asked and Kyle's eyes glared at her.

"You must be just as use-!" Cleopatra was interrupted.

"What did you just say?" Cleopatra asked but Kyle made sure to alter his voice slightly so he wouldn't sound the same.

If it did, there was a chance that she would recognize who he was and that was something that Kyle couldn't risk.

In truth, there was always that 1% that Cleopatra wasn't who he thought she was even though that was unlikely but not impossible.

Kyle kept that possibility at the back of his mind and knew he had to show this woman, whoever she was, that she had to watch her mouth.

"For someone that speaks so much, I would think your ears would work just as well as your mouth," Kyle said, stepping in front of both Ella and Jane.

"I don't know you but I will not allow you to disrespect these two any further. If that is what you wish to do, we will leave," Kyle said.

He knew that this woman had no reason to allow them inside the mansion.

If it was within her power, she would have turned down Ella so there had to be a deeper reason why they were here.

If this was the case then there was no way that Cleopatra would throw them out of the house simply because Kyle spoke back.

This was what Kyle banked on because he was aware that it was up to him to make a strong first impression.

Jane subtly smiled alongside Ella, this was the protection they had craved for so long.

Kyle was surprised she didn't recognize him in all honesty, this must be a Superman/Kent Clark moment.

It was her perception because he had identical features but maybe it was because the mask had hidden the green tinges mixed into his hair that made it difficult.

Kyle didn't care about this but he wasn't backing down either, he was going to stand his ground.

"Well? Are you going to be more respectful?" Kyle asked to which she rolled her eyes in annoyance.

"You have some guts I give you that. It looks like my sister is now dating someone who has a spine for a change," Cleopatra said but everyone was confused.

However, they knew that this would work in their favour if her older sister thought they were dating as there was a tone of respect.

The fact that Ella didn't shoot down this notion right away was something to note as well.

Kyle knew he had to put on the performance of his life without crossing any boundaries at the same time which would be difficult to do if he wanted to make this convincing.

He could see the slight down in Cleopatra's eyes and this made him put his hand around Ella's waist.

"Do you want us to leave? I expect the next word out of your mouth to be an apology," Kyle said sternly, looking her right in the eyes.

He could tell that Cleopatra was suspicious of him but he didn't know if this was because she found him familiar or due to his relationship with her sister.

Cleopatra kissed her teeth and turned around, Kyle already knew that was the best he was going to get but Ella looked up at him with a slight smile.

This took her back to the restaurant where he protected her as well when she felt awkward.

"To think you would go as far as hiring an actor to play boyfriend is quite sad…" Cleopatra scoffed at the ridiculousness.

"... And the only reason I haven't kicked you out is because there is something that Mom and dad wanted me to give you," Cleopatra added.

Jane and Ella were aware that she would look down on Ella even more if she found out that Kyle wasn't her boyfriend but the good news was that they could all hear the uncertainty in her voice.

This meant that she wasn't certain herself if this was true or false but what had Ella's attention was the fact that her parents had left something for her.

"What?" Ella blurted out involuntarily because Cleopatra had told her something different when they met at the hospital.

She was certain her parents wouldn't want her to be at their funeral but this would explain why Cleopatra let her in.

It wasn't because she suddenly had a heart but rather, due to this which made a lot more sense.

"That is right, follow me," Cleopatra said and Ella was reluctant because she didn't know what her sister was up to.

"Only you, if I may add," Cleopatra reinforced, looking at Kyle with disgust.

Kyle sighed as he removed his hand from her waist to allow her to go with her sister.

Cleopatra had her eyes on him before looking at Jane and right then, a grin creased her face as if she had discovered something. However, this was an act that escaped Kyle's gaze because his attention shifted from Ella to Jane for a split second.

Calista was with a strange woman in her house claiming to be Kyle's sister.

She was quite the beauty with a prominent scar just below her cheek but it looked like one gotten over the years because it was barely visible.

The fact that she was followed already made this a dangerous situation because she now knew where she lived.

The lady reached for her purse and rummaged through it but Calista was on guard because she had no idea what it was she was looking for.

She had stalked her already, broken into her home so there was no telling what she was capable of. Calista was endangering herself but it was a risk she hoped was worth it.

Calista kept an eye on her as she carefully looked at the picture but her jaws dropped the moment she saw who was in it.

It was Kyle, there was no doubt that this was him but there was something off-putting about the look in his eyes. Calista looked in them but saw no one, they were empty, very similar to the man she met that day.

"Do you need any more evidence?" The lady asked but Calista was hesitant, returning the picture.

"What is your name?" Calista asked hesitantly only for the lady to smile as she said.

"Kelly,"

Chapter 192 - 192: A Night To Forget?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Ella was alone with Cleopatra, and she noticed her sister's demeanor had changed despite Cleopatra's best attempts to hide it. She led her into a room that their parents had kept off-limits to them as kids. They weren't allowed anywhere near it, so it wasn't an exaggeration to say that Ella had never been in it. This was because she left the house at an early age and had never been considered part of the family. Naturally, they allowed Cleopatra in once they deemed her old enough to handle herself in such a situation.

She was the heir to everything they had worked so hard in life for but it was a pity that they didn't live long enough to see Cleopatra extend their bloodline with a husband and a child.

"Something is wrong," Ella thought to herself as she noticed little jars scattered around the room with names attached. She could barely make out the names because the room was barely illuminated, but she noticed that the air in here was chilly. For an average citizen, this would run their bills through the roof, but not to Cleopatra. Money was the last thing on her mind because she had enough to last multiple lifetimes.

However, this was a room where her family line for centuries had been cremated and kept inside but Ella was oblivious to this fact.

"Here it is…" Cleopatra said. Ella could hear her voice crack slightly, but her older sister never once turned to look at her. Ella stepped forward and noticed her parents' names on two separate jars. The anger she carried toward them melted at that very moment. She thought that this place was safe, even after she got her car they didn't snoop the way they did but that changed the moment she found out that she could potentially be a famous person.

It was surreal having a neighbour turned celebrity living right next to them so in that sense, one couldn't blame them for their reaction.

Even if it did cross certain boundaries that shouldn't be crossed.

Calista pressed the doorbell and could hear rapid movements within the house followed by whispers.

The door finally swung open to welcome her and it was a giddy lady who couldn't contain her smile even though she was aware of why Calista was at her front door.

"What can I do for you, Miss Wave?" The neighbor was filled with excitement which made it harder for Calista to maintain her rage.

For the first time, she felt like someone important, a celebrity because the way this older woman looked at her was one of admiration, not contempt.

"I wanted to ask if you could take down the picture you posted, it is sending the wrong message," Calista got right to the point even though her voice had softened.

"I'm afraid it is too late for that, it is already viral!" The lady responded.

Calista could tell this was her first viral tweet so the dopamine she got from that must still be in her system.

But she was right, there was no point doing this if it was already viral, however, this gave Calista an idea.

"Alright… How about a favour?" Calista said and of course, who would turn down a request from a 'celebrity'?

Ella had gone through Jane's phone and couldn't get her mind off what she saw, it kept replaying in her head over and over again.

She never saw Kyle in that light but that picture did something to her brain chemistry because now, that was all she thought about.

Jane was still sleeping and Ella wondered if she should check for more of these things on her phone.

She already saw this so what harm could come from searching for others? It wasn't like he was naked, to begin with so it should be fine.

She reached for her phone once again but Jane turned in her sleep before eventually opening her eyes.

Ella had retracted her hands fast enough to evade Jane's gaze.

"Welcome back to the land of the living," Ella greeted her friend, who was still somewhat dazed.

"Do we still have more food?" Jane asked, sounding like she was half-asleep.

"You can't possibly be serious!" Ella barked at her friend but Jane just stared at her blankly before returning to sleep.

Chapter 216 - 216: I Have A Problem. [FIXED!]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Kyle could feel a panic attack sneaking up on him despite being on the wheel, every emotion he had suppressed came rushing to the surface.

"What the hell was that?" Kyle thought to himself, speeding away on the road.

He couldn't take his foot off the gas pedal; it felt like if he did, his problems would catch up with him.

Kyle's vision became blurry shortly after, his breathing became heavier and before he knew it, his limbs tensed up.

Kyle hit the brakes suddenly and the car screeched to a halt, nearly crashing into another vehicle but Kyle managed to avoid it at the last second.

"I need some help," Kyle thought to himself, the fact that he didn't crash into the car showed his superior skills.

Kyle was breathing heavily and the car he nearly ran into was shouting at him but the voice was drowned out.

Kyle was glad that he managed to escape the accident unharmed but it showed that he had a problem on a much deeper level.

Kyle drove away without engaging or saying a word because he knew that things would escalate if he did engage.

Kyle left the vicinity right away but the panic attack had somewhat subsided, it wasn't as bad as it previously was but Kyle was feeling the effects.

He finally realized that he needed help and there were trained professionals for these things.

Kyle found himself in a therapist's office but it wasn't on the cheap end either.

But this was what he wanted because as open-minded as Kyle was, he didn't want anyone to know that he was seeing someone or there was a chance that they would treat him differently.

"Do you have an appointment, sir?" The secretary asked but Kyle knew that there was nothing money couldn't do.

If there wasn't a slot open then he would simply buy one but Kyle was out of it because he was trembling physically.

The secretary noticed this and looked worried because it looked like this man needed a hospital, not a therapist.

Kyle noticed that there was no one else here so the therapist couldn't be all that busy.

This was a dying profession and the internet made it more accessible as it no longer required physical presence.

Zoom would do even though it was preferred for them to be there in person because the energy would be different and it would help the therapist read them a lot better.

"Sir, do you need a doctor?" The lady behind the desk but Kyle rolled his eyes in irritation.

"I cannot, I'm sorry… Unless you made an appointment," The lady responded.

Kyle knew she was just doing her job and this wasn't necessarily in her power to begin with.

"How about $50,000?" Kyle asked and the lady's attention was piqued at that moment.

She was wondering why he was offering that sort of money, to begin with when there were cheaper therapists around.

However, this was a fleeting interest because what happened next surprised Kyle as she turned it down, this not only hinted at her being paid well but she didn't show any shock at being offered this amount.

"As I said, it is out of my hand, sir," The lady responded and Kyle accepted his fate.

He had to wait and that was what he intended to do.

He couldn't let just anyone in his mind, it had to be the best of the best.

Cassandra had tried to call Kyle multiple times but there was no answer at the other end, it went right to voicemail and this had her worried.

It was gentle, Kyle wasn't scared of him, he was scared of himself.

Cassandra knew it was important to give him some space to sort himself out but also wanted him to know that he didn't have to do it alone because she was there for him whenever he needed.

Something told her that Kyle wasn't used to having people to rely on so he carried the entire weight on his shoulder.

No one should have to face the world alone, that is no way to live a life and Cassandra found herself wanting to heal parts of Kyle that she didn't break.

But this would only make it harder for her to let go, Cassandra wasn't stupid and was aware that all of this could come to an end at any given moment.

It could be when she grows older or stops looking the way she did because beauty fades over time.

Only a few women maintain their beauty in their older years or age 'gracefully' as they say.

The reality is old age comes for all and she was already in her thirties, she had a few good years on her before the wrinkles came into play.

Cassandra wasn't thinking that far ahead because if anything, it was a blessing to have Kyle in her life right now.

The present had to be utilized but why was she developing feelings for him despite knowing all of this?

Cassandra wondered if it was her loneliness getting the best of her but that couldn't be the case, if it was sex she sought it was easy for her to get that.

She stared at her phone, waiting for a response from Kyle, that was the only thing on her mind right now and who could blame her?

Cassandra's phone rang but it was from the wrong person, it was from Justin but she had no plans of picking up the phone regardless.

This wasn't something she had any interest in despite being well aware that this might be important.

She resisted the urge and the call turned into a missed one but a text message came in shortly after.

Cassandra felt nothing for him but this didn't mean he stopped being the father of her child.

She was aware that she had to remain in contact with him on the off-chance that this case didn't go as planned.

She read the message and it was Justin asking for her back, this was a shock considering he was the one that pushed for a divorce.

He acted like she made him miserable and it was because of her that he wasn't happy in the marriage but Cassandra was aware that things were going according to plan.

Cassandra was disgusted by this message and it took everything in her not to rain curses at him.

However, she knew how she reacted to this could be used against her.

Justin had used their kid as a means with the cliche.

"If not for me, then do it for our child,"

Cassandra knew this wasn't genuine, his lawyer must have told him to phrase it this way because this could act as potential evidence.

This was a good response because it couldn't be misinterpreted.

He was now the recipient of those feelings and this meant she could look at things a lot more logically when it came to her ex-husband.

But Cassandra soon heard a knock at the door, she knew who it was without answering.

She had already changed the locks pending the verdict so Justin couldn't enter the house whenever he pleased.

The woman could tell from his eyes that he was desperate.

Chapter 219 - 219: Therapy Begins! [FIXED 1/2/25!]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

There was a time in Kyle's life when all he wanted was love, he wanted to be normal but life had other plans for him.

It started right from his birth, Kyle always felt like there was something wrong with him, a feeling he couldn't shake because no matter how hard he tried, he felt like a broken vessel.

The first moment he took his breath, his mother didn't feel happiness or relief, she felt disgust.

She couldn't believe that he looked a lot more like his father despite spending 9 months in her womb.

It was unfair and this was how Kyle had unknowingly inherited his mother's rage.

Kyle was never an expressive child, all he ever wanted was to gain his mother's approval.

He wondered if being harmed by her was her way of loving him, which in turn gave him a distorted perception of love.

But were his memories accurate? Kyle wasn't sure because most of the things he recalled, he didn't remember them happening.

It felt wrong, it felt false, and he didn't have his Total Recall either to help him navigate through this.

It has been proven that people's memories are the least reliable source of information because they can only process that information through a single lens.

"Mama, look what I made!" A five-year-old boy, dawned in dirty clothing said to his mother, looking up at her with hopeful expectations.

The mother had a bottle of liquor right in front of her and didn't pay him much attention, Kyle got up to his feet and walked towards her.

He had a bright smile on his face because he made it for her and only when he was a few feet from her did she acknowledge his presence.

"Oh… You are still here," The mother said in a dismissive tone but she looked at the piece of paper he held up.

"What is that?" His mother questioned.

"That is me, you, and papa!" Kyle was filled with excitement but the look on his mother's face contorted into something he couldn't recognize.

"What did you just say?" His mother was infuriated but Kyle noticed this right away however he didn't back up.

"It is me, you and pa-!" Before Kyle could finish his sentence, a shattering bottle echoed.

His mother had thrown it across the room in a fit of rage but the truth was, she narrowly missed her target. And that target was Kyle.

This was due to the alcohol in her system but Kyle had a wide smile on his face after his mother reacted this way.

"She liked it!" Kyle thought to himself, violence was his form of love and the more violent the reaction, the more he thought she liked it.

Kyle always kept to himself so he didn't have much experience of the outside world but this also included friends.

He didn't have an idea of how dysfunctional his family was because, despite his mother's rage, she still looked after him or rather, forced him to learn to look after himself.

A survival skill that he was grateful for.

But it was impossible for a child that age not to have a single relationship outside of his home and this was how Kyle began to interact with a much older man.

This man was well in his thirties but he was a bum, he was the definition of bad life choices.

However, this man was the man who taught Kyle how to survive and during that time, Kyle was exposed to some violent things that no one should see, more so his age.

But it was fine because this was "love" and this further shaped his mind.

However, it all changed one day…

Wait? What changed?

-

Kyle was brought back to reality after his brain became scrambled, he couldn't recall what happened next which was strange.

It was like there was a lapse in his memory and Susanna instantly knew whatever happened next must have been traumatic enough for him to suppress it.

This was good progress and Kyle appreciated the look in her eyes, it wasn't one of pity.

Kyle hated being pitied because this was a story he told not to get sympathy but to be understood.

"I have taken something away from your story. You hold no remorse for your mother and you barely mentioned your father in it," Susanna pointed out, writing something in her note.

"And this man that raised yo-!" Susanna was interrupted by Kyle.

"He didn't raise me. He was just some homeless junkie on the road that would do anything to get his fix," Kyle corrected her.

"My mother raised me…" Kyle added.

Susanna noted this and realized that Kyle must have developed some form of Stockholm syndrome, which he had no control over.

"I see. Tell me more about him," Susanna knew she had to know just how much influence he had over him.

And how deep he managed to dig his claws into Kyle. At the beginning of the story, Susanna thought that his mother was the one who brought him here but saw something that Kyle, ironically, failed to see.

Kyle had only spoken highly about his mother showing his complicated feelings towards her but Susanna was aware that she couldn't trust the foundation he was laying. Kyle couldn't as well, that was why he was here for to help him connect the missing threads.

Whatever he did with this man was the reason why his memories were "false"

"I saw him at a bar before you say anything. No, I didn't go there to drink but to get something for my mother…" Kyle paused with a smile on his face.

"Do you know she used to call alcohol medicine? Kyle, help mommy to get her medicine," Kyle said but the fact that the bar sold drinks to a child showed just how lacking laws were wherever he lived.

"Did your mother ever meet your friend?" Susanno asked, bringing his mother into the conversation because Kyle seemed fixated on her.

Susanna knew the most effective way to get to the bottom of it would be to make him lower his guard.

"I don't think they did… No, I can't remember," Kyle looked lost, trying his hardest to answer this question.

"You presented this man as an insignificant part of your story but you only began to repress your memories after you met him," Susanna pointed out.

Kyle could see where she was coming from because it was true that he did that.

"You're right," Kyle admitted, not knowing what else to say because he needed help.

To get help, he needed to be honest.

"Did he make you do anything you didn't want to do?" Susanna asked but once again, Kyle had no answer for this.

"No… Or at least I don't think so," Kyle responded.

"Kyle, what kind of things did the man make you witness," Susanna asked and this was where Kyle's face had a tinge of worry.

"Whatever I say here is confidential, right?" Kyle made sure this was the case and Susanna nodded gently.

"I know he robbed and beat up people but as I grew up, I realized he did a lot worse," Kyle confessed but this was proof that he was oblivious to most things going on around him at the time making him innocent by default.

Susanna knew what he meant without him having to elaborate but she was curious why Kyle used past tense to describe his mom.

That could only mean one thing, she had died but did this man have anything to do with it?

Is that why Kyle can't recall his influence in his life?

Susanna knew this was an assumption on her end but it would make sense.

From what she had heard, Kyle's mother was neglectful but if Kyle survived until that age, she must have been taking care of him to make sure he didn't die when it would have been a lot easier to abandon him.

"Kyle, what happened to your mother? Can you tell me?" Susanna asked and Kyle caught on to what she was implying due to the series of questions.

"She killed herself," Kyle responded but there was no feeling in his voice.

That was to be expected as he had years to get over it so the reaction to his mother's suicide wouldn't be a big deal.

"I see and did the autopsy confirm that?" Susanna asked, looking at Kyle but once again, there was no judgement.

"Autopsy? We were poor, who cares if we die," Kyle said with a mocking chuckle at the end of the sentence.

However, this confirmed that Kyle had no way of knowing if this was a suicide and the reason his mind had such huge gaps was because the truth might be directly linked to him.

"Love is violence," This was something he had said on multiple occasions so how would he show his mother he loved her?

Chapter 220 - 220: Kyle Unravels. [FIXED 6/2/25!]
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

There was no one who knew what Kyle was going through except Cassandra, she had a rough idea of what Kyle was going through but now she couldn't stop thinking about him.

That single event made it impossible to get him out of her mind and this also meant that it increased her emotional attachment to him.

That was how feeling worked, you could have a random dream about someone you never truly noticed in your daily life and that dream would serve as the catalyst for interest.

Once the mind fixates on something, there is no way to escape it.

"There is something off about Kyle," Cassandra thought to herself but it was beginning to show that Kyle's actions were affecting his various relationships in different ways.

All the women in his life had attained a level of curiosity but this was normal when it came to a potential mate.

Cassandra knew that Kyle was rich but knew nothing about him except what he had told her.

She also wondered if he was in a dangerous line of work because the way he casually announced that he was stabbed was strange.

It was almost like it was a normal occurrence and for her son's safety, she needed to know what kind of man she was letting in her life.

Even though the benefits that came with it were something she couldn't live without.

She would have lost the case long ago if Kyle hadn't intervened and was aware that this was her mind-spirited.

Her thoughts, in no way, shaped reality and she knew this much but there was nothing she had asked Kyle that he didn't answer.

She simply hasn't been asking the right questions but that was about to change.

Cassandra was focused on the case but Justin was at her door, knocking even though she had no intention of opening the door for him because she was aware that he was up to no good.

Cassandra knew she had enough money to get a new house but she wanted this case to be over before making any drastic decision.

If she got full custody, she planned to disappear without a trace and make it impossible for Justin ever to find her.

Suddenly, the knocking stopped and this went on for a couple of minutes.

"Has he gone?" Cassandra was hopeful and decided to confirm if this was the case.

She peeped through the window and there was no sign of her ex-husband.

Cassandra knew she had to be careful because Justin could get a little crazy if he didn't get what he wanted.

But his face suddenly popped up right in front of the window, startling Cassandra.

"Cassey… Please…" Justin pleaded and Cassandra could see the desperation in his eyes.

But that soft spot she had for him that he had exploited throughout their marriage was gone.

All she felt was disgust looking at him right now, wondering what it was she found attractive about this man in front of her.

"Get out, Justin," Cassandra and she could tell that Justin was shocked by the words coming out of her mouth.

It wasn't laced with anger, it was soft spoken but it was her eyes, those were the most telling signs.

There were no emotions behind them and Justin smiled to himself, he didn't know if he should be proud of her for breaking the shackles he had around her or feel sad that he could no longer exploit her.

"Is this the end, Cassey?" Justin asked but hearing him calling her made her recoil.

"Don't call me that… Don't ever call me that again," Cassandra responded before shutting the blinds.

A smile crept up on her face because she had regained the power she lost during her time with him.

-

Kyle was done with his therapy session and only then realised how much he needed it.

He felt vulnerable, Kyle didn't like it because of his memories.

That woman made him doubt them with her subtle body language change but there was one thing she didn't do and that was make him feel judged.

Paying a stranger to listen to his problems felt weird, how lonely was such a life especially when he had people that would gladly do it for free.

But it wasn't their job to be his emotional tampon and that was why people like these existed in the first place.

People always make that mistake and this creates pressure in their relationships because it forces people to show up in ways they aren't capable of.

The last thing Kyle needed was to feel like a liability and Susanna made sure this was the case.

Kyle was on his way home as he needed to enjoy his newfound freedom.

Jane was out of the house so he had it all to himself, cohabitation was a difficult thing and Kyle liked Jane's presence but needed that alone time to clear his head.

This was something he couldn't do as long as she remained in his home.

Kyle arrived at his house shortly, he wondered if Nate was able to convince the woman to join their cause.

There was only so much an OF model could do once it was out there in the world.

It would always come back to bite them in the ass as this would put them at a disadvantage when job hunting in the everyday world.

Background checks were one of those things that became a requirement overtime especially if the place of work was anywhere respectable.

Kyle heaved a sigh of relief and arrived at his place in no time.

He wondered if Jones was alright because it was unusual for him to be quiet for this long.

He hadn't even thanked him and knowing Jones, there was a low chance he had forgotten.

However, Kyle didn't need to wait long because he saw Jones leaving the building just as he pulled up.

Jones' head was buried in his phone so he didn't see Kyle.

Kyle walked up to Jones before he could get into his car and whispered.

"You're welcome,"

This startled the older man and Jones nearly stumbling was proof of this.

Kyle reached to catch him so he wouldn't hurt himself.

"Don't touch me!" Jones barked at him but Kyle couldn't tell if he was messing around or not.

"Are you okay? It is me," Kyle told him wondering if Jones didn't get a good look at him but with a single look into his eyes, he could see Jones' anger directed at him.

Kyle knew there was only one thing that could get a man like this.

"I see. She told him," Kyle said with a hidden smile on his face because this single act showed that there was not only hope for Jones' marriage but that Aiysha was truly a good woman.

"You're disgusting, Kyle," Jones blurted out but for some reason, Kyle wasn't offended because Jones spoke the truth.

"Ha! No lies there but it isn't what it looks like," Kyle confessed.

There were multiple times where he could have taken advantage of Aiysha but kept himself in check because of the respect he had for Jones.

And it was ironic that Jones was reacting this way considering he was the one that cheated in this marriage. Not that Kyle was judging as he knew the circumstance of their marriage, it wasn't based on love but a lack of option on Jones' part.

"You find that funny!?" Jones barked but Kyle remained calm.

This reaction was valid but this was the first time he had seen Jones get out of character.

It was like his mask had dropped and Kyle got a closer glimpse at a side of him he kept under wraps.

"Your anger is justified, I did cross a line and I would be lying if I said I didn't have a dirty thought or two…" Kyle confessed but Jones could tell this man didn't regret his actions.

"However, I did not act on those thoughts…" Kyle clarified.

"What the fuck is wrong with you man? THAT'S MY FUCKING WIFE!" Jones barked at Kyle.

"... And she still is," Kyle responded.

Jones didn't know how to feel about this and before he knew it, his fist found Kyle's jaw.

The punch packed enough force to send Kyle tumbling to the ground.

Jones instantly regretted his action, looking at his fist in disbelief as the people around began to take note of the commotion.

He expected Kyle to retaliate but all Kyle did was smile at him.

"That was a nice punch…" Kyle's tone was indifferent but Jones knew the punch might have been too much however he wasn't thinking straight.

Jones hopped in his car and drove off, leaving Kyle to pick himself off the ground.

Kyle didn't look particularly worried like he expected this to happen.

He just stared blankly at the faraway car.

"Love is violent, huh?" Kyle muttered under his breath with a smile.

He walked past them and into the building like nothing had happened.

But Jones' punch had split his lips open and Kyle could taste his blood.

The truth was, Aiysha never rejected him because he didn't make a move on her.

However, he wasn't going to let Jones know.

The fact that Aiysha told him was good enough for Kyle.

Kyle took it into his own hands to test her after Jones had shown concerns of his wife cheating and this was why he went from avoiding her.

Kyle realized that now was the best time to leave this building. He should have done that long ago because if he did he wouldn't be in this situation.

Kyle walked back to his apartment but the moment he reached the doorsteps of Jones.

He contemplated confronting Aiysha but she did nothing wrong. If anything, she had earned his respect but Kyle knew this gave Jones the security in his marriage that he was looking for.

If Aiysha could reject a handsome billionaire like himself the Jones had nothing to worry about.

In Kyle's twisted mind, he was helping out a friend and his job was done.

Kyle walked into his apartment and collapsed on his bed.

The mental strain on his mind was something that he didn't anticipate because Kyle had doubts about everything that occurred.

He realized that there was something he wasn't seeing but this couldn't deter him from his goals.

That would be a miracle if it did as it would cure all of his problems and he might not need the therapy but the system had no form of individuality.

It couldn't answer questions either so it wasn't useful when it came to information as it had a fixed set of responses.

Kyle hated this and what Susanna was insinuating was something he never entertained until now.

If he couldn't recall the role that man played in his life or why he repressed memories related to him, he couldn't trust the circumstances of his mother's murder.

"I have to go back…" Kyle muttered under his breath. He had to revisit his past.

He couldn't sleep and quickly got up from his bed. Kyle knew it would be foolish to drive his car in that neighbourhood as he would be robbed in a matter of minutes.

If anything, the place had gotten worse over the years but everything began with Clarence.

The moment Kyle killed him, there was something violent in him that was trying its best to get to the surface.

Kyle hopped back outside but while outside, he ran into Aiysha.

She couldn't even look him in the eyes because she was aware of their scuffle.

Of course she was, she watched it through the window but Kyle didn't mind it.

All Jones did was defend his marriage like a man should do and Kyle didn't hold that against him.

Kyle, however, knew it was time to end whatever relationship he had with his wife and if need be, with Jones.

Kyle walked past her without saying a word but his body language was mostly unchanged, showing he wasn't affected by the situation.

Kyle needed some fresh air and knew that the chemistry with the only man he could call a friend had changed in the blink of an eye.

She didn't try to deflect because she knew that her intentions weren't in Kyle's best interest.

But Kyle didn't say another word and that was the end of the conversation because he hung up.

This was a common theme with elderly people but just because they were older, didn't mean they were better suited to face life.

But this was a stupid saying nonetheless, Kyle wasn't going to live a miserable life just to make the women in his life happy.

"Can you please keep quiet?" These were words Kyle wanted to say but couldn't.

Chapter 222 - 222: WE ARE BACK!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Kyle was dropped off at his destination and he gave the man a tip with a good review because in as much as he didn't like the old man's company.

This was the sad reality of life, you didn't know anyone's situation and Kyle wasn't going to let his mood affect someone's source of income.

The moment he stepped out of the car, the stench that invaded his nostrils reminded him of death, it was filthy.

Kyle couldn't believe this place hadn't been torn down but that would be impossible because even if it was crime infested, it was still a place where people lived.

"Home, sweet home," Kyle muttered to himself, the fact that he had to walk a fair distance before he could enter this city was proof of its high crime rate.

Kyle also made sure not to wear anything flashy because it would be stripped away from him in no time but there was something that he noticed.

There were a lot more homeless people around, this also included children but they weren't just homeless but malnourished as well.

"What the hell is happening here?" Kyle thought to himself but before he could take another step. He felt a light tug on his trouser, Kyle looked down and saw a child with a bowl in hand.

"M-Money.." The child said but Kyle knew this tactic, they must have perceived he was new here but there are certain things that never change.

If Kyle was to give money, then a couple of people would trail him and rob him of everything else he might have.

Kyle sighed before taking out a $5 bill and handing it to the child. He knew all eyes were on him but he lived here before so he knew how to get out of these sorts of situations.

Kyle proceeded to get out a lot of coins before tossing it up in the air and he was swarmed in mere seconds but this allowed him to slip out of sight during the commotion.

Evading potential criminals that were just observing him from the shadows.

It wasn't much but Kyle knew that this was enough to get them through the next couple of days.

Kyle was in good physical condition so this meant that his little sprint didn't take the wind out of him.

But this meant that he had to take a little detour on the off-chance he was being followed.

Kyle hated this place but this was where he grew up and the place that held the secret in rediscovering who he was.

However, Kyle overestimated his ability because the place he remembered was in the past.

The 'safe' corners were no longer safe, this was a haven for criminals and Kyle had wandered right at the center.

"Oi…" A cold voice rang out behind him but Kyle wasn't scared even though he knew the chances he was standing in front of a killer was high.

"Empty your pockets you little shit!" The man barked and it was followed by a swift blow to the back of Kyle's head that sent him tumbling to the ground.

Kyle turned around and noticed this man was severely underweight but he could also see marks on his arms that indicated he was a junkie.

Kyle kicked him off easily because of the difference in weight class before punching him across the face.

He was only here for a couple of minutes and was already involved in a life-or-death situation.

But there was something that snapped in Kyle the moment his fist connected with this man's face.

All the stress and frustration that had accumulated was released onto this man, his hand pummeling away at his face.

Kyle remembered how powerless Clarence made him feel and he was no longer going to live that way but fortunately for Kyle, he came to it before he could kill the man.

Even though he had rearranged his facial structure, Kyle noticed his fist had a few bruises as well.

"Maybe coming here wasn't such a good idea," Kyle thought to himself, crouched over an unconscious bum covered in his blood.

Isabella welcomed the signing of a proficient talent because she knew this would boost their social media presence and online attention.

And it was normal for the big dogs to consume the little dogs before they could become threats to them.

This would set them up for a lifetime but money was something she noticed wasn't a problem for Kyle but his influence was limited.

This was time-sensitive but Kyle had trusted her to run it how she wanted, didn't that include this sort of thing?

"You are…!"

Chapter 223 - 223: Destined Encounter Or...?

Kyle got back up and his mind was clearer but soon realized he was more damaged than he thought.

This was what he couldn't risk because if there was something that could implicate him.

Kyle had to bury it, he looked at the broken man beneath and thought about calling an ambulance but they wouldn't come.

Kyle sighed, realizing he couldn't leave this man like this because he was the one responsible for this.

He was trying to survive even if it meant harming other people, this has always been how the world worked.

Survival of the fittest doesn't always have to be violent, it could be something as simple as social discrimination where the poor do not have the same opportunities as the rich.

There was so much wrong in this world but this was something that Kyle always knew.

He experienced this harsh reality at an age no one should have to.

Kyle leaned over the unconscious man and elevated his head with his ragged shirt, staining his hand in the process.

However, Kyle didn't care about this because, for some reason, he wanted to make sure this man was going to be okay.

He looked at the damage he did to his face, there was no way this man wouldn't need medical attention.

Kyle came here for a mission but he was occupied with something else, he didn't understand why this was happening but knew he couldn't beat everyone that attacked him or he would have a case on his hands.

Kyle's wound might have healed but he felt the scar throbbing, like his body was calling for something.

Kyle looked over his shoulder and assumed a defensive stance but what he saw made his heart drop.

It was the same child he had seen previously, the one he handed a note but Kyle noticed that she had bruises on her body she didn't have previously.

They must have tried to take the money from her. Of course, they did but Kyle noticed in her little clenched fist that she still had the dollar note he gave to her.

"DON'T HURT BIG BROTHER!" The girl screamed at the top of her lungs before blindly charging at Kyle.

If Kyle was a bad man, there was no way a child was saving this man before him if he did intend to harm him but Kyle realized that he might be the bad person in this story.

Kyle took a step away from him as the child acted as a barricade between her and her older brother.

The tears in her eyes showed Kyle that this child was scared, the way she trembled with every breath.

Kyle quickly placed his hands behind his back to hide his fists, he couldn't let her see it.

"I met him like this. You see that," Kyle pointed at the cloth that kept his head elevated.

The way he positioned his hand made it impossible to see his knuckles.

"I did that, your brother needs medical attention," Kyle's voice was soft and this was enough to convince the child that he wasn't a threat.

She lowered her hand that had a small knife in it and Kyle heaved a sigh of relief.

He needed to gain her trust because there was no way that he was going to use force to subdue her.

"Can you help?" The girl stuttered but Kyle shook his head.

"But doctors can," Kyle responded but the girl looked confused hearing this word.

"Doctors?" She asked with a confused look on her face like this word was alien.

Kyle knew she was illiterate but there are certain words that people knew regardless of their educational background.

"Yes, people that fix others," Kyle responded with a raised brow and the look on the girl's face changed, the excitement in her eyes telling a different story.

She ran to her brother, completely ignoring Kyle like she didn't just perceive him as a threat moments ago.

"You hear that, brother!" The girl was filled with excitement and Kyle remembered that she was a child, no more than 3-4 years old.

"You're going to be okay!" The girl said but the brother was unresponsive, this sight made Kyle feel guilty which was strange considering he hadn't felt much remorse throughout his life.

"Your brother is having a little nap," Kyle responded, trying his best to make her feel safe.

Kyle could have left but found himself rooted in place, he couldn't leave the man like this especially now that he confirmed he had a younger sister.

What were the chances of that happening? It was almost like the universe brought him here for this reason.

"Can the doctors fix Mama and Papa?" The kid asked but Kyle raised a brow in confusion.

"Your parents? Where are they?" Kyle asked.

"They are sleeping but big brother put them in the ground so they don't get cold," The girl said.

"I see…" Kyle responded. He had no intention of breaking her illusion but there was no doubt that their parents were dead.

"What is your name?" Kyle asked the child which prompted her to turn around.

"Jasmine," The kid responded and Kyle knew that he would have to introduce himself as well to break any sort of ice.

"I'm Kyle, nice to meet you, Jasmine," Kyle greeted her with a smile to which the kid returned.

There was nothing to be done at this point because Kyle still planned to return to his 'home' but he couldn't carry this man with him so what was the next course of action?

"Jasmine, I will need you to stay with your brother until I return, can you do that?" Kyle asked and Jasmine nodded.

"Are you going to bring the doctors?" The clueless child asked.

"Yes, I am," Kyle responded but did he truly have any intention of coming back?

Chapter 224 - 224: 911!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
