Priscilla already knew that Oliver saw things a lot more differently than she did but he also served as her anchor.

"You overthink," Oliver told Priscilla.

"I don't think that's the case but there is something strange about him," Priscilla said.

She was sitting right in front of him and, as usual, Oliver was stuffing his face with food.

"Maybe you like him?" Oliver tried to goad her but she didn't give him a reaction.

"Oliver, are you not too old for such jokes?" Priscilla responded without her facial expression changing for even a second.

"You're so uptight," Oliver responded before taking a bite out of his doughnut.

"He just needed your help and didn't act like you had nothing to gain from this," Oliver pointed out, his voice changing to show his seriousness.

"You aren't wrong," Priscilla admitted because she was self-aware, she didn't fool herself with delusions.

"But if you feel something is wrong, I guess it is worth looking into," Oliver pointed out.

All jokes aside, there was no reason for Oliver to dismiss her completely because he was aware that Priscilla had an eye for such things.

Oliver was going to look into it but he was also surprised that Priscilla could work with anyone.

She was a pain in the ass that took her job far too seriously, this was the only thing she had going in her life.

"We should probably hit the road," Oliver spoke but realized he was the only one still eating.

Priscilla was done with her salad a long time ago but Oliver appreciated the fact that she didn't try to hurry him up.

They rounded up and left the place, hopping into their new car but soon after, Priscilla's phone rang.

This was happening a lot more frequently ever since Kyle came into her life, she had no friends so she wasn't expecting any calls.

She already knew who it was without her even looking at the phone.

"Your boyfriend is calling," Oliver teased but once again, Priscilla didn't react to this tease.

She remained stoic but she wasn't going to leave Kyle hanging because it could be related to the case they were working on.

Fortunately for her, it was Oliver on the wheels, he was their designated driver.

Priscilla put it on loudspeaker because she wanted Oliver to be aware of the situation.

He was her partner and thus, there was no point in hiding anything from him.

[[Priscilla! I need an ambulance right now! I have no idea if it is possible but I will cover whatever fees they will require!]] Kyle was freaking out.

Oliver looked concerned but Priscilla looked indifferent more or less.

"Where are you?" Priscilla remained calm because Kyle was stressed by the situation.

Kyle proceeded to give her the location but what the hell was he doing here?

This place was not only renowned for its high crime rate but Priscilla wasn't the only one that shared this look.

Oliver was visibly surprised as well, looking right at Priscilla.

They knew that no ambulance would come there because the ambulances themselves would be put in danger.

It would be stripped and sold for parts, and the medicine and equipment within would be abused as well.

"We are going, aren't we?" Oliver asked the moment the call came to an end.

"We need to call it in," Priscilla said, tucking her phone away.

Oliver expected her to be more concerned because Kyle did mention a child in all of this.

The fact that she was technically closer to Kyle than he was, he hoped that would have softened her up.

"There is a child involved!" Oliver barked but all Priscilla did was sigh.

"Children die every day, Oliver. Do you try to save everyone?" Priscilla asked in a dismissive tone.

Oliver forgot who he was talking to, Priscilla was all logical and used little emotions when making these decisions but that was why she was such a great cop.

She knew when to make the right calls and Oliver knew empathy could be considered a weakness in some cases.

There is a reason rules exist, it is to combat decisions that would otherwise be made based on empathy.

"There is nothing we can do. If we go in alone and get ambushed, what happens then? Are you so eager to make your wife a widow?" Priscilla asked and once again, she made a good point.

"You know what? I refuse," Oliver responded and fortunately for him, he knew where Kyle was.

He was the one driving as well so he had control over the decision he was about to make.

Oliver steered the car in the opposite direction before flicking on his siren.

"What are you doing?" Priscilla asked in an indifferent tone.

"The reason I became a police officer… I'm going to save a life," Oliver responded but Priscilla didn't oppose this.

All she did was redirect her eyes on the road without saying a word because Oliver wasn't her underling but her partner.

He had every right to make a decision she opposed but she would still have his back as he has had hers since they became partners.

"Fine," Priscilla responded before taking out her phone to inform Kyle that they were on their way even though he asked for an ambulance.

The injuries the man suffered were a few broken bones and fractures but was the beating severe enough to cause brain damage?

That was to be seen but Kyle had done all he could in this situation and had to leave it up to the professionals even though he now understood he had to brush up on his medical knowledge.

It would be a lot easier to do with Total Recall.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 225 - 225: Nakamura's Manipulation.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Isabella was entertaining someone she had no business entertaining because this woman was no other than Nakamura.

"This place is a lot smaller than I thought it would be," Nakamura said and Isabella's excitement evaporated because this man had dealings with Kyle so why was he here?

She wondered if he came here looking for him but there was no way someone as important as him would make that kind of mistake to begin with.

"Mr. Nakamura, what can I help you with?" Isabella asked and this surprised Nakamura.

"You know who I am?" Nakamura asked, to which Isabella nodded. Kyle then briefed her about everything regarding his business with this man.

Nakamura eyed her from head to toe, Isabella looked like a powerful woman and Nakamura thought about what would make a powerful-looking woman submit to him.

However, Yuki soon came into view, this was the first time they were meeting and with how enticing Isabella looked.

It was a shame she wasn't the one that came to meet him but why was he here was the big question.

"Of course I do, Mr. Nakamura. I make it my business to know who works with us," Isabella responded sternly with a smile on her face.

Nakamura didn't give her a verbal response either way because Yuki took over the conversation on his behalf.

"I'm Yuki, the assistant of Mr. Nakamura and I will be speaking in his stead," Yuki said and Isabelle couldn't object.

She wondered if she should call Kyle but this was something that she could handle.

"That is fine by me. Please, state your business," Isabella responded, looking right at her.

"Mr. Nakamura wishes to get a list of your most prominent talents," Yuki said but this was something that could be done over the phone.

There was no reason for them to come all the way here considering how busy he was running Distort.

Isabella had a brief look of confusion plastered across her face before it was replaced with a smile.

"A list?" Isabella asked because she understood there had to be a reason they came to her and not ask Kyle.

"That is correct. As you know, Mr. Nakamura has an ongoing contract with this Agency and Mr. Nakamura would like to see the list of talents under your Agency as screening for his next movie has already begun," Yuki responded.

It was weird that Nakamura was staring at her or would it be more accurate to say he was checking her out?

Isabella was an elegant woman with class that made the every day woman look like maids.

She had that mature beauty that was described in books.

He didn't say a word and it felt a tad bit strange because there was no reason for him to be here if he wasn't going to say anything.

"I will have to compile it and filter them out," Isabella said right away but this wasn't a good enough answer.

"Mr Nakamura would like to do that himself, he wants everything," Yuki wasn't bulging, she was standing her ground.

"I'm sorry but I have to follow protocol," Isabella stood her ground as well but Nakamura sighed loudly as this was taking too long.

"Do you plan to merge?" Nakamura finally spoke but revealed he knew about the proposed merger which shouldn't be possible.

Isabella tried to maintain her composure but this surprised her.

"How do you know about that?" Isabella asked but Nakamura had ears and eyes everywhere.

This was how he was able to make the most out of every situation he had been in.

Be it leveraging his position or manipulating it to his advantage.

"I know things I shouldn't, Miss Isabella," Nakamura said before gesturing for Yuki to leave them both alone.

Yuki didn't hesitate and left the area without thinking twice, she was like an android that operated based on her master's command.

"Now that we are alone, I just wanted to confirm this merger would not compromise our existing agreement," Nakamura said and Isabella realized the reason why he came here in person was to judge how she reacted.

Certain conversations were best had in person so you could get a direct feel of the other person's energy.

Nakamura was a seasoned businessman and liked to have these types of interactions face-to-face because people could lie a lot easier over the phone but it was a different case when it was in person.

He thought that Isabella was the original owner of this place before meeting Kyle after the meeting but she was just the face.

However, there was no way that this woman here didn't hold any power but Nakamura wasn't looking to intimidate her.

"The merger? I'm sorry but I cannot discuss that," Isabella responded.

"I didn't ask about the merger, I asked about how it would affect our deal?" Nakamura said, his voice was as soft as silk.

"Unfortunately, I cannot give you a definite response until we make a decision," Isabella stood her ground.

"She's not intimidated?" Nakamura thought to himself with a satisfied smile.

"That is fine but don't you think it is strange that this came right around the time this Agency was about to make a name for itself?" Nakamura began his true motive and planted this into her head.

"The strong will always devour the weak before they become a threat," Nakamura said out of the blue like an ancient monk bestowing knowledge onto his disciple.

This might look like a blessing but Nakamura knew more than anyone that it wasn't, they were simply eliminating competition because Distort involvement with them had been revealed.

And at the pace at which JPD rose in the rankings, there was no doubt it was only a matter of time before it grew into something bigger than them.

However, this was the conclusion of their little meeting, it took a little over twenty minutes and this confirmed Isabella's suspicion.

This was going to be the most important decision she would have to make.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 226 - 226: Is This My... Home?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Kyle left them in that area after he got confirmation that Priscilla and Oliver were on their way.

But there was something strange, Kyle couldn't remember where his house stood.

No, that wasn't right, the houses here looked very different from what he remembered.

Kyle saw a passerby and stopped her in her tracks. This lady was covered in tattoos from head-to-toe but Kyle didn't judge by this.

What he judged her by was the fact that she was living in a crappy place such as this.

The lady looked at Kyle from head to toe like he was lost.

The way he looked, the way he carried himself, this man didn't live here.

"Are you lost bud?" The lady asked with a raised brow.

"Unfortunately, no," Kyle responded but the lady wasn't having it.

There was no reason for someone who looked as good as him to be here unless he was looking for some good stuff.

"Alright, I can fix you up real quick but it is going to cost you," The lady said but Kyle knew that if he turned her down, she could assume he was a police officer or potentially lead him into a trap.

"Hahaha! I'm not looking for that and I lived here once upon a time but I got out," Kyle said.

"You got out? No one gets out of here, bud," The lady was quick to rubbish his claim.

But Kyle's facial expression changed in an instant prompting the lady to take a step back because she felt threatened.

"You will be wise not to judge a book by its cover…" Kyle warned her in a blood-curdling voice.

"W-Woah! Take it easy bud!" The lady didn't want any trouble but Kyle needed her help nonetheless.

"Is this XXX-XXX-XXX Street?" Kyle asked to which the woman nodded.

"That can't be right, the houses don't look the same," Kyle said under his breath but the woman heard him.

"They have been the same for years. The same shitty foundation but no one lives in this neighbourhood anymore or at least not anyone fucking sane," The lady informed Kyle.

This worried Kyle because how could he have imagined his childhood home differently?

That shouldn't be possible but if these houses have been the same then there was no way that someone had changed the structure of every house in this neighborhood.

This further proved how unreliable his memory was and how most of the things he thought he knew were proving to be false.

"Unless you were begging to be killed," The lady added but this caught Kyle's ear.

"Killed? What do you mean killed?" Kyle asked.

"Do you believe in rumours?" The lady was talkative like she had been waiting for someone to tell this story all her life.

"They exist for a reason, right?" Kyle responded but he began to notice that there was no one around.

This place was deserted so how did he run into this woman? Did she follow him into somewhere secluded?

Kyle realized he had dropped his guard and this woman might very well be an enemy.

"A serial killer, the man that drank the blood of his victims," The lady said but Kyle rolled his eyes at this nonsense.

"A serial killer?" Kyle repeated with a head shake trying his best not to laugh at this ridiculousness.

"That is right…" The lady repeated but Kyle noticed from her face that she was dead serious but she also stated that no one sane lived in this area.

"Tell me, do you happen to stay here?" Kyle asked.

"How did you know!?" The lady asked, looking at Kyle like he had read her mind.

"Just a hunch but tell me more," Kyle knew he had nothing to lose and if he couldn't trust his memories then he had to trust the memories of others.

There was a chance that he remembered it wrongly, he was a child so this was normal but Kyle felt lost.

Kyle felt like his life was a product of his imagination, he had no idea how to stand by what he knew.

"He was a man that had no form, some called him a monster but it is unknown what he truly was, we know for a fact that he was responsible for a series of murders that occurred a few years back," The lady said but that was absurd.

Of course, he was a man, this wasn't some supernatural world where angels and demons existed.

However, this place was also far from crime-free and there were murders here frequently.

Be it rape, be it a crime, or be it just murder, it was a common occurrence.

So why did she sum it up to some serial killer even though this would be the perfect place for one to operate?

"Is that all?" Kyle asked, partly disappointed by this absurdity but he expected a better ending.

"Or are you just stalling for your friends to make their move?" Kyle asked and the woman's eyes widened.

"You can see them too!?" She questioned but Kyle was taking a guess, this was him taking a guess but this woman was mentally unstable.

"Um…" Kyle muttered under his breath but he had gotten confirmation that there was no one here so that meant he could go into the house.

The building might be different but there was no doubt that this was the street and the road that led to his home.

Kyle left the crazy woman there and walked into his house, the moment he stepped in, he was hit with an overwhelming stench that nearly made him throw up everything left in his stomach.

The house was empty but some things reminded him of that time.

The cracked ceiling remained the same hole that always made water leak through the door of the house every rainy season.

They always kept a bucket beneath but the hole was a lot bigger now and Kyle realized that this was no doubt the house he grew up in.

He couldn't contain the emotions building up inside him and in the blink of an eye, tears began to stream down his cheeks.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 227 - 227: Bittersweet.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Kyle wept at the life that he never knew, he cried because he knew that his childhood was stolen from him.

He quickly wiped his tears and began to move around in the house, the creaking floor showed how much it had aged.

It felt like it was going to cave in at any moment, but this was its usual state.

Kyle didn't know how it was still standing but it was a lot sturdier than it gave it credit for and he found himself standing in the room that once belonged to his mother.

"Mama…" Kyle found himself saying before clamping his mouth shut, not understanding why he muttered that word.

It had been years since he left this house yet it looked the same despite signs of it looking like someone had lived.

"My home… It feels strange being here again," Kyle muttered to himself, running his fingers along the walls.

Kyle noticed that there was something on the floor but unlike everything covered in dust, this was something a lot different because it didn't share the same fate as the rest of the objects within the house.

"That is weird," Kyle said before crouching to inspect the object, it was a piece of metal but that was it, an underwhelming find but could it be that someone broke into the house with this item and disposed of it the moment they discovered this place was a dump.

But there was a poignant smell coming from this house, it invaded his nostrils the moment he opened the kitchen door.

The stench, it was like something died in here and Kyle could see a decaying rodent in the corner of the room.

It came to find food in this building but ended up starving to death but this didn't solicit any sort of empathy from Kyle not because he was some heartless monster but due to this simply being a rodent.

Kyle expected him to remember something, to have some kind of epiphany but nothing came to mind, unfortunately.

But Kyle soon heard sudden movement at the back of the house and without thinking twice he rushed outside to see who it was.

Kyle barged out the door and saw there was no sign of life, it was just the wind.

He felt like he was losing his mind at this point but Kyle knew he had to remain calm.

This was turning out to be a waste of time but at the corner of his eyes, he saw something or someone standing there.

Kyle jerked his head towards that direction only to see the woman he had just spoken to staring right at him.

"What the hell are you doing here!?" Kyle was startled but the woman had a creepy smile on her face.

Kyle knew that she was bad news and wanted nothing to do with her because she belonged in the asylum.

"My friends want to know why you are going into that house?" The lady asked, prompting Kyle to look around to see her so-called friends but his eyes met nothing.

"Are you with someone?" Kyle asked but the lady raised a brow.

"They are standing right beside me," The lady responded with such seriousness.

"How did I get myself in this fucking mess?" Kyle thought to himself, his return has been far from uneventful.

He nearly beat a man to death and now he was interacting with someone that was schizophrenic because she had imaginary friends.

This was the effect this place had on people, it turned the sanest of people into lunatics, a product of their environment.

But before the conversation could evolve, his phone rang, it was Priscilla and without giving it a second thought. Kyle was on his way.

The lady was left standing there but her eyes looked at the house before shuddering.

"A lot of bad things happened in that house," The lady said under her breath but with Kyle nowhere in sight, her words fell on deaf ears.

-

Oliver and his partner arrived at the given location, all eyes were on them as people looked at them in confusion but there was no doubt that they felt threatened by their presence.

"What the hell is this place?" Oliver questioned, he couldn't believe that it was this bad.

However, Priscilla didn't have the same reaction, she looked indifferent because they had seen over a dozen kids that needed help.

"So… which of the children was he talking about?" Priscilla asked Oliver and Oliver realized that they might have been too hasty.

They didn't have all the information but he also didn't know it would be this bad either.

"How about you call him?" Oliver suggested and that was exactly what she did.

Priscilla engaged Kyle in a conversation but Oliver was taken aback by the state of this place.

However, he soon felt a hand on his gun and Oliver reflexively defended himself by swinging his arm back but it collided with something that had far less body mass than he assumed.

This meant the force he exerted was for an adult, not a child but Oliver knew right away the moment his hand collided.

It hit the child right in the face, sending him tumbling before Oliver placed his hand on his holster to make sure his gun was still there.

Priscilla looked at it unfold and once again, didn't react showing she had more experience in navigating these situations than Oliver.

"I-I'm so sorry!" Oliver apologized, attempting to help the boy up but the boy ran away without looking back.

The fear in everyone's eyes tripled and Priscilla sighed knowing that this was going to be a long day.

The police were pigs or at least that was how some people viewed them and this meant that not everyone had the idea that the police were their friend.

Who could blame them considering they had been abandoned by them for so long or this place wouldn't look the way it did?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 228 - 228: Safety Before Danger.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Nothing was stopping Kyle from taking the much-needed step to redeem himself.

He felt lost however he couldn't escape his fate.

Kyle came back home but this place didn't feel like home.

It felt like a prison. The air wasn't refreshing; it was poisonous, but Kyle needed to face this.

Something was standing in his way, what was stopping him from remembering?

Where did this system spawn from and why was it giving him skills associated with the things he lacked during his childhood?

Kyle ran to where the police officers were located because he knew things could escalate if he left them alone.

The people here weren't a fan of the cops and perceived them negatively.

But upon Kyle's arrival, he saw that Priscilla had everything under control but there was no ambulance in sight.

"What the… Where is the ambulance!?" Kyle was furious but Priscilla didn't look bothered by his tantrum.

"Where are they?" Priscilla asked, ignoring his request.

Kyle knew there was no need to get all worked up because Priscilla was the kind of person who was in complete control of her emotions.

Oliver looked worried about the situation, Kyle could tell with a single glance that he was the empathic one among them.

"This way," Kyle led them to the corner in which the fight occurred.

He was worried that the damage he had done to the man could potentially leave him brain-damaged but he had a little sister to take care of so if Kyle took him away from her.

It was only a matter of time before she died as there would be no other means for her to survive.

Kyle didn't know about this or he would have taken a different approach but he had a choice to make in the end.

Kyle led them right to the child and her unconscious brother, the child had a wide smile on her face as she was munching on something.

"Hello Jasmine," Kyle greeted the child and she turned away with excitement in her eyes.

"Kyle!" Jasmine exclaimed, running right at him, she had 0 survival skills if he was this trusting of strangers.

It was understandable considering the way she lived her life but this also showed her brother's inability to protect her.

If he was a junkie, there was no way he could protect his sister when he was only worried about his next fix.

Jasmine hugged him and Kyle looked over at the officers, trying to give them an explanation but there wasn't any to be given.

"Are these the doctors?" The child questioned but this was proof of her innocence.

She stared at them with a look of curiosity but Kyle understood it was his duty to educate her about these things.

"Those are police officers and they are here to help your brother," The moment Kyle muttered "police officers," He noticed Jasmine tense up.

This wasn't a new word to her, she had this and immediately ran to her brother.

"DON'T HURT HIM!" Jasmine exclaimed with her arms outstretched like she was forming a barrier that rejected anything outside of it.

"Jasmine, it's not like that. They are here to help," Kyle reassured.

"LIAR! POLICE ARE BAD!" Jasmine exclaimed and tears welled up in her eyes but Oliver stepped forward.

Kyle didn't know what he had planned but felt safer with Oliver handling this situation as opposed to Priscilla.

Oliver was more in touch with his humanity rather than Priscilla.

He stepped up to Jasmine and the way she was trembling, it was like her little body would crumble at any moment.

"It is fine… We are here to help your brother," Oliver reiterated.

But his voice softened and thankfully, the girl was still on the naive side so she was easily swayed by words.

"You promise? Pinky swear!" Jasmine exclaimed but Oliver had a kind smile.

She wanted a pinky swear and got it, Oliver did it and that was enough to gain her trust.

She stepped aside and Oliver gestured for Priscilla to come over.

They examined the unconscious man and Kyle found himself feeling anxious.

He didn't want more blood on his hands and the thought of taking this girl's brother from her would make him feel guilty for the rest of his life.

It was something that he wouldn't be able to move past.

"Okay, it looks like he will be fine. You did some harm to him but it isn't as bad as it looks," Oliver said.

"But we cannot know the full extent of his injuries until we get to the hospital," Oliver added but Kyle could tell by his tone that this wasn't something that he needed to worry about.

Or could it be that Oliver was simply trying to reassure him?

Kyle felt a smaller hand crawl into his, it was Jasmine.

Kyle realized he couldn't leave her here alone and would have to take her back with him.

At least, until her brother was alright.

But he was a junkie, Kyle didn't know how safe Jasmine would be with him.

Oliver gently lifted him and placed him in the back of the police car.

Despite Priscilla's opposition to coming here, she helped her partner in every way possible.

This was the nature of their partnership, just because they didn't agree didn't mean they wouldn't do their best to offer support to each other.

"Do you want to follow us?" Oliver asked, revving the engine.

"We will be right behind you," Kyle reassured him.

Oliver zoomed off and Kyle was quick to call an Uber but no one accepted his request.

Of course, they won't and this prompted Kyle to chat with one of the drivers via the app.

It was a difficult conversation but Kyle offered to pay double the price.

And luckily, he had nothing but good reviews from the various drivers that had previously worked with him.

This was his saving grace because the driver soon arrived and thus concluded his journey back home.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 229 - 229: Jasmine And Kyle.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

They arrived at the hospital, and he was taken in, but Priscilla and Oliver stayed behind alongside Kyle. Jasmine stood awkwardly, looking around at the room filled with people, but she saw a lot of things she had never seen before.

"Kyle, we will need to ask you questions about what happened to him," Priscilla began but Oliver had to step in.

"Now isn't the time, Priscilla. Leave it for later," Olive informed his partner that there was a time and place for these things.

Priscilla kept quiet but Kyle was aware that she couldn't read certain social queues so this was something he didn't particularly mind.

"It is fine…" Kyle said before looking at Jasmine, she was so entranced by everything she saw that she didn't even pay attention to the conversation they were having.

"We got in a fight, he tried to rob me," Kyle said but Priscilla was suspicious by the degree of the wound that he had sustained.

"There is no way he stayed conscious after several blows… You went overboard and could have killed him," Priscilla informed him.

Kyle kept quiet because there was nothing else to say, she was right.

He couldn't control his rage and nearly destroyed a family even if it was in self-defense but the question was, when did it stop being self-defense?

Oliver didn't intrude on the conversation either because Priscilla was asking all the important questions even if the timing didn't favour them.

"I got carried away and nearly killed a man…" Kyle said, taking accountability for his action because he knew he was in the wrong.

"But I didn't," Kyle added but the sense of relief that overcame him when he said these words was evident.

The conversation couldn't go further as a loud rumble was heard. Kyle looked down and realized that it came from Jasmine.

Why the hell was it so loud? Even adults couldn't make such loud noises but this was also proof of how hungry she was.

"Are you hungry?" Kyle asked her and she nodded with a smile.

"Food, food, food!" Jasmine repeated with a big smile on her face, a smile untainted by the world but how?

Kyle didn't know how she was able to be so happy despite living on the streets in one of the poorest and most dangerous neighbourhoods in the country.

Kyle looked at Oliver for approval and he gave him an approving nod.

These people were police officers and not his friends, they did bail him out of a sticky situation.

Kyle had to give them the respect that came with their badges and this was something Oliver appreciated.

Kyle left with Jasmine, who was holding his hand tightly like he was taking her on a tour.

Kyle noticed she looked at the uniformed doctors with curiosity and realized she had no idea what they were.

"Those are the people that will help your brother," Kyle said and Jasmine smiled before turning to him.

"REALLY!?" Jasmine exclaimed with happiness in her eyes but this only drew the attention of everyone around them.

Jasmine quickly ran to one of the doctors but not every doctor was friendly, this was a highly stressed job and Kyle knew this.

"Doctor, is my brother okay?" Jasmine asked a random doctor but the doctor was confused about who she was talking about.

She looked at Kyle before returning her eyes to the girl and saying.

"I'm sure he is alright!" The lady responded with enthusiasm.

Fortunately, the doctor they had run into wasn't a piece of shit and treated Jasmine like a kid.

Optimism was a thing everyone needed in their lives regardless of how grim the reality was.

"You hear that!? He is fine!" Jasmine exclaimed before running back to Kyle.

Kyle smiled at how excited she was and this reminded him that not everyone that was exposed to the same circumstance would turn out the same.

"I told you…" Kyle responded.

"Now they can fix Mama and Papa next!" Jasmine said but Kyle wasn't about to break the news that they were dead to her.

Her brother might be a junkie but it was clear that he managed to shield her from the death of their parents.

That was something that Kyle could respect because it showed that he had his sister's best interest at heart at least.

Kyle took her to the cafeteria and knew he could get her some much-needed food.

If she lived on the street, there was no doubt that basic meals would be luxuries.

Kyle knew he had to get her something that was healthy, he could treat her to snacks later as he was aware it must have been some time since she ate anything remotely healthy.

Kyle got something for her and they both returned to the table, Kyle wasn't a fan of hospital food but this had to do.

Jasmine was salivating but never attempted to take a bite out of the food in front of her.

Mashed potatoes with a little stew in the middle but this was the best meal in the world to a girl that was used to eating scraps.

"Let's see who can finish their food first!" Kyle urged her, playing his favourite game.

And Jasmine didn't need much encouragement as she dug her spoon into the mashed potatoes.

"THIS IS YUMMY!" Jasmine's face lit up before stuffing her face.

Kyle watched with glee as she devoured the food in front of her, pretending to compete with her.

But she finished her food in no time, Kyle had asked for a smaller potion due to her size but she exceeded his expectation.

Jasmine's stomach was swollen and it didn't take up to three minutes before she was fast asleep.

"Poor thing, she must have been tired," Kyle thought to himself as she laid her head on his lap and Kyle was scared to move.

He didn't want to wake her up from her slumber and decided to stay still until she woke up.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 230 - 230: Girls Night Out.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Calista was back trending online, and with this came more attention. No one on earth didn't like attention.

There was something wrong with all of this, but Calista didn't care, as she knew it could positively influence her reputation.

Calista was going to use Lorenzo to rise to the idol she knew she could become.

But she hadn't gotten Kyle's approval just yet and had no idea how he would react to this considering she had already betrayed him once.

She didn't know if Kyle had forgiven her either because they hadn't spoken for a while.

Lorenzo was in a relationship and there was no telling how this rumour would affect him but it was a good cover.

The world was more accepting of homosexuals but there was still a stigma that came alongside it. More so, she had no idea how Lorenzo's relationship with his boyfriend or what arrangement they had but it was strange that no one was able to bring up anything about his relationship the moment he started to trend which meant that it must have been kept a secret.

Sex sells and especially with thirsty women, men weren't the lustful creatures as society painted them out to be, the true perverts were women.

Calista didn't care much about this as it would help their brand so there was no such thing as bad publicity.

She was in contact with Ella and Ella wanted to meet up, Calista was cautious because Kyle had made the connection.

She couldn't afford to slip up and with the attention on her, she knew she might have to change her neighborhood as well.

She couldn't entertain people in her home anymore but there was a sense of comfort that came with it.

She proposed they meet up at a suite in the city, a very expensive suite and this could also serve as a good place for her to show Ella the kind of lifestyle she could expose her to.

Calista liked Ella but their friendship was based on the wrong reason.

She got ready but Ella had told her she wished to introduce someone to her, this was what sweetened the pot.

Calista hoped that this person was Jane but she didn't know how Kyle would respond to this.

She couldn't aggravate him but she had plausible deniability because Ella never stated the person's name.

It took a couple of hours for them to eventually meet but Calista was going all out as she drove her Lamborghini as this would grant her special treatment by default.

She went ahead of Ella to get the place ready and this included booking a room to have a 'girls' night' out.

Calista arrived and like she expected, she was treated like a celebrity but it was clear that they had no idea who she was.

That was to be expected, not everyone would know her because she was relatively new to the scene but money was one language that everyone recognized.

She was given the VIP treatment and she entered the five-star hostel but the elegance within the hotel was something that she didn't expect.

She has been to some expensive places herself over the years but this took the pick because she could afford it.

Ella and her friend arrived shortly after but the place was way too big that Ella got lost the second she stepped foot inside.

She had to shoot Calista a quick text and to her relief, she got a response right away.

Ella was led by one of the waitresses, the waitresses wore clothes that were more expensive than what Ella and Jane wore combined.

This not only showed that they were well paid but their image was just as important.

Ella and Jane followed the stunning lady who could easily pass as a model.

Everyone here was on another level and Ella realized she was not only underdressed but this place was something she wouldn't have been able to afford in a million years with her previous salary.

But that had changed with her recent payout and she was glad that she could or she would have been self-conscious the moment she stepped into this place.

She might be underdressed but it didn't mean it was out of her price range.

The switch was amazing, it was like Kyle had multiple masks he wore to fit into any situation he found himself in.

"Are you okay?" Kyle caught her staring at him but the malice she sensed of him that day was nowhere to be found.

Whoever this child was, she needed to find out more about her.

-

Jane and Ella had a much-needed time together but Jane was missing Kyle.

She didn't want to seem clingy so she tried her hardest to stop herself from texting him even though it physically hurt not to do so.

Ella left the house in the morning because she had to make a living and knew she couldn't do so sitting at home.

The fact that she was from a rich home and had access to some of that wealth might be the opportunity she needed never to work again.

The fact that Kyle took a chance on her despite not knowing anything about her was a good testament to where his heart was.

She knew he did it for Jane but judging by the dynamic of their relationship. There was no reason for him to go this far for her because he already had her in his pocket.

This meant all he did was because he wanted to not because he had to, which made him one of the few people to ever do that for her.

She was worried about Calista and knew that even if Jane didn't see her as a threat, there was no telling how far she was willing to get her way with Kyle.

Kyle was at the top of the food chain and there was no way that others wouldn't try to claim a seat beside him.

People that would make Calista look ordinary but Ella also knew there was a chance that Kyle had been intimate with her.

He was a man after all and there was no way he wouldn't have thought about it at least once and Calista trying to subtly mark her territory was a give away.

The problem was when did something occur between them, was it before he started seeing Jane or before?

The timeframe mattered the most because if it was before then it wasn't their business but if it was after, Ella knew that there was no way he was stopping because of Jane.

She had her fingers crossed and allowed her best friend to remain oblivious but she also caught on that Jane missed Kyle a lot more than she let on.

This was why she gave her one suggestion before leaving for work.

"Surprise him,"

But would this single piece of advice be the thing that destroys this relationship as Calista was currently with Kyle.

Chapter 243 - 243: Interlude
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Kyle sighed. He had enough of all of this and knew he had to make active plans to sell his home to a selected few, as he was becoming a public figure.

If his home was easily accessible, it could spell bigger problems shortly.

"I will get it," Calista said because Kyle was still stuffing his mouth with all the food he could eat.

Kyle didn't think too much of this and gave her the go-ahead because what was the big deal if she was in his house?

Calista answered the door and part of her wished it was Jane so she could rub it in her face but this wasn't the case unfortunately as the person behind the door was revealed to be Aiysha.

A lady she had a run-in with already and left a terrible first impression but Calista wondered why she was here.

"Is there a problem?" Calista began the conversation but Aiysha had no time for some confrontation, it wouldn't bear any fruit.

"I want to see Kyle, is Jasmine alright?" Aiysha asked right away, looking past Calista like she wasn't even there.

She liked Jane a lot better but wondered why this woman was here when it was clear that Kyle was involved with the other lady.

However, this was none of her business and she wasn't going to comment about it either.

It was one of those things that was beyond her control but she had to find a way to humble this woman before her.

Jasmine soon entered the fray and while she hadn't seen Aiysha, she recognized her voice.

She walked towards the door and stared blankly at Aiysha for a whole minute without uttering a single word.

Jasmine walked past Calista, grabbed Aiysha's hand and led her right to Kyle.

Calista couldn't stop her or say a word because she was still on the fence with Kyle and had no way of telling the importance of this woman in his life.

However, there was one thing she noted, this lady was attractive upon closer inspection.

There was no way that Kyle's body wouldn't respond to such an attractive lady but Jasmine was what worried her.

Aiysha was brought to the kitchen by Jasmine and even Kyle was shocked to see her.

She had no reason to be here but it must be because of Jasmine.

She must have come to check if she was doing alright, even Kyle was surprised by how well her body responded to the treatment.

Although she had a slight fever, her immune system was showing that it was stronger than that of the average person.

"How is she doing?" Aiysha asked Kyle as Jasmine returned to her seat to continue her meal.

"She is much better, thanks to you," Kyle confessed. Calista heard this as she came to join them but it felt awkward with her there.

"I'm glad to hear that, I came to check in on her," Aiysha said to which Kyle flashed her a smile.

"Thank you very much, Aiysha. I mean it, if you need anything, let me know," Kyle showed his gratitude and it was genuine.

Calista found herself getting jealous because this woman had something she didn't possess.

A nurturing instinct which came from motherhood but the ring on her finger told her she was married.

Aiysha realized this was the same woman who informed her husband about Kyle's condition.

But this was only part of the reason she was here, she wanted to speak to Kyle regarding what her husband had said to him to clear up any misconception.

However, this would have to wait, unfortunately.

Ella had gone to the Agency to speak to Isabella about a plan to produce a song.

She had the means to but knew she needed the go-ahead from Isabella as she was no longer an independent artist.

She was the person who spoke on behalf of her band as well, they had gotten their cut and this was a reminder that they could earn a living doing this.

This was good as it reignited some spark in the band but Ella worried that money might change the motive of her band.

If money was the thing that brought some spark into their lives then there was a chance that it would distort their reason for making music.

Ella knew all of this was a possibility but had to trust her band not to lose sight of the goal they all shared.

However, she knew how a person's past had a habit of sneaking up on them so she had to come clean to Isabella so she understood what she was getting herself and the label into.\\

She couldn't be the reason why Kyle's agency suffered due to her poor choices in life even though her deceased parents had given her a way out of poverty.

A card she wasn't sure she needed due to how long she had been doing this in the first place, she wanted away from her past and had done so much work on herself to be able to stand on her own two feet.

All that wasn't about to be undone but the idea that she had that option relieved the stress on her shoulders.

"Good morning Carly, is Isabella in?" Ella asked to which Carly nodded with a bright smile

"She is but let me inform her that you are here," Carly was being professional but her aura was inviting.

"Thank you, Carly," Ella responded but she was nervous for some reason because this felt like a big step in the journey she was about to take.

Chapter 244 - 244: Cleopatra's Ties?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

They were seated across each other, careful to keep their distance as they filled the air with small talk.

"You said you wanted to talk to me," Kyle began the conversation.

"Yes, I do but I don't want you to take this the wrong way," Aiysha began and Kyle immediately caught on to what she wanted to say.

"It is fine…" Kyle said. If they were as transparent in that relationship as he thought then Jones must have told her what they discussed.

"I know my husband might have said some things that might have been misinterpreted but I wanted to make sure that you understood he didn't mean it literally," Aiysha said.

Kyle was getting drained by this couple, it felt like they were going around in a circle.

"Do you know what's funny?" Kyle asked, drawing Aiysha's attention.

"I don't think I want you as bad as your husband thinks… As bad as you think," Kyle added and Aiysha's ego shattered upon hearing those words.

They kept going on about him hooking up with her without ever wondering if Kyle wished to.

"I-I'm glad to hear that," Aiysha said but her voice cracked, she was astonished because of the way Kyle looked at her.

It reminded her of the time she was a fat woman with people looking at her.

It made her feel small even though this wasn't how Kyle intended it to be.

She knew what he meant but had gotten so used to everyone looking at her like they wanted to screw her brains out that she forgot how it was to be looked at like this.

It was easy for Kyle because he had the ability to mimic any character he had seen portrayed on screen and all he had to do was play the part of the cool disinterested character.

The erotic books that women seemed to be addicted to also seemed to have at least one of these characters and it spilt into the media.

Kyle realized how wonderful this skill was, it was a lot more handy than he thought because it allowed him to escape these sorts of interactions.

"Tasha, on the other hand, I think we will get along just fine," Kyle added and Aiysha wanted the ground to swallow her up as she felt the confidence she had used for years to build begin to crumble.

She built her body into a temple so she would never have to fight for the validation she starved of all her life and this should be good news as it would help establish healthy boundaries.

So why… Why did her heart clench at the mention of Tasha?

"So Aiysha, we're good. You and your husband have nothing to worry about," Kyle smiled, adding a faint smile.

-

Cleopatra wasn't sitting idle and knew she had to do something regarding all of this because there was no telling what could happen should things escalate.

She had the means to find out who Kyle was and did just that, there was no way he wouldn't exist in the system especially since he grew up in the system.

She gathered information rather fast showing that there was a level of wealth one would reach where the laws no longer applied.

One that even Nakamura was yet to reach but there remained an unanswered question.

That was how Kyle came about his wealth.

"Kyle, just who are you…" Cleopatra thought out loud but there was a man on his knees, sucking on her toes.

This was no doubt a humiliation ritual because Cleopatra was fully clothed and this man looked like he could be on the cover of Vogue's magazine.

"That is enough, you pig," Cleopatra said before kicking him hard across the force, sending the smaller man flying.

She knew that there was no way Kyle came about his wealth legally and these establishments of his that popped out of thin air were suspicious, to say the least.

She couldn't wrap her head around it but it was something that she knew she had to accept, the possibility that Kyle had acquaintances in the underworld.

This was a place no normal person could mingle in without selling a part of his soul but how did he get into contact with Ella and Jane?

She wasn't worried for her sister's sake, of course as this was solely personal.

However, she made progress in discovering who Kyle was and that had to do with his last family.

The only past family of his who remained in the country as the others had relocated.

The man she kicked was on his knees with his bloodied nose, the look in his eyes genuinely looked like he hated her but what was he doing here if this was the case?

It was simple, money. This was the power of money as it could make any situation possible.

Cleopatra looked at the man and he quickly looked away but all this elicited a smile from her.

"It looks like I still have to punish you…" She muttered under her breath and the man looked genuinely frightened.

There were chains and binds on the bed but this wasn't for pleasure, it looked like it was for torture.

"I-I'm sorry! I didn't mean to!" The man tried to plead his case but there was something strange about this entire situation.

If he didn't enjoy it, he could always walk which meant he couldn't even if he wanted to.

That was the case as this showed a glimpse of how sadistic Cleopatra was but not only that, it also showed that she might be associated with people from the underworld as well which wouldn't be a surprise.

If this was the case, Kyle had made an enemy he might not be able to defeat with his system and his skills alone.

You can't outrun a bullet, after all, you are no Barry Allen.

Chapter 245 - 245: The Meeting.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

This was why he had made inquiries about purchasing a house. No, mansions.

It could either make or break what he was trying to do but he couldn't babysit as he had a goal to achieve.

Kyle knew Nakamura was using him but there was no doubt their relationship was beneficial.

He set up a meeting that Nakamura immediately accepted because he understood that Kyle was a person whose limit was the sky.

Only a selected few could resist the offer but it showed that it wasn't money Kyle was after. He was far too ambitious to want something that mundane.

An ambitious man was the most dangerous person in the world because there was no telling what he was willing to do to achieve it.

They picked a secluded area that only the richest of the rich could afford which meant Kyle had made sure to tell Nakamura to keep his assistant away.

Whatever he wanted to discuss with him, he didn't want the information to reach any third party.

They met and Nakamura noticed something right away, it was the way Kyle carried himself.

He looked far more confident in how he carried himself.

Nakamura had a hidden smirk as he realized he wasn't wrong about this man and was glad he made contact with him when he did.

"Kyle, it is good to see you again," Nakamura greeted him, offering his hand for a handshake.

A gesture Kyle willingly returned.

He gave Nakamura a firm shake but it was clear he was dictating the course of their relationship going forward.

"Nakamura, I'm glad you honoured my request. Forgive my choice of meeting, I have heard wonders about this place and wished to see it for myself," Kyle was learning the etiquette of the elite.

"Wow, he has changed so much," Nakamura thought to himself or was he always like this?

"Now, for business… We have concluded the movie that tied us together and I'm certain you're aware of the attempted takeover of my Agency," Kyle began, not even waiting for a waitress to come over to their table to take their order.

He had no time to dilly dally and Nakamura couldn't contain his smile because this man reminded him of him when he was younger.

"The movie has indeed concluded but I believe we have discussed our future relationship, have we not?" Nakamura asked, pausing briefly to allow Kyle to digest what he had just said.

"... And I'm aware of the attempted takeoff-!" Nakamura was interrupted by Kyle, who called the attention of the waitress.

"Nakamura… I'm aware that you came to my Agency to give unsolicited advice to my CEO?" Kyle cut him short and Nakamura had no idea who he was talking to.

This wasn't the same person he spoke to just weeks back but maybe it was because Kyle no longer needed to impress him that his true colors were beginning to show.

"That is correct. I'm a businessman, I was simply protecting my interest," Nakamura responded but he could tell Kyle didn't like this with a single look.

"Don't do that again, understood?" This was all Kyle said and Nakamura could feel the power dynamic change.

There was no other answer he could give but to comply and that was exactly what he did.

Nakamura nodded like a good boy and Kyle gave him an approving smile.

"I'm glad we are on the same page but now I need your connections to know what it would take to buy out the Agency that attempted to buy mine," Kyle said.

Showing the waitress what he wants with Nakamura doing the same.

"The Gifted House is currently valued at approximately over $100,000,000,000 due to its heavy influence in some of the world's most renowned talents today," Nakamura said.

He expected Kyle to react to the value but he didn't.

"So if I was to offer them double that, do you think they would sell?" Kyle asked but this hypothetical situation sounded like he could make it a reality at the snap of his fingers.

"Owning 100% rights is almost impossible but you can buy the shares of the shareholders there," Nakamura advised but there was one thing he left out.

These people had protection and not from the police because when he became too big.

You attract unwanted attention so there had to be a mutual understanding between them and those that had the potential to disrupt their operations.

"Is that so?" Kyle asked, sounding almost sarcastic.

"That is so…" Nakamura responded.

"Are you telling me there is something that money cannot buy?" Kyle asked but he could tell Nakamura's mouth had become tighter which was unlike him.

He was keeping something away from him and Kyle didn't like that.

"Say, do you think Distort would be able to turn down such an offer?" Kyle asked, indirectly threatening him.

"You never know until you try," Nakamura didn't bulge because if there was anything he knew better than the back of his hand. It was a shit test.

"Nakamura, what do you know about the woman in the mask?" Kyle asked but he noticed something out of place.

It was just for a moment but Nakamura looked unsettled for a split second.

"I know there is nothing good that comes from her… And she takes her privacy very seriously," Nakamura responded, this sounded like a warning for Kyle not to dig further.

"Is that so?" Kyle responded with a chuckle to quickly dispel the building tension but Nakamura had on a straight face.

"This is not a joke, Kyle," Nakamura added because he knew that if Kyle became too curious.

It wasn't just him that his actions would affect.

He entertained this possibility but the thought didn't cripple him with fear, quite the opposite.

This new world he was stepping into was dangerous and had no idea what kind of influence Cleopatra had but it was enough to frighten Nakamura.

This meant that whatever he found during his search was enough to instil the fear of God in him.

Kyle knew Cleopatra was dangerous, she had to be to maintain the wealth she possessed but how dangerous was she?

And how far was she willing to go with Ella getting a chunk of what their parents worked for, there was a high chance that she would begin to see her sister as an enemy.

And that would inevitably put Jane within firing range, something he couldn't let happen.

Kyle knew it might be time to speak to Cleopatra directly but he had no means to reach her.

Kyle drove to his establishment headed by Nate to see how it was going, it felt like it had been forever since he had seen the man who had become an important part of his plans.

Nate held it down and Kyle couldn't have asked for a better partner but it was too early to say where Nate's role would come to an end.

"Kyle! It has been ages!" Nate jumped off his feet but Kyle was surprised he was here.

He never came here without Nate being present begging the question if he was attending his classes.

Kyle had already granted him leave with full pay so he could treat this as a part-time job until he had the time to immerse himself in this line of work.

Nate had recruited an Onlyfan model and Kyle was curious to see how that would work out as it was a niche crowd she appealed to which meant there was potential for it to grow if exposed to a wider audience.

Men would do or entertain anything as long as it was presented erotically even though Kyle had his doubts.

"Nate, I'm glad you are. Things look like they are going well here," Kyle commended his friend.

Ever since getting the system, his life changed drastically to the point that it almost came to an end.

"What can I say? I like to get things done!" Nate was excited and Kyle could tell this wasn't an act either.

"Oh right, before I forget. There was someone that came looking for you not too long ago," Nate said before rummaging through his pockets.

Kyle stood there patiently, waiting for Nate to get whatever it was he was looking for.

"There you are…" Nate muttered under his breath before retrieving a business card.

Kyle collected it and stared at it for a couple of seconds but there was nothing on it.

Just a number and this put him on guard as he still had that warning ringing in his head so he had to be careful not to be caught up in anything that could implicate him.

"You say this came in this morning? Can you tell me what the person looked like?" Kyle questioned.

Nate placed his finger below his chin, trying his hardest to recall any information that might help Kyle but for some strange reason, the person had on a face mask.

That wasn't necessarily bizarre as there were people who wore them to protect against the environment and weather.

This could be the case and that was exactly what he told Kyle, the man who became not only his superior but friend.

"I see, thank you very much, Nate… I will check this out later, how is Kelly settling in?" Kyle asked about their latest investment.

There had to be some positive step in their business in terms of revenue even though he wished his system would grant him something that would allow him to earn passively.

This would encourage him to put up even more establishments and would reduce the pressure to spend on beautiful women as there would be other means to earn that money.

Kyle looked at the number in his hand before squeezing the card and tossing it away.

Nate noticed this but before they could go any further, Kelly walked in.

This was the first time Kyle was having a good look at her and if he was going to judge a book by its cover.

This was going to be one freaky book but Kyle could sense that Nate liked her enough to hire.

He would never have guessed that Nate was into such girls, to begin with.

"Not bad, Nate… Not bad," Kyle thought to himself, the girl tried to greet him but Kyle removed himself from their space.

He left Nate alone with her as he had no interest in getting to know her, he already had enough drama as it stood.

And the number he had was etched into his memory.

"What is his problem?" Kelly questioned, rolling her eyes as she thought he was looking down on her due to her line of work.

"Everything… That man is just amazing," Nate praised his boss but it was almost like he was talking to himself.

"Are you good, sweetheart? It sounds like you're in love," Kelly said to which Nate quickly snapped back. He had let his intrusive thoughts out in front of the baddie.

"Hahaha! Of course I do, he is my brother!" Nate doubled down, there was nothing to be ashamed of because this man changed his entire life.

But unknowingly, this made Kelly grow curious about the kind of person he was.

Chapter 247 - 247: Hello Kyle...

Kyle wasn't going to make the same mistake as he entered his car right away but through his rearview mirror.

Kyle knew he was in potential danger but had to remain calm in this situation as it had the potential to escalate.

Kyle remained in his car because there was a good chance others were lurking around.

He knew this was calculated and wasn't a random occurrence but remaining calm was the only thing he could do.

However, the car pulled away and drove off, leaving Kyle puzzled about what just happened.

"Am I overthinking it?" Kyle thought to himself.

That was likely the case as it was the only explanation but Kyle knew he couldn't become the strongest man in the world if he didn't tame the underworld in the process.

"This is going to get complicated…" Kyle muttered under his breath but before he could pull off the driveway, a knock on his door was heard.

"The fuck!?" Kyle muttered before turning his head towards the direction it came from, the fact that he couldn't see the person prior showed they knew the art of stealth because his attention was peaked.

Kyle had two options, he could leave this person in the dust and drive off but something told him this wouldn't be a good idea.

Kyle lowered his window but at this moment, he wondered why he had no weapon to defend himself against potential threats.

There wasn't much he could do if a gun was pulled on him and Kyle was beginning to understand this was going to happen a lot more often if he planned to dip his feet in that water.

"Can I help you with something?" Kyle questioned but the man in the black suit and shades simply gestured for him to unlock his door.

Kyle was skeptical because he looked like someone right out of a secret agent movie with the way he was dressed.

Kyle popped the door open and the man casually entered before opening a laptop, pointing it in Kyle's direction.

"Ummm… Hello to you too?" Kyle said but the man didn't say a word.

He noticed what was on the laptop screen, it was a call but Kyle wondered what happened to phone calls.

"This is a bit extra…" Kyle thought to himself but he knew it was connected to the card, if so, why didn't the person just call him back?

[[Hello Kyle, I have been wanting to meet you.]]

The voice greeted from the other end, the bass in his voice felt unnatural to the point that Kyle thought it was being altered.

Kyle didn't respond because he wasn't sure what this was and had his fill of bad misfortune to blindly leap into this.

[[I understand your hesitation but this is for my own safety/]]

The voice said but what did he mean by this? Why would he need protection when he was connected?

"Is that so? Who are you and what do you want from me?" Kyle got right to the point because he wasn't comfortable with this entire situation.

He assumed these were people from the Agency he attempted to buy but this was too sneaky to be that.

[[You will know who I am very soon but the question is… Do you understand that you're a man that have some powerful people's attention.]]

The voice said and all that came into his mind was Cleopatra, did she send this person?

Kyle knew he had to remain calm and not jump to any conclusion until there was definite proof.

"You're the one that sent the text…" Kyle said and there was a loud silence that followed.

[[You're perceptive, I give you that. But that isn't the purpose of this conversation.]]

"Get to it, I have things to do," Kyle responded right away. He needed to maintain his frame because if he faltered for even a second, it might be the end of him.

He had to give off the impression he wasn't to be messed with.

[[Impatient, are we?]]

Kyle knew he had to tread carefully because one wrong move could put whoever was on the line on the offensive.

The last thing he needed was enemies and also knew he needed allies, this couldn't have come at a more convenient time.

"I know I have gotten the attention of others due to my wealth but what of it?" Kyle responded, finally answering his question.

[[You do not exist in any database nor can we trace your bank account. The only people I know that go to such lengths are people with secrets to hide.]] The voice said at the other end.

"I see what this is… They want to see who is backing me," Kyle thought to himself.

"What can I say? I have powerful allies," Kyle responded but there was silence.

[[That is false… You have NO allies.]] The voice responded showing they had done thorough research.

[[... Unless you're talking about Jane, Ella, or Nate…]]

This was when Kyle's blood ran cold, whoever this person was had done their homework or they shouldn't know about these names.

"What do you want?" Kyle asked, showing willingness to negotiate.

[[I want us to meet.]] The voice said.

"Then why don't you come out here and say hello?" Kyle mocked, looking around but something soon invaded his nose.

[[Don't worry, I will bring you to me.]]

The voice responded and Kyle realized the man behind him had sprayed something that soon made him lose consciousness.

Chapter 248 - 248: A Glimpse Into The Underworld?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Kyle was knocked unconscious by whoever was in his car but the truth was, he wasn't oblivious to what could happen.

He had seen the Squid Games. This was sketchy, but even with that, he managed to hold his breath long enough and pretend to be passed out by pressing his face into the passenger seat he had collapsed in.

He did inhale the gas but it wasn't enough to knock him out, Kyle wanted to see who this person was and if it was something he needed to worry about.

He was on his guard but he had no means to defend himself should a gun be pulled on him. He had watched a few movies and self-defence but was it applicable in a real-life situation?

It was difficult to know if this was accurate but Kyle wasn't going to gamble his life on this.

They had moved his body to another car and drove off, Kyle memorized the turns and interval between those turns thanks to his Total Recall.

This allowed him to know where they were taking him even without his eyes, which was smart if he wanted to call for help.

The car came to a stop after a few hours and Kyle was certain they had taken him out of LA.

Kyle might have known the way but this was a place he had never been to.

"Stay still, Kyle…" Kyle thought to himself and the trunk of the car which he was dumped in opened.

They had placed a bag over his head so he couldn't see but once again, Kyle had to remain completely still or he risked giving himself away.

He was placed on something soft but Kyle realized they didn't bind him meaning they weren't treating him as a prisoner.

He heard distinctive whispers but they weren't audible due to the distance it echoed from.

"At least they are smart enough not to talk within my immediate vicinity," Kyle thought to himself.

He snuck a peek and noticed that he had been dropped in an apartment, not just any apartment, a fancy one at that.

Kyle quickly closed his eyes back and before he knew it, the door opened and just by listening to the footsteps, Kyle instantly knew this must be the boss.

"Huh? Is this him?" A voice asked but it was that of a woman however this wasn't the person who Kyle spoke to over the phone.

Kyle could feel her inch closer towards him until he eventually felt her breath brushing against his skin.

"Hm…" She muttered under her breath as she inspected him but the door opened once again and Kyle could feel the entire room atmosphere change the moment the figure entered.

The girl that was inches from his face backed away.

Kyle slowly opened his eyes, pretending to come to it and the man before him was covered from head to toe in tattoos but that wasn't his most intimidating feature.

It was the large scar above his right eye that alerted Kyle that whoever this was, he must have been through some hard times.

"Where am I?" This was the first question that came out of his mouth as this was a kidnapping.

There was no other way to phrase what these people had done as a crime after all.

"I apologize for my methods but rest assured, you're safe," The man's voice was exactly the same as it was over the laptop.

He needed the demand to outdo the supply first; this would make any opening like a VIP section in a star-studded club.

"Greetings, sir! I hope you're doing great today." Carly greeted because she knew it was only a matter of time before she got a salary raise.

Kyle noticed there was a line to see Isabella and could already imagine how much work was going on.

She had her hands full, but this was one of those things because even if they were to close right now, they wouldn't be losing anyone.

Once again, demand was everything, and this was what Kyle was trying to achieve at present, but he didn't know how Isabella was handling it all without help.

He hadn't noticed any new hire, which would explain why the line was so long; it couldn't continue this way.

They had to find a faster, more efficient way to process potential applicants.

"Hello Carly, is Isabella here?" Kyle greeted her with a smile.

Carly nodded, but Kyle leaned in towards her, which caught her by surprise.

"I need a favour, can you close early?" Kyle requested that he didn't have the power to ensure this came to pass.

"S-Sure thing, boss," Carly responded, to which Kyle flashed her a smile accompanied by a polite nod.

He went to the office, skipping the line, and all Carly could think about was whether Isabella was in trouble because of whatever it was they had going on.

It must be urgent for Kyle to take this route, but either way, she alerted her superior that Kyle was here so he wouldn't catch her off guard. This was Carly's way of looking out for her/

Priscilla was keen on figuring out Kyle, and once she had her teeth on something, she gnawed away at it until she tore it to shreds.

This was a long shot on her end, but her hunch was something she never doubted.

This would also explain why he suddenly took an interest in the man, Kyle was using their relationship to his advantage.

And this rubbed Priscilla the wrong way; there was one thing she didn't like, and that was being used to serve the agenda of another.

She was connecting the dots, but she was worried she might not be able to do so in time because this man was always two steps ahead.

Chapter 260 - 260: This Is My Dream...

"I'm afraid we aren't," Kyle replied, looking her directly in the eyes. This sent shivers down her spine.

it would take an insane amount of money and influence to establish branches around the world.

Even with that, locals of said nations might rebel against it because they are foreigners.

Isabella raised a brow—this was similar to what he said when they first spoke,

but never had she heard him talk about conquering the world like he was Genghis Khan.

Her heart began to race—not out of arousal, but because this man's dream embodied what she thought was impossible.

for the first time, he had shared his dream with her, and Isabella was impressed by the vision he held.

Cassandra was done with her outing with Dexter, and to her surprise, she enjoyed it a lot more than she thought she would, which was something entirely new to her.

It had been a while since she had spent time with another man, and she couldn't lie—she enjoyed the experience.

It was different because they were friends, and there was nothing sexual about their interaction.

[[Hey, Cassey, I had fun today. We should do this again soon. Let me know when you get home safe.]]

Cassandra was tempted to text him right away, but there was no rush—he would be fine either way.

She wondered what would be an appropriate thing to say because she didn't want to send mixed signals that could potentially lead him on.

If there was one thing she'd figured out about men, it was that they would sleep with a woman if she said yes, regardless of whether they truly wanted to or not.

However, this couldn't be the same with Dexter, because he could get any woman he wanted.

And by any woman, she didn't mean just anyone with a pretty face, but a woman with the status that came with it.

While she tried her best to be an understanding partner to Kyle, the fact that he could go so long without reaching out to her worried her.

She found herself wanting to be a bigger part of his life, but who could blame her? She was once a married woman.

It wasn't strange that she wanted to ravage Kyle with the love that had been abused by her ex-husband.

And this was the only reason she decided to send a text message back to Dexter.

[[Hello Dexter, I had a great time too! We should do it again sometime soon. I just got home, thank you for checking in!]]

Cassandra responded soon after, but she stared at the text—

Analysing it from a third person's point of view, because there was a chance Kyle could stumble upon it in the future.

But there was something she didn't—and couldn't—know.

Chapter 261 - 261: Dive Into The New World!

This wasn't the image he wanted to introduce himself to the world with, but he was careful enough to keep his private life out of the media—or his life in general.

It had to be doable, as not every celebrity had their life known. Some even managed to keep their kids secret, so this should be an easy feat considering they were adults.

It would also help Cassandra's child, having multiple parental figures. Even those who might not want one could still have the parental experience, should they choose.

He wasn't sure if things would go according to plan, because that was one thing life had taught him. Things never went according to plan, but he could adjust to whatever life threw at him.

This was his true strength, and this was one thing Kyle wasn't going to gamble with.

This would show how damaging it is firsthand, but there was no way he should be able to afford it, which meant there was a chance it was distributed for free among the poorer communities.

A place no one would look twice at. And this was exactly what was done to the place he grew up in.

Kyle knew he had to clean up that place so he could bring an end to the destruction and trauma it held.

Kyle knew AI was going to play an important part in the future—there was no doubt about that—but there were bad ways to utilise this.

And one couldn't master the good without understanding the bad.

Kyle planned to utilise both to his advantage, but knew things were yet to take off.

The universe shot him a sign, because soon after, his phone began to ring.

It was the real agent of a person whose number he had gotten from a prominent agency, even though their fees were a bit over the top.

It was something he could afford. There was no doubt that Kyle could find a cheaper route, but what would be the point?

It wasn't fixed in stone, but "the higher the fees, the better the service" was the usual motto.

They only attended to the best of the best, which Kyle qualified for.

"Hello, what do you have for me?" Kyle requested right away, showing they had had previous interactions with one another.

[[Yes sir, we have a house opening in Bel Air, but I'm afraid you face fierce competition in purchasing it, as some high-profile celebrities have shown interest.]]

The woman on the other end informed him, but Kyle had no problems with this approach because this was one way to inform clients they had competition to prompt them into buying.

"Is that so? Remind me how much this house is?" Kyle asked, and the woman at the other end hesitated for a millisecond.

It would have gone unnoticed by anyone else, but not Kyle—he was paying attention.

Nothing was stopping him from buying this mansion; it wouldn't put a dent in his bank account.

[[The value sits at $180,000,000 bu-!]] The lady was interrupted by Kyle.

"That is fine. I will give you $220,000,000 to keep it from the hands of prying clients. I will pay in cash as well," Kyle responded, but this sounded like an impulsive purchase.

There was a good chance she had inflated the price to get something out of the deal, and Kyle overpaying meant she got approximately $40,000,000.

This was life-saving money, and there was no reason she wouldn't use everything in her power to reserve this purchase for Kyle.

Kyle had done something most people failed to realise was the most important thing when it came to sales.

You have to appeal to the handler, not to the seller, because at the end of the day, you get to the seller through the handler.

The call came to an end soon after, and the woman had sent him the account he should send the money to.

Kyle hadn't made any big purchases in a long time, ever since the cars, and was relatively frugal by nature due to how he had to live most of his life.

It was embedded in him, but Kyle was about to let loose—he was about to double his spending and truly relish being a billionaire.

This was the first move, and he knew that once the house was bought, the ladies wouldn't be opposed to living together, because they all had one thing in common.

They all wished to please Kyle. But Calista remained an unknown variable, because Kyle had seen signs that not only know she was untrustworthy.

She couldn't believe it was this easy to close this sale, as it took less than seven minutes.

"Yes, sir! We are! If there is anything you want help with, let me know!" Tracy said.
